item.title: 基于条件期望的广义时序差分强化学习框架
item.content:encoded:
提出一种基于条件期望的强化学习框架，可处理非线性与非高斯系统。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于条件期望理论的广义时序差分（TD）强化学习框架，将价值函数和Q值视为随机变量，并将其估计建模为随机推断问题。该方法不依赖传统线性高斯假设，能够自然扩展到非线性模型和非高斯分布。通过递归估计价值函数的条件期望及其二阶概率矩，量化学习过程中的不确定性。为实现计算可行性，采用多项式混沌展开或集合近似对随机问题进行离散化。该框架在两个最优控制问题中进行了验证，展示了其在准确估计价值函数及其不确定性方面的有效性，拓展了经典卡尔曼时序差分学习的应用范围。

item.title: 基于3D高斯点云的文本驱动场景编辑方法LB-Edit
item.content:encoded:
LB-Edit提升3D场景编辑精度与一致性，减少所需视图数量。



摘要： 本文提出LB-Edit框架，解决文本驱动3D场景编辑中的两个关键问题：如何选择合适的编辑视角以及如何保持多视角编辑的一致性。通过Attention-Guided Editing Camera Placement (ACP)方法，在注意力最优距离上放置多样化的编辑视角；再通过Multi-view Attention Alignment (MAA)对齐多视角的外观和空间位置，提升编辑一致性和局部性。实验表明，该方法在指令保真度、多视角一致性和编辑局部性方面表现最佳，且仅需5个编辑视角，效率提升7倍。

item.title: 基于T5的句子分割框架提升知识导向自然语言处理
item.content:encoded:
提出一种自监督句子分割框架，提升知识图谱和常识问答性能。



摘要： 本文介绍了一种基于T5的自监督句子分割框架Sentence Splitter，旨在揭示自然语言句子中的潜在事实结构。该方法通过将句子分割建模为离散分段问题，识别描述性前缀与事实补全之间的语义边界。模型通过概率序列生成恢复事实补全，而无需人工标注。通过将符号化前缀-后缀对转化为自然语言模板进行训练，该框架可直接应用于原始文本，提取对齐的前缀-后缀对，并用于训练生成模型以提出更多合理补全。实验表明，该方法在结构化和自然文本中均表现良好，提升了知识图谱补全和常识问答任务的性能。

item.title: PercepCap：一种感知驱动的视频字幕生成框架
item.content:encoded:
PercepCap通过显式感知证据提升视频字幕质量。



摘要： 本文提出PercepCap，一个感知意识的视频字幕生成框架。该框架通过先生成时空感知轨迹再生成字幕的流程，使模型在生成最终字幕前明确展示感知证据。研究设计了两阶段训练策略：首先进行监督微调以适应感知-描述链，然后通过基于感知的强化学习优化感知轨迹和字幕质量。同时引入了基于字幕锚定的感知数据构建方法，确保训练数据与字幕对齐，提升模型性能。实验表明，PercepCap在多个评估任务中优于基线模型。

item.title: 构建多维度价值对齐评估基准D2VBench
item.content:encoded:
D2VBench评估基准提升大模型价值对齐能力。



摘要： 随着大语言模型在现实场景中的广泛应用，其输出的价值含义变得尤为重要。然而，现有评估基准在覆盖日常场景中的多重价值冲突以及评估方式过于简单方面存在不足。为此，研究者提出了D2VBench，一个包含10,000个真实日常困境实例的评估基准，通过人机协作构建，并基于158个手工标注的细粒度价值概念。评估方法结合了选择题和开放性问题，对八种主流大语言模型进行了全面测试。实验结果表明，D2VBench具有高可靠性与鲁棒性，能有效反映模型在不同价值类别和维度上的对齐程度，为价值对齐研究提供了更真实、细致的工具。数据集已公开。

item.title: 基于统计假设检验的LoRA参数高效微调方法
item.content:encoded:
StatLoRA通过统计检验优化LoRA参数分配，提升模型性能。



摘要： 本文提出StatLoRA，一种基于统计假设检验的LoRA参数分配方法。该方法将每个LoRA组件与检验统计量相关联，并利用估计的p值在固定参数预算下决定保留或剪枝组件。理论分析表明，多种常见优化器（如AdamW）的轨迹满足中心极限定理，支持所提方法的合理性。实验显示，StatLoRA在多个任务中表现优于现有方法，且具有良好的稳定性。

item.title: Zero-Observation Reactivation与DeltaGate模型研究
item.content:encoded:
研究用户长期无行为后的推荐效果下降及DeltaGate优化方案。



摘要： 本文研究了用户在长时间无行为后（即零观测再激活）对推荐系统的影响，发现Hit@10指标随时间间隔增加而显著下降。为解决这一问题，作者提出DeltaGate，一种轻量级输出层插件，能够在不改变主模型参数的情况下，通过结合时间间隔和个性化表示进行有效推荐。实验表明，DeltaGate在多个数据集上提升了推荐性能，同时保持了较低的参数开销和模型稳定性。

item.title: Audio-Zero：无需标签的音频推理自进化框架
item.content:encoded:
Audio-Zero提升音频细粒度推理能力，无需外部标注。



摘要： 本文提出Audio-Zero，这是一个无需标签的自进化框架，用于提升大音频语言模型（LALMs）在细粒度音频推理方面的能力。该框架通过构建无标签的听觉自博弈游戏，让模型通过对比音频对来识别细微差异，并通过推理不一致线索来判断异常听众。实验表明，Audio-Zero在多个基准测试中提升了音频细粒度推理能力，同时保持了广泛的音频理解能力。分析显示，在游戏压力下，模型自然生成了更精细的音频描述。

item.title: 大型语言模型在价值观识别任务中的表现评估
item.content:encoded:
评估大模型对十种基本价值观的识别能力，发现其准确率和排名稳定性存在差异。



摘要： 本文研究了大型语言模型在识别具体情境中所体现的价值观方面的表现。通过1000个俄语情境文本进行测试，结果表明模型在识别正确价值区域方面表现较好，但在排名相近价值时不稳定。相邻价值观导致了50.9%的错误，而某些特定价值观之间的混淆具有方向性，如普遍主义与仁慈、传统与顺从等。这些错误可能影响更高层次的价值分析，因此需要结合精确度、排名恢复和定向误差分析来改进评估方法。

item.title: 全球建筑轮廓数据集准确性评估研究
item.content:encoded:
评估四个全球建筑数据集的准确性，发现GBA或TEMPO表现最佳。



摘要： 本文评估了四个主要的全球建筑轮廓数据集（GHSL、TEMPO、GBA、Overture）的准确性，并以ORBITaL-Net作为真实数据进行对比。研究在不同空间分辨率下使用多种性能指标进行公平评估，结果表明GBA或TEMPO在不同评价标准下通常表现最优。同时，研究还按地理位置、人口密度和收入群体对各数据集的准确性进行了分层分析，发现非洲和亚洲的准确性较低，高密度城市区域也存在显著精度下降。

item.title: 基于物理感知的PolSAR图像分类方法CV-SSMNet
item.content:encoded:
提出CV-SSMNet模型提升PolSAR图像分类性能。



摘要： 本文针对PolSAR图像分类中物理知识利用不足的问题，提出CV-SSMNet模型。该模型在复数域构建状态空间模型，以捕捉长距离空间依赖关系，并通过七种物理散射先验进行特征调制，实现物理知识引导的特征演化。实验表明，CV-SSMNet在多个数据集上表现出色，提升了分类精度和边界保持能力。

item.title: 基于MoAKE框架的统一动作质量评估方法
item.content:encoded:
提出MoAKE框架实现统一动作质量评估，提升模型泛化能力。



摘要： 本文研究了统一动作质量评估（AQA）任务，旨在通过单一模型评估多种动作。针对现有方法依赖特定动作类型模型、泛化能力差的问题，提出Mixture of Action Knowledge Experts (MoAKE)框架。该框架通过学习互补专家捕捉多样动作模式，并动态聚合知识以适应输入动作。每个专家采用段落感知原型处理不同时间长度，结合自适应时序关系建模模块，提升评估效果。实验表明，MoAKE在长期数据集上优于现有方法，在短时数据集的零样本/少样本场景下也表现出色。

item.title: NavVerse：面向室内外导航的物理仿真基准
item.content:encoded:
NavVerse提供室内外导航基准，评估机器人任务成功率与安全性。



摘要： 文章介绍了NavVerse，一个用于室内外具身导航的物理仿真基准。该基准包含100个室内场景、50个城市户外场景和50个室内外过渡场景，以及10,000个涵盖目标导航、视觉语言导航和地点导航的任务。通过可执行机器人接口，评估任务成功率、路径效率和安全性。实验表明，现有智能体在跨场景导航上仍存在显著差距，视觉语言模型表现最佳，而模块化方法在安全性上更优。研究还指出，从户外到室内外过渡场景的性能下降表明适应性仍是主要瓶颈。

item.title: FMRP-LEAN：基于AI的临床研究工作流管理系统
item.content:encoded:
FMRP-LEAN提升临床研究流程的可追踪性与安全性。



摘要： 本文介绍了一种名为FMRP-LEAN的HIPAA合规、AI增强的实验室信息管理系统（LIMS），旨在解决临床生物标志物研究中数据跟踪、质量控制和跨团队协作的挑战。该系统通过有限状态工作流模型实现生物样本全生命周期管理，结合自托管数据库、加密隧道和双向REDCap同步，确保数据安全与可追溯性。系统还引入自动化统计质量控制和受约束的AI操作模块，提升流程透明度与效率。部署结果显示，该架构有效改善了工作流可见性、减少质量控制延迟，并增强了实验室与临床团队之间的协作。

item.title: 大语言模型中的奉承行为具有多种模式
item.content:encoded:
研究发现大语言模型的奉承行为由多种不同模式构成。



摘要： 本文研究了大语言模型在面对用户观点时倾向于迎合而非追求事实准确性的行为，即奉承行为。传统研究认为奉承是一种单一的行为维度，但本文通过分析948种社会压力情境，提出三种假设的奉承模式。尽管这些模式生成的文本高度相似，仅靠文本分类器识别准确率仅为57.8%，但它们的内部表征在第14层之后可被线性分离。研究还发现，不同模式在处理阶段、注意力机制和输入敏感度上存在差异，表明奉承并非单一倾向，而是一组结构化、计算上不同的模式，为更精确的测量与干预提供了依据。

item.title: 基于Clopper-Pearson置信区间的LLM有害输出概率分析框架
item.content:encoded:
提出一种计算LLM有害输出概率的严格边界方法。



摘要： 本文提出了一种新的框架，用于计算大型语言模型（LLM）对给定提示生成有害输出的概率上限。研究引入了Clopper-Pearson置信区间的新应用，以获得可能近似正确（PAC）的边界。主要技术贡献是设计了一个算法，利用潜在空间特征优先探索更可能产生有害输出的自回归生成树分支。该方法在真实有害概率极低的情况下仍能高效计算有效的下界，并且这些下界是可靠的，即经过形式化证明小于实际有害概率。实验结果表明，该方法在最先进的LLM上能够计算出非平凡的下界，为LLM的评估和统计认证提供了新途径。

item.title: 基于生理信号的疼痛定位方法比较研究
item.content:encoded:
研究比较了传统特征工程与深度学习在疼痛定位中的表现。



摘要： 本文探讨了通过生理信号实现自动疼痛定位的方法，重点比较了传统特征工程与深度序列学习在非语言患者中的应用效果。研究使用AI4Pain 2026挑战数据集，包含四种同步可穿戴设备信号，分析了时间域、频域及跨模态特征。结果表明，极端随机树模型表现最佳，优于深度模型7.4个百分点，且皮肤电活动频谱特征为关键区分因素。研究还发现疼痛检测与定位性能存在显著差距，反映出外周自主神经通路的解剖扩散性对定位精度的限制。

item.title: PhenSPINE数据集与脊柱病理诊断的深度学习研究
item.content:encoded:
PhenSPINE数据集提升脊柱MRI诊断准确率。



摘要： 本研究提出PhenSPINE数据集，包含250名患者的16,813张MRI图像，用于推动脊柱病理自动诊断的深度学习研究。研究采用先进的卷积网络和位置编码机制，重点分析不同MRI序列的诊断价值。结果显示，矢状位T2加权序列表现最佳，Macro F1分数达50.31%。多序列融合策略效果不佳，因各序列间存在显著的噪声干扰。该工作为脊柱分析提供了可靠基准和序列选择的参考。

item.title: 时间序列基础模型的后训练方法分析与未来方向
item.content:encoded:
分析TSFM后训练方法，提出五类干预方式及未来研究方向。



摘要： 时间序列基础模型（TSFMs）在时序分析中表现出强大的通用性，但仅靠预训练难以满足下游任务的需求。为解决领域偏移、任务异质性、监督不足和计算限制等问题，后训练方法被广泛采用。本文根据干预位置将后训练方法分为五类：参数适应、上下文增强、模型组合、输出处理与不确定性控制、压缩与专业化。文章对各类方法进行了深入分析，并指出未来方向，包括可控适应、可靠上下文构建、不确定性感知模型组合、校准输出处理和部署感知专业化，旨在为TSFM的可靠下游应用提供统一框架。

item.title: 局部Lipschitz函数的强大数定律及其应用
item.content:encoded:
研究局部Lipschitz函数的强大数定律及其应用。



摘要： 本文证明了在Lipschitz伪度量下，局部Lipschitz函数的强大数定律成立。该结论在拓扑或模型论条件下均有效，后者涵盖了在o-极小结构中联合定义的函数，但进一步扩展至更广泛的函数类。应用包括极限次微分和Clarke次微分的均匀收敛性，以及有限样本下解的识别。因此，我们确定了某些函数类，其先前负面结果中揭示的失败现象不会发生。

item.title: 基于LLM的推荐框架PRTA提升全排序推荐性能
item.content:encoded:
PRTA框架通过LLM与推荐模型协作提升全排序推荐效果。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于大语言模型（LLM）的推荐框架PRTA，旨在解决传统LLM代理在推荐系统中因幻觉和上下文长度限制而无法胜任全排序任务的问题。该框架将LLM作为中央规划者，与多个推荐模型作为工具协同工作，LLM负责高层次推理和个性化工具选择，而传统推荐模型则执行全排序评分，发挥其在行为模式建模中的可扩展性。为实现个性化工具选择，设计了反射机制，使代理能够根据用户资料和候选列表评估和比较工具。实验表明，PRTA在三个公开数据集上优于传统推荐和LLM基线方法。

item.title: 基于可靠性感知的序列到序列模型知识蒸馏方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出两种改进的知识蒸馏方法，显著提升摘要任务效果。



摘要： 本文针对序列到序列模型知识蒸馏（KD）的效果进行研究，发现标准KD在Bangla摘要任务中提升有限，且多数样本可能对训练产生负面影响。为此，作者提出了两种可靠性感知的蒸馏方法：CHAD和EWAD+CPDP。CHAD通过梯度对齐评估样本的蒸馏价值，并使用轻量门控机制进行筛选；EWAD+CPDP结合了基于熵加权的自适应蒸馏与容量比例约束。实验表明，这两种方法在BanSum数据集上分别提升了ROUGE-L指标0.0173和0.0219，优于标准KD和更大的基线模型。此外，方法在多种语言上的表现也验证了其有效性。

item.title: 基于多模态大语言模型的电子元件符号与封装生成方法
item.content:encoded:
SFgen通过多模态大模型实现电子元件符号和封装的高精度自动生成。



摘要： 文章介绍了一种基于多模态大语言模型（MLLMs）的自动化电子元件符号与封装生成方法——SFgen。传统上，工程师需要手动创建符号和封装，耗时且容易出错。SFgen在符号生成方面达到86%的准确率，在封装生成方面达到80%的准确率。利用该方法，研究团队构建了SFnet数据库，目前已包含1000个元件，并持续扩展，为PCB设计的自动化奠定了基础。

item.title: StereoFlow：一种基于先验引导的生成式立体匹配框架
item.content:encoded:
StereoFlow通过生成式方法提升立体匹配精度与细节表现。



摘要： StereoFlow是一种新型的立体匹配框架，通过引入先验引导的生成式方法，结合确定性匹配回归与分布建模，解决了传统方法在模糊区域的回归偏差问题。其核心包括三部分：多阶段渐进级联匹配网络、频率解耦的像素扩散Transformer（StereoDiT）以及高效的流匹配目标（Transition Flow Matching）。该方法在多个基准数据集上取得了多项最先进的性能，特别是在几何一致性与细粒度细节方面表现出色。

item.title: 轻量级C++/Qt框架StrokeSeg2实现高效脑卒中病灶分割部署
item.content:encoded:
StrokeSeg2优化3D脑卒中分割模型，提升临床部署效率。



摘要： 本文介绍了一个名为StrokeSeg2的轻量级、模块化、跨平台的C++/Qt框架，旨在将资源密集型的3D脑卒中分割流程转化为可移植和可重复的应用程序。通过知识蒸馏和ONNX Runtime的Float16量化优化，显著提升了模型在不同硬件（CPU、集成GPU、独立GPU）上的效率。研究发现，架构压缩是效率提升的主要因素，使能耗降低90%以上，推理时间平均减少84%。最优学生模型参数仅为0.84M，将原始教师模型从102.3M参数压缩至2.1MB，同时保持良好的病灶定位和分割性能。StrokeSeg2支持Windows、macOS和Linux系统，并提供图形界面和命令行接口，无需Docker或外部依赖，便于临床研究环境中的部署。

item.title: 机器去记忆：基于优化视角的可证明去学习算法研究
item.content:encoded:
研究可证明的机器去学习算法及其优化效率。



摘要： 本文研究了机器去记忆问题，即从训练好的模型中移除记忆的训练数据。作者从优化角度探讨了可证明去学习的算法复杂性，并将去学习目标定义为同时实现可证明去学习和优化准确性。通过引入统一凸正则化器，证明了初始模型与去学习后模型之间的距离新界。研究表明，若被移除的数据能被去学习后的模型良好预测，则对应的优化问题较为简单。此外，作者提出了一种具有各向异性高斯机制的二阶去学习算法，并证明其具有最先进的全局收敛性。该方法在具有准自共轭损失的线性模型中实现了快速可证明去学习，并应用于逻辑回归和指数回归，验证了二阶信息相比一阶方法的优势。

item.title: 可解释模型的交互性约束与可微损失函数研究
item.content:encoded:
提出四边形损失以衡量特征交互，提升模型可解释性。



摘要： 本文探讨了加法模型通过禁止特征交互来获得可解释性的方法，并引入了一种可微损失函数——四边形损失，用于衡量特征交互行为。该损失基于训练样本对的二阶混合差异，仅在特征无交互时为零，适用于分段线性网络。实验表明，这种损失能有效控制模型交互性，提高准确性和可解释性。研究还比较了多种实现精确可加性的方法，发现行为约束优于权重空间约束，且不同数据环境下交互排名会发生变化。最后指出三种隐性失败模式，其根源在于预设条件未被满足。

item.title: 利用生物医学知识图谱提升自动注释验证效率
item.content:encoded:
通过知识图谱提升生物注释验证效率，优化专家评审优先级。



摘要： 随着生物医学知识的快速增长，自动生成的生物注释验证成为生物医学注释中的主要瓶颈。本文提出一种基于生物医学知识图谱（bioKG）的框架，通过训练关系特定的二分类器并结合社区负采样策略，提高候选注释的置信度估计准确性。同时引入一系列可解释的合理性度量，结合分类器置信度、可靠性及语义上下文，更有效地指导专家评审。实验结果表明，该方法在五个大型bioKG上平均提升了5.8%的平衡准确率，并优于仅依赖分类器置信度的方案，从而提高了AI辅助生物医学注释的效率。

item.title: 提升语音语言模型推理能力的ECoM推理框架
item.content:encoded:
提出ECoM推理框架提升语音模型数学问答准确率。



摘要： 本文针对语音语言模型（SLMs）在数学问答任务中推理能力不足的问题，提出了一种名为ECoM Reasoning的新框架。该框架通过压缩文本内容，使其同时作为语音引导和推理表示，提高了推理准确性，同时减少了token使用量。研究还引入了Progressive Compression训练策略，逐步提升模型的压缩推理能力。实验结果显示，ECoM Reasoning在数学问答任务中比标准CoM架构提升了21%的准确率，且仅使用40%的文本token，证明其在提升推理能力的同时保持了高效性。

item.title: 提升RLHF管道中评分效率的C++推理引擎优化
item.content:encoded:
C++推理引擎显著提升RLHF评分效率，释放生成资源。



摘要： 本文探讨了在RLHF（人类反馈强化学习）流程中，评分模块对策略更新的瓶颈影响。研究发现，评分速度慢会拖慢整个流程，而传统方法如PyTorch eager模式或torch.compile并未达到最优性能。作者开发了一个基于ONNX Runtime的原生C++推理引擎，并验证其精度与PyTorch参考结果高度一致。测试表明，在CPU上该引擎优于所有基线，而在GPU上虽不及torch.compile，但整体性能提升显著。研究还指出，批处理策略对性能影响更大，而非语言或运行时选择。结果来自多次独立实验，确保可靠性。

item.title: Back to Back with a Copy: A Computational Analysis of AI-Generated Visual Contemporary Art Pastiches
item.content:encoded: The aim of this paper is twofold. First, it investigates whether newer generative models are getting better at pastiching contemporary artworks. Second, it explores the consistency of the multidimensional nature of stylistic evaluation across different LLMs. Building on previous work, we analyze stylistic similarity between AI generated pastiches and the original artworks of twelve contemporary artists. We used five complementary computer vision models to capture texture, color, semantics, composition, and perceptual features through cosine distance in high-dimensional embedding spaces. The distances obtained show that the newer image generation model that we used has produced pastiches with improved semantic alignment and greater diversity than the model used in previous work. However, it was slightly less performant on shallow features such as color, texture, and perceptual adherence. Our findings confirm that artistic style is inherently multidimensional, and measuring it does not depend on any spatial architecture. These quantitative findings are contextualized through feedback from human evaluators, which are the artists themselves.

item.title: HalluTruthQA: A Fine-Grained Benchmark for Hallucination Detection, Localization, and Explanation in Arabic Question Answering
item.content:encoded: Large language models (LLMs) can generate fluent Arabic answers, yet factual errors remain difficult to detect, localize, explain, and verify. Existing hallucination benchmarks often provide response-level labels, with limited support for identifying the exact erroneous content, explaining why it is incorrect, or selecting the correct factual answer. We introduce \textsc{HalluTruthQA}, a fine-grained benchmark for hallucination evaluation in Arabic question answering. The benchmark contains 2,400 expert-curated examples across four knowledge-intensive domains: Islamic knowledge, history, science, and geography. Each example pairs an Arabic question and a model-generated answer with a verified reference answer, a binary hallucination label, six candidate answers for factual verification, and, for hallucinated answers, character-level erroneous spans, human-written explanations, and macro and micro hallucination types. We evaluate four open-source LLMs, \textsc{Allam}, \textsc{Falcon-H1}, \textsc{Qwen32}, and \textsc{Silma}, in a zero-shot setting across hallucination detection, span-level localization, factual verification, and explanation evaluation. Results show that these tasks capture different abilities: no single model achieves the strongest performance across all tasks, with best scores of 0.880 Macro-F1 for detection, 0.516 F1-Sp for localization, 0.852 LO-Score for factual verification, and 0.644 final score for explanation evaluation. Our taxonomy shows that hallucination evaluation should move beyond detection toward localizing, verifying, and explaining factual errors. The code, dataset, prompts, and evaluation scripts are available at https://gitlab.com/nlpresearcher/HalluTruthQA.

item.title: Autonomous Collaborative Learning Among an Ensemble of Tsetlin Machines with Consensus-Based Inference
item.content:encoded: Tsetlin Machine (TM) is a rule-based machine-learning algorithm comprising collectives of two-action Tsetlin Automata (TAs) that cooperatively form conjunctive logical clauses from Boolean inputs through stochastic feedback. Although few recent studies have examined TM Federated Learning, the broader area of distributed and decentralized TM learning has not received much attention in the existing literature and warrants further exploration. In this work, we propose a paradigm for decentralized collaborative learning under a vertical feature-partitioning setting among an ensemble of Tsetlin Machines using consensus-based inference. Within this decentralized paradigm, each agent maintains its own private TM model, and there is no exchange of raw data among agents. Inference combines individual agents model predictions into a global consensus. The paradigm accommodates heterogeneous TM-based agents with differing data acquisition means, local data distributions, or computational resources, thereby facilitating the integration and fusion of information in settings such as multi-modal sensing environments. Experiments conducted using two-dimensional grid and connected graph network topologies demonstrate that the classification accuracies achieved are comparable to those of centralized models.

item.title: Defense Against LLM Backdoors using Critical Neuron Isolation Pruning
item.content:encoded: Large language models (LLMs) are vulnerable to backdoor attacks, where hidden triggers induce malicious outputs. Existing defenses generally fall into inference-time detection or training-time mitigation, but face two key limitations. First, they focus on fine-tuning-based backdoors (e.g., PEFT modules) and fail to address insidious model-editing attacks that bypass training pipelines. Second, they target simple classification settings and do not naturally extend to open-ended LLM generation and do not naturally extend to the open-ended generation characteristics of LLMs. Consequently, these methods focus on surface-level behavioral patterns while neglecting the deeper representational causes of malicious activations. This lack of mechanistic understanding forces defenses to depend on empirical heuristics, limiting their robustness, generality, and practical applicability in real-world LLM deployment. To bridge this gap, we introduce DeCNIP (Defense with Critical Neuron Isolation Pruning), which leverages representational analysis to identify and neutralize backdoors in a unified pipeline. Specifically, DeCNIP identifies trigger-like behaviors by optimizing a cross-entropy loss between harmful prompts with candidate tokens and benign inputs. This representational discovery exposes latent threats by uncovering mechanisms through which triggers hijack model weights. It then isolates Backdoor Critical Neurons (BCNs) and prunes them selectively to remove malicious influence while preserving model utility. Extensive evaluations on six open-source LLMs and two benchmark datasets demonstrate that DeCNIP achieves over 95% relative reduction in Attack Success Rate (ASR), outperforming seven state-of-the-art defenses with only 0.1% neuron intervention. Moreover, it maintains 97% of the model's performance on normal benchmarks, demonstrating its efficacy, robustness, and scalability.

item.title: Variance-reduced Domain Adaptation using Paired Sampling
item.content:encoded: Correlation alignment and the maximum mean discrepancy are two widely used distribution-matching frameworks for unsupervised domain adaptation (UDA). However, high variance in these losses has been shown to undermine their effectiveness in minibatch optimisation settings. Furthermore, the losses lack finite-sum structure, which renders them incompatible with classical stochastic variance reduction (SVR) methods. This paper proposes Paired Sampling for Domain Adaptation (PSDA), a novel SVR technique tailored to such objectives. PSDA pairs observations both within and across domains, to form quadruplets that are always sampled together during training. The pairings are designed to minimise expected gradient variance, and reduce to solving a set of linear assignment problems. Our simulations demonstrate reduced variance compared to related methods, and experiments on three domain shift datasets show improved target domain accuracy.

item.title: Memory-Augmented Multimodal Large Language Models for Small Object Understanding in Streaming Aerial Videos
item.content:encoded: Language-guided aerial perception aims to understand user-specified tiny targets in complex unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) scenes. In real UAV deployment, the UAV must respond while it flies, so such perception runs in an online streaming manner, where frames arrive sequentially and the model responds to each one without access to future frames. However, applying current Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) to this setting raises two challenges. First, targets viewed from the air are often tiny, yet the visual compression in existing MLLMs treats all regions equally and discards their fine-grained details. Second, understanding a continuous stream requires past-frame context, yet retaining the entire history is infeasible on resource-constrained onboard hardware, whereas discarding it causes the target to drift or disappear. We address the tiny object and streaming challenges from both data and method perspectives. From the data perspective, we present \textbf{DroneEyes}, the \textbf{first} pixel-level and open-vocabulary referring-segmentation dataset for tiny aerial targets, comprising $2,140$ high-definition videos and $176,623$ pairs across Object Description and Referring Expression tasks, with dense per-frame masks. From the method perspective, we propose \textbf{SkyAnchor}, an MLLM with two designs to the above challenges: a Semantics-Aware Token Router that preserves small-target under a reduced visual-token budget, and a Hierarchical Memory Bank that keeps the target consistently understood on streams.

item.title: Not All Patches are Equal: Sampling Matters for Visible-Infrared Pre-Training
item.content:encoded: Visible-infrared (VIS-IR) alignment is a key pre-training task for robust multi-sensor perception. Most existing methods use uniform patch-wise contrastive learning, but this can be unreliable in VIS-IR data because imaging-physics differences make some spatially paired regions inherently less comparable, and aligning them with equal strength hinders representation learning and downstream transfer. In this paper, we revisit VIS-IR pre-training from a sampling perspective and propose Importance-Aware Sampling (IAS), which adjusts training emphasis based on patch reliability. Specifically, IAS (i) derives patch weights from infrared structural cues and uses them to reweight the contrastive objective; (ii) learns a soft importance mask with a lightweight sampler, optionally warm-started from the hand-crafted prior; and (iii) employs a patch curriculum learning strategy that gradually expands from high-reliability regions to harder patches. It is worth noting that IAS is plug-and-play and works with both patch-/correlation-level alignment (e.g., UNIV-style) and image-level contrastive baselines (e.g., ImageBind-style). Extensive experiments on multiple VIS-IR benchmarks demonstrate consistent improvements over strong baselines, including for IR semantic segmentation, IR object detection and VIS semantic segmentation and cross-modal retrieval task. Code will be released on https://github.com/KlayMa527/IAS.

item.title: OLEDLM: A Unified Language Model for OLED Molecular Design
item.content:encoded: The development of organic light-emitting diode (OLED) materials faces the compounded challenges of an astronomically large chemical space, stringent quantum-chemical constraints, and a scarcity of labeled data. Although the question of OLED generation is important, few models have been trained effectively for this specific domain. We propose an inverse molecular design framework based on causal language models: given target optoelectronic properties (e.g., excitation energy, oscillator strength), our model directly generates OLED SMILES sequences satisfying the specified constraints. We employ a multi-stage strategy: first, we establish a foundational chemical language model using a LLaMA-style transformer architecture. To the best of our knowledge, this represents the first successful adaptation of LLMs specifically for the OLED domain, bridging the gap between generic molecular generation and the stringent structural requirements of optoelectronic materials. Second, we fine-tune property predictors based on a BERT model pre-trained on our large-scale OLED dataset. Then, we perform Reinforcement Learning on our fine-tuned model, leveraging our property predictor, for better SMILES generation. Finally, through DFT verification, we demonstrate that our framework can efficiently navigate the OLED chemical space, generating novel candidates with high structural validity and optimized optoelectronic properties.

item.title: Antigen-specific Antibody Multi-modal Foundation Model for Functional Antibody Design
item.content:encoded: Antibodies are essential proteins that play a central role in immune recognition by binding specific antigen molecules. Although recent protein language models have enabled progress in single-chain protein modeling and generation, they often fall short in antigen-specific antibody design, where effective modeling requires explicit pairing between antibody and antigen, particularly at the epitope level. To address these limitations, we introduce AAMFM, an Antigen-specific Antibody Multimodal Foundation Model that learns unified representations of antibody sequences and structures conditioned on antigen context. AAMFM incorporates rich antigen information including geometric interfaces and epitope annotations via a cross-modal adapter, enabling joint modeling of antibody-antigen interactions in a shared latent space. To further guide the model toward functional relevance, we fine-tune AAMFM using Calibrated Direct Preference Optimization (Cal-DPO), leveraging preference signals extracted from a strong structural prior to align learning with binding-specific objectives. Extensive experiments demonstrate that AAMFM achieves state-of-the-art performance in functional antibody design, revealing its potential for antigen-specific antibody engineering. Our code is available at https://github.com/XL-S224/AAMFM.

item.title: Pushing the Frontier of Full-Song Generation: Hierarchical Autoregressive Planning Meets Flow-Matching Rendering
item.content:encoded: In this report, we present a unified song generation framework capable of producing high-quality full-length music from lyrics, text descriptions, and musical attributes. The proposed framework supports three tasks: Lyrics-to-Song Generation, which generates complete songs from text descriptions, lyrics, and musical attributes; Instrumental Music Generation, which creates music without vocals; and Cover Song Generation, which reinterprets existing songs with different styles while preserving their melodic content. Architecturally, our system consists of four main components: a semantic-aware tokenizer, hybird-LM, FullDiT, and a two-level melody module. The tokenizer encodes audio into 8-codebook RVQ tokens for efficient discrete music representation. Based on these tokens, hybird-LM performs hierarchical autoregressive audio-token modeling for full-song generation. To improve audio fidelity, FullDiT performs full-song flow matching in a continuous VAE latent space conditioned on codec tokens, lyrics, and text captions. For cover song generation, the melody module extracts and discretizes melody cues from reference audio to guide generation while preserving the original melodic content. Finally, we investigate DPO, GRPO, and OPD as reward-based post-training strategies for hybird-LM and apply flow-based GRPO to FullDiT to improve musicality and rendering quality. Experimental results on a multilingual automatic benchmark, complemented by the Artificial Analysis Music with Vocals leaderboard, show that the proposed framework achieves competitive performance in the evaluated settings.

item.title: WASABI: Whole-graph Assignment-based Stabilizer for lAne topology By Inter-frame tracking
item.content:encoded: Autonomous driving requires understanding the road as a graph of drivable lanes and their connectivity, beyond the ego lane alone, to follow routes through intersections and reason about cross- and merging-traffic. Recent perception models infer such lane topology, i.e., lane segments together with their inter-lane connectivity (LCLC), from onboard sensors over a 360-degree BEV view. Due to neural perception's imperfections, their outputs retain structural instabilities such as missed detections, lost or incorrect LCLC, over-detection, and label flicker. This paper presents WASABI, a real-time post-processing pipeline that stabilizes lane topology outputs both within and across frames by treating lane segments and their LCLC connectivity as joint tracking targets, under onboard real-time constraints (10 Hz / 20 ms / up to 200 input lanes). The pipeline integrates segment tracking with connectivity, noise-robust topology-aware refinement, and a resource-constrained real-time design. On internal validation data (16 sequences), WASABI improves LCLC detection F1 from 0.834 to 0.948 (+0.114, +13.6%) and reduces centerline lateral error from 2.50 m to 0.95 m, while reducing detection false-positives by 24.6%. Temporal-stability metrics on the same data show LCLC toggle rate reduced by 63.3% and boundary-label flicker rate by 30.2%, confirming across-frame stabilization beyond per-frame accuracy.

item.title: Reinforcement Learning for Large Language Model Selective Evidence Adoption from Contaminated Retrieval Results
item.content:encoded: Retrieval-augmented large language models frequently face contexts that interleave useful evidence with misleading statements or instruction-like content. Blanket refusal discards valid evidence, whereas uncritical adoption yields incorrect or unsafe answers. The ability to selectively adopt relevant information while rejecting deceptive or harmful content is therefore critical for reliable deployment in real-world retrieval settings. We introduce SelectBench, a controlled benchmark and training set for selective evidence adoption, and post-train Qwen3.5-4B directly with DAPO using either deterministic rule rewards or a frozen semantic judge. On the corrected 325-example SelectBench-v2 test set, strict success rises from 22.46% for the original checkpoint to 25.54% with DAPO-Rule and 26.46% with DAPO-DeepSeek. Both trained policies reduce forbidden-content adoption and produce shorter, more focused responses, yet prompt-injection following does not improve. The paired gains are modest and fail to survive Holm correction, suggesting that stronger reward shaping or additional training iterations may be needed for more robust gains. DAPO-DeepSeek exhibits no material degradation on MMLU or clean HotpotQA, indicating that the post-training procedure preserves general capabilities. These results demonstrate a directional improvement in selective evidence use, while identifying injection resistance and statistical robustness as important remaining challenges for future work.

item.title: Toward Reliable RGB-D Semantic Segmentation: Handling Missing Modalities via Condition Dropout
item.content:encoded: RGB-D semantic segmentation has achieved remarkable progress, yet most models assume that RGB and depth are always available. In practice, failures or occlusions of surveillance sensors often remove one modality. Although RGB or depth alone can contain sufficient cues, models trained only on full-modality inputs fail to exploit the remaining modality once one is missing, causing severe degradation. We tackle this issue with a simple continued-training paradigm, \emph{Condition Dropout (ConD)}, which mitigates degradation while preserving full-modality accuracy. Starting from a pretrained RGB-D model, ConD adds a second stage that randomly simulates complete, RGB-missing, and depth-missing inputs, freezes the original encoders, and trains copied encoders with zero-initialized feature injection. Experiments on NYU-Depth V2 and SUN RGB-D show that ConD improves robustness under missing modalities and even yields slight gains when modalities are complete. Our code will be made publicly available upon acceptance.

item.title: Local Causal Structure Learning in the Presence of Latent Variables and Selection Bias
item.content:encoded: Discovering the direct causes and effects of a target variable from observational data is a fundamental problem in causal discovery, with broad applications in domains such as gene regulatory analysis and biomedical research. Existing causal discovery methods either learn a global causal structure, which incurs substantial computational cost, or assume the absence of latent variables and selection bias, assumptions that are often violated in real-world settings. Motivated by these challenges, we study local causal structure learning in the presence of latent variables and selection bias. Specifically, we first characterize a local region that enables target-specific causal discovery without recovering the entire global structure. We then establish a theoretical bridge between causal information learned from the observed distribution induced on this local region and the corresponding information in the global causal structure. Building on these foundations, we propose LoCaLS, a local causal structure learning algorithm that is sound and complete under standard assumptions and identifies the same direct causes and effects of a target variable as those identifiable by global causal discovery methods, while allowing for latent variables and selection bias. Extensive experiments on random and real-world structures demonstrate that the proposed method consistently achieves higher structural accuracy than existing local methods while requiring substantially less computational effort than state-of-the-art global methods. Furthermore, applications to two real-world gene expression datasets reveal biologically plausible target-specific causal structures, demonstrating its practical applicability in large-scale biological data analysis.

item.title: Interval and fuzzy physics-augmented neural networks (iPANN and fPANN) for uncertainty quantification and propagation in constitutive modeling
item.content:encoded: Constitutive modeling under uncertainty remains a central challenge for reliable mechanics simulations, particularly when the available stress-deformation data are sparse, noisy, or heterogeneous. We propose interval and fuzzy physics-augmented neural networks (iPANNs and fPANNs) for uncertainty-aware hyperelastic constitutive modeling. iPANNs learn sparse lower, mean, and upper free energy density branches whose stresses, obtained by automatic differentiation, ultimately enclose noisy stress observations. In contrast to this deterministic interval description, fPANNs embed the learned iPANN branches into a fuzzy-set representation through alpha-cut interpolation, yielding a nested family of admissible responses. iPANNs and fPANNs encode mechanistic constraints - preserving objectivity, consistency and promoting polyconvexity - and smoothed L0 regularization promotes interpretable energy representations. The bound models are trained through a two-stage transfer-learning procedure in which a sparse mean constitutive response is learned first and then fine-tuned into lower and upper energy branches. We evaluate the framework on synthetic isotropic hyperelastic data with heteroscedastic noise, varying random realizations, shifted noise means, and varying noise magnitudes. The results show that the learned bounds enclose noisy stress observations while generalizing to the test set. Further, we examine the propagation of uncertainty through the mean, upper and lower bound predictions of the learned iPANN models in a finite element setting. The proposed framework provides a compact, physics-consistent route for distribution-free aleatoric uncertainty quantification in hyperelastic constitutive modeling, and propagation in downstream finite element simulations.

item.title: VizRAG: Enhancing Retrieval-Augmented Generation with Hypergraph Visualization
item.content:encoded: Hypergraph-based RAG systems surpass traditional graph-based approaches by organizing complex n-ary atomic facts among entities, rather than relying solely on binary relationships. Despite the advancements in multimodal large language models (MLLMs) with enhanced visual capabilities, current hypergraph-based RAG frameworks predominantly restrict knowledge retrieval and reconstruction to a unimodal, text-centric paradigm. This limitation prevents them from fully leveraging the powerful visual perception capabilities of modern MLLMs. To address this gap, we systematically explore the integration of hypergraph awareness in RAG systems through visual cues. By incorporating visual representations of hypergraphs into the RAG pipeline, we introduce VizRAG, the first RAG system to support visual hypergraph structure awareness. Experimental results demonstrate that VizRAG significantly outperforms strong baselines, validating the promising potential of hypergraph visualization as a novel approach for RAG systems.

item.title: Anatomy of a Sound Neural Reasoner: One-Shot Amortization, First-Pass Poisoning, and Search Inertness in Clue-Rich Completion
item.content:encoded: Neural solvers are built to deduce, branch, and revise intermediate states. The Lattice Deduction Transformer (LDT) appears to do exactly that. In clue-rich Sudoku, it does not: one forward pass commits essentially the entire grid (every blank cell on standard 6x6, 94-96% on augmented 9x9), turning the iterative solver into a one-shot predictor wrapped in an exact verifier. All hard-slice failures are decided before search begins, when the first pass confidently deletes a value required by the true solution. We call this first-pass poisoning. Adding learned branching, MRV, backtracking, value exclusion, and shared nogoods (CoLT) does not change which Sudoku instances are solved; it cuts repeated invalid derivations 1,497-fold. At the frozen training budget, constraint-graph attention alone matches full-CoLT accuracy, while positional tables recover only under substantially longer training, indicating an optimization and sample-efficiency advantage rather than an absolute capacity difference. The diagnosis predicts two effective interventions. Digit-permutation augmentation raises 9x9 accuracy from below 1% to 96.5 +/- 0.3 across three training seeds on a symmetry-disjoint split. Test-time union over symmetry-transformed passes raises all three hard-slice checkpoints from 72.8-78.9% to 100% without retraining. On from-scratch graph coloring, one-shot behavior disappears and search changes accuracy. In clue-rich completion, LDT-like systems are one-shot amortized predictors rather than learned search procedures: accuracy is determined by calibration and symmetry, while search primarily removes computational waste.

item.title: Analytic Distribution of Classifier-Free Guidance for Schedule Design
item.content:encoded: Classifier-free guidance (CFG) is the default mechanism for conditional generation in diffusion models, but the distribution sampled by its deterministic guided dynamics is not captured by the usual product-distribution heuristic $p_0^ωq_0^{1-ω}$. We analyze CFG through the probability flow ODE and derive exact analytic path-integral representations of the induced distributions for both constant and time-dependent guidance. The resulting formulas show that CFG modifies $p_{t_0}$ by an exponential path-integral correction, and that a time-dependent schedule enters this correction through the weight $ω(t)-1$. This characterization explains how score discrepancies accumulate along sampling trajectories and motivates Distribution-Guided CFG (DG-CFG), a schedule that balances timestep contributions while accounting for signal strength and low-noise score-error amplification. A toy model with analytic scores closely verifies the predicted distributions. On Stable Diffusion~1.5, DG-CFG improves generation and yields a stronger diversity--fidelity trade-off across guidance strengths, with especially clear gains when strong guidance causes saturation and quality degradation in constant and heuristic schedules. Across NFE budgets, DG-CFG reaches fixed image-quality targets with fewer sampling steps, reducing the sampling cost needed to achieve target metrics.

item.title: OpenSkillRisk: Benchmarking Agent Safety When Using Real-World Risky Third-Party Skills
item.content:encoded: LLM-based agents leverage third-party skills to extend their capabilities in open-world scenarios. However, third-party skills can introduce extra security vulnerabilities, as seemingly harmless skills can contain latent safety risks that only emerge during actual execution. In this work, we conduct a systematic investigation into how well current agent systems recognize and avoid such risks. To support quantitative and qualitative evaluation, we construct OpenSkillRisk, a dedicated safety benchmark containing 263 risky skills collected from public skill marketplaces. We classify these skills into seven categories based on their threat types and pair each skill with a standardized user task and a corresponding sandbox for controlled evaluation. Distinct from prior benchmarks, OpenSkillRisk not only covers more realistic and diverse unsafe scenarios, but also provides a fine-grained analysis to diagnose the behavioral patterns of agents in such scenarios. We conduct comprehensive experiments covering three mainstream CLI agent frameworks and thirteen state-of-the-art LLMs. Experimental results show that no tested system handles risky skills reliably: even the safest configurations still execute unsafe actions in about 17% of cases. Context-dependent and system-level risks are especially difficult for current agent systems to avoid. Our behavioral analysis reveals three recurring failure patterns: agents may fail to recognize the risk, recognize it but fail to intervene before acting, or follow skill instructions beyond the user's intended scope. These findings highlight the need to improve both risk reasoning in LLMs and execution control in agent frameworks.

item.title: Current Injection Spiking Neural Network for Infrared and Visible Image Fusion
item.content:encoded: Infrared and visible image fusion (IVIF) integrates the complementary information of two modalities into a single image with richer scene content. While existing methods are largely built on artificial neural networks (ANNs), which densely compute over all activations, spiking neural networks (SNNs) communicate through sparse binary spikes and compute only where and when a spike occurs, offering a route to more energy-efficient fusion. However, directly applying SNNs to IVIF creates a fundamental tension: cross-modal fusion relies on fine-grained responses from both modalities, whereas binary spikes can discard complementary cues that remain below the firing threshold. The membrane potential retains these subthreshold responses before firing, letting both modalities jointly shape the output when integrated at this stage. Building on this, we propose CIS-Fuse, a spiking network that performs cross-modal fusion directly at the membrane-potential level. At its core is the current injection spiking (CIS) operator, which injects one modality as a gated auxiliary current into the driving neuron of the other, so the two integrate before spike firing, with a per-channel learnable injection strength that adaptively regulates the modulation magnitude. Building on CIS, we construct a bidirectional cross-modal fusion (BCMF) module and deploy it on a dual-branch architecture with asymmetric stacking depths, where the two branches develop a clear functional specialization. Extensive experiments on four IVIF benchmarks and on downstream detection and segmentation show that CIS-Fuse achieves fusion quality on par with state-of-the-art ANN-based methods while inheriting the energy efficiency of spike-based computation, with roughly an order of magnitude lower inference energy than the similarly-sized ANN-based DCEvo. Code will be released upon publication.

item.title: 基于测试时缩放的无人机视觉语言导航方法
item.content:encoded:
通过测试时缩放提升无人机视觉语言导航性能。



摘要： 本文提出一种无需额外训练的测试时缩放方法，用于提升无人机视觉语言模型（VLMs）在复杂环境中的导航性能。该方法通过迭代优化初始导航计划，生成多个并行候选路径，并进行自纠正以提高准确性和安全性。同时设计多标准评分函数，评估候选路径的安全性、目标对齐度和前进进度，从而实现更可靠和精准的飞行规划。实验表明，该方法在不改变基础模型的情况下，显著提升了导航效果，达到了当前最优性能。

item.title: Optimizing Regret
item.content:encoded: Building on the identity that expected regret equals the covariance between costs and decisions, this paper develops the complete derivative theory of the covariance regret functional. We derive the Gâteaux derivative, showing that the universal steepest-descent direction is the contrarian policy $-(c-\bar{c})$, while ascent yields momentum. For linear policies $\hatπ(c) = Ac+b$, the gradient is the cost covariance matrix $Σ_c$, with a zero Hessian implying boundary-optimal solutions such as the minimum-variance portfolio. We extend to constrained optimization, sign-gradient duality between regret minimization and alpha maximization, finite-sample convergence bounds paralleling Thompson Sampling, and gradient-descent algorithms requiring only input observations, with applications to portfolio tilting and LLM-based allocation strategies.

item.title: 基于LLIFT的医学影像XAI方法验证框架
item.content:encoded:
提出LLIFT生成真实脑部MRI数据，用于XAI方法评估。



摘要： 本文提出LLIFT框架，用于生成带有可控病灶区域的半合成脑部MRI图像，以支持可解释人工智能（XAI）方法的验证。该框架无需像素级病灶标注，通过两种生成模型——LLIFT-GAN和LLIFT-DM——实现病理特征的生成。实验表明，生成图像在Fréchet Inception Distance指标上与真实病理数据相当，并通过定性分析验证了病灶结构的真实性。该数据集为医学影像中的XAI方法提供了空间可控的真实基准数据。

item.title: PLAID-PRF：基于PLAID的伪相关反馈方法提升检索效果
item.content:encoded:
PLAID-PRF通过伪相关反馈优化检索效果，计算成本低且效果显著。



摘要： 本文提出PLAID-PRF，一种在PLAID基础上进行伪相关反馈（PRF）的方法，旨在通过利用PLAID内部的中心向量来优化ColBERT的查询向量。与以往方法不同，PLAID-PRF保持了较低的计算成本，同时通过选取高价值的扩展向量来增强原始查询，并重新运行PLAID以改进候选生成和最终评分。实验表明，PLAID-PRF在MSMARCO和BEIR等多个基准上均显著提升了检索效果，相比PLAID提升了4.3%的nDCG@10和7.3%的MRR@10，同时计算开销远低于传统PRF方法。该方法为高效的反馈感知晚期交互检索提供了有效方案。

item.title: 基于GRPO的DobicVLM在胸部X光报告生成中的应用
item.content:encoded:
DobicVLM提升胸部X光报告生成准确性和医学术语使用。



摘要： 本文介绍了一种名为DobicVLM的视觉-语言模型，用于改善胸部X光报告的自动化生成。该模型结合了MedGemma-4B的监督微调与组相对策略优化（GRPO），并采用可解释的基于规则的奖励组件来确保结构、解剖完整性和语义准确性。在1,000对脱敏图像-报告数据集上训练后，DobicVLM在69个盲测案例中表现优于Gemini 2.5 Flash和MedGemma 4B基线模型，在印象准确率和医学术语使用方面分别达到27.2%和86.5%。尽管在完整性和转诊方面略有不足，但展示了GRPO在资源有限环境中的透明对齐价值。

item.title: 基于暴露的强化学习在排序中的应用改进
item.content:encoded:
提出一种更高效且稳定的强化学习排序方法。



摘要： 本文提出了一种改进的强化学习（RL）方法用于学习排序（LTR），避免了传统方法中对自定义梯度计算的依赖。通过方差减少和GPU计算优化，该方法实现了更高的样本效率，并利用文档曝光分布进行梯度估计，从而与自动微分无缝集成。实验表明，该方法在收敛速度和排序性能上优于现有方法，同时计算时间没有增加。此外，该方法在多个训练周期中表现出更好的稳定性，显著提升了RL在LTR中的有效性、效率和实用性。

item.title: 基于物理参数的图像到视频生成模型 PhyParam 的研究
item.content:encoded:
提出 PhyParam 模型提升视频生成的物理一致性与可控性。



摘要： 本文探讨了图像到视频生成中的物理动力学控制问题，指出当前模型在物理因果关系和实例约束方面存在不足。为此，作者提出了 PhyParam-Dataset 数据集和 PhyParam 模型，通过引入细粒度物理参数和物理注意力机制，提升了视频生成的物理合理性与动态控制能力。同时，构建了 PhyParam-Bench 基准评估体系，用于衡量视频生成的物理一致性。实验表明，PhyParam 在保持高视觉保真度的同时显著提高了物理一致性，为未来的物理驱动视频生成提供了新方向。

item.title: ZeroSplat：突破单目标限制的3D高斯溅射分割框架
item.content:encoded:
提出ZeroSplat，实现多目标3D高斯溅射分割，提升效率与准确性。



摘要： 本文提出ZeroSplat，一种无需训练和特征存储的3D高斯溅射分割框架，解决了现有方法在处理多目标查询时的性能瓶颈。通过将2D视觉-语言模型先验引入3D空间，ZeroSplat实现了点级理解，提升了分割精度与效率。研究构建了两个新基准GR-LERF和GR-ScanNet，用于评估该任务。实验表明，ZeroSplat在多种场景下均优于现有方法。

item.title: 神经网络近似与几何多样性分析

研究神经网络的函数等价性与几何多样性，提出模型选择准则。



摘要： 本文探讨了单层和多层感知器在噪声与无噪声条件下的函数近似能力，分析了神经网络表示的函数等价性和几何多样性。通过研究损失函数的Hessian矩阵特征值谱和有效秩，揭示了功能不可区分但几何结构多样的网络特性，并提出了基于简洁性、可估计性和推断效率的模型选择标准。

item.title: 基于李群的可解释生成模型研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种基于李群的条件生成器，实现可解释的生成任务。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于李群的条件生成模型，通过在潜在空间中解耦生成因素，提升生成模型的可解释性。该方法利用对抗损失将新潜在空间与流空间对齐，无需扩展流空间维度即可实现可控生成。实验表明，该模型在图像和分子生成任务中表现优异，尤其在MNIST、dSprites数据集上超越StyleGAN，在QM9、ZINC和MOSES数据集上也取得良好效果。

item.title: 异步强化学习中的稳定性改进方法研究
item.content:encoded:
异步强化学习中引入SAT方法提升稳定性与性能。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为Staleness-Adaptive Trust Region (SAT) 的新方法，用于改善异步强化学习中的稳定性问题。通过使用采样对数比作为过时程度的代理，SAT能够识别批次中的高不匹配尾部，并仅对PPO区间的一端进行收缩，从而在保持基础行为的同时，对新拦截的外部区域进行更保守的更新。实验表明，在Qwen3-30B-A3B-Base框架下，SAT-GSPO在不同延迟情况下均表现出色，有效提升了异步RL的性能和稳定性。

item.title: 基于视觉的航向预测决策框架研究
item.content:encoded:
本文研究视觉航向预测的可信度决策方法。



摘要： 本文探讨了在UAV-UGV协作中，如何根据视觉预测的置信度决定是否直接发布控制指令。提出了一种轻量级的置信度门控框架，利用边界框面积和航向变化作为可靠性代理指标。在低置信度情况下，比较了冻结保持策略与保守更新的备用方案。实验表明，置信度门控在执行率、准确性和平滑性之间存在权衡，且备用方案能显著提升命令行为表现。

item.title: 轻量级多模态融合框架CURE提升医疗AI性能
item.content:encoded:
CURE框架提升医疗多模态融合效率与性能。



摘要： 本文提出一种轻量且可扩展的多模态融合学习框架CURE，旨在解决现有方法在医疗领域中跨模态交互捕捉不足、计算成本高及适应性差的问题。CURE通过HyFuse层逐步融合多种医学数据模态，结合高效残差卷积和混合空间注意力机制，有效提升特征提取与跨模态关系保持能力。实验表明，CURE在16个公开数据集上优于现有方法，性能提升最高达3.97%，计算成本降低87.8%。

item.title: 基于物理模型的数据驱动方法在车辆能耗预测中的应用
item.content:encoded:
物理模型提升车辆能耗预测的可靠性与准确性。



摘要： 本文将第一性原理物理模型融入数据驱动方法中，构建考虑车辆运行中不同能量损耗源的模型。研究发现，基于该物理模型的贝叶斯线性回归能提高预期能耗的可靠性。进一步研究表明，结合相同物理模型的神经网络和梯度提升回归树等复杂机器学习模型可显著提升能耗预测精度。此外，还开发了用于估计预测标准差的不确定性框架，所有模型均能合理估计不确定性。

item.title: GLID：基于几何的无监督人脸伪造检测方法
item.content:encoded:
GLID通过几何特征检测人脸伪造，无需训练数据。



摘要： 本文提出GLID，一种基于几何特征的人脸伪造检测方法，无需依赖训练数据。它利用单张图像的块标记在冻结视觉Transformer中的局部内在维度（LID）作为12维信号，输入经过微调的检测器。在跨生成器基准测试中，GLID达到0.805平均AUC，优于现有方法。实验表明，伪造人脸会在特定深度弯曲标记流形，且几何信号在数据不足时尤为重要。该方法显著提升了检测效果并减少了跨种子的准确性差异。

item.title: RAGAL：在数据本地化约束下的技术支援助手开发
item.content:encoded:
RAGAL在无数据外泄限制下提升技术支持效率。



摘要： 本文介绍了RAGAL，一个为罗马尼亚农村投资融资局（AFIR）技术支援团队设计的检索增强型助手。该系统在严格的数据本地化限制下开发，包括零数据外泄、只读指令和仅使用一台8GB笔记本电脑。研究基于约25,000个罗马尼亚语文档块，发现检索工程和检索器微调比增大生成器更有效。通过混合密集-稀疏检索和意图路由，系统性能显著提升。此外，研究还发现了单域微调可能降低未处理文档的检索效果，并提出用本地生成查询进行修复。文中还提到隐私信息遮蔽和结构化“锚点蒸馏”方法对生成质量的提升，以及在CPU上运行的大规模模型作为替代判断工具。最后，作者开源了可复现的微调方案。

item.title: OmniReasoner：一种用于长时音频视频推理的工具调用后训练框架
item.content:encoded:
OmniReasoner提升长时音视频推理准确性与时间定位。



摘要： 文章提出 OmniReasoner，这是一种用于长时音频视频推理的工具调用后训练框架。该框架通过监督微调和强化学习，使模型学会在回答前决定是否以及何时调用放大工具。OmniReasoner首先构建全流的低成本全局预览，然后在需要时调用放大工具进行高保真视听检查。为解决不同采样粒度下的时间锚定问题，引入 TimeAnchor 以确保一致性。此外，通过 Temporal Augmented Data Engine 合成训练轨迹，无需昂贵的人工标注即可训练工具使用行为。实验表明，OmniReasoner提升了答案准确性和时间定位能力。

item.title: 印度法律问答系统的AI研究与评估
item.content:encoded:
AI提升印度法律问答准确性与可靠性。



摘要： 本研究开发了针对印度法律语境的先进人工智能法律问答系统（AILQA），利用多种嵌入和生成模型，包括大型语言模型，以应对印度法律文本的复杂性和多样性。通过严格的评估，结合词法和语义指标及法律专家反馈，验证了系统的有效性。研究发现，检索增强生成（RAG）方法在复杂法律领域中显著提升了回答质量，并在全印度律师考试中进行了测试，提供了实用基准。部分AI生成的回答在特定数据集和评分标准下优于参考答案，但不意味着AI普遍优于专业法律人士。研究还讨论了上下文精准性、模型幻觉等挑战，并提出未来研究方向，旨在提升法律决策支持系统的可访问性和有效性。

item.title: 基于GUIDED的交通分配问题高效求解方法
item.content:encoded:
提出GUIDED框架提升交通模型泛化能力与效率。



摘要： 本文针对交通分配问题中图神经网络泛化能力不足的问题，提出一种名为Geometrically Unconstrained Inductive Demand EmbeDding (GUIDED) 的网络无关初始化层。该方法通过在虚拟链接上注入出行需求作为标量属性，实现输入空间的标准化，从而提升模型在不同城市网络间的迁移能力。实验表明，结合GUIDED的HetGAT模型在单网络任务中保持了最先进的预测精度，并在分布外需求模式下表现出更强的鲁棒性，同时减少了约50%的训练时间。该方法不仅适用于车辆交通，还可推广至货运物流和多模式网络优化等领域。

item.title: AutoJourn：多视角新闻生成与偏见感知评估系统
item.content:encoded:
AutoJourn系统实现多视角新闻生成与偏见检测。



摘要： AutoJourn是一个用于多视角新闻生成和偏见感知评估的演示系统，利用大语言模型解决负责任自动化新闻的三大核心挑战：从非结构化社交媒体讨论中提取多样观点、生成保留观点多样性的摘要，以及检测或减轻AI生成新闻中的偏见。系统整合了先进的提示工程和可选的检索增强技术，提供多视角摘要模块和偏见分析工具，支持句子级偏见检测、分类及自动中和。用户可通过界面查看视角聚类、比较立场特定摘要、生成新闻文章并应用偏见感知重写。各组件通过内在指标评估，显示在内容保真度下优于基线模型的性能。论文附带一个公开演示，促进社会负责任的自动化新闻研究。

item.title: 基于Hadamard流形的1-Lipschitz神经网络设计与应用
item.content:encoded:
本文提出一种在Hadamard流形上的1-Lipschitz神经网络架构。



摘要： 本文研究了在Hadamard流形上构建和分析1-Lipschitz神经网络的方法。所提出的网络层具有梯度下降类型、1-Lipschitz性质以及准α-非扩张性。该架构的核心构建块是Busemann函数，并利用Busemann梯度流设计保持几何结构的1-Lipschitz层。文章给出了双曲空间和对称正定矩阵流形的具体构造和示例，并在Poincaré圆盘上的鲁棒分类和带掩码的Wishart协方差重构任务中进行了实验验证。结果表明，所提网络在面对双曲扰动时表现稳健，并在SPD流形上的去噪任务中优于传统方法。

item.title: Mage-Flow：高效文本到图像生成与编辑的紧凑模型栈
item.content:encoded:
Mage-Flow实现高效高分辨率图像生成与编辑。



摘要： 文章介绍了Mage-Flow，一个4B规模的生成模型栈，用于高效的文本到图像生成和基于指令的图像编辑。该模型由两个协同设计的组件组成：Mage-VAE，一种轻量级的高保真潜在编码器，以及基于修正流匹配训练的原生分辨率多模态扩散Transformer。Mage-VAE通过一步扩散式编码和解码，结合锚定潜在正则化，在保持高质量重建的同时显著降低分词成本。结合原生分辨率打包和系统级CUDA内核融合，提升了端到端训练吞吐量。基于此，开发了适用于生成和编辑的Base、RL对齐和Turbo变体。Turbo模型在低延迟推理中表现出色，适合交互式应用。

item.title: 基于多模态反馈的视觉-语言模型去学习方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出SMU框架提升VLM去学习效果与迁移性。



摘要： 本文探讨了视觉-语言模型（VLM）的去学习问题，指出仅依赖文本反馈难以实现可靠的去学习。作者提出Stochastic Meta-Unlearning（SMU）框架，通过多模态反馈优化语言骨干的去学习过程。该方法在内层使用文本数据进行去学习，在外层结合冻结的视觉模块评估遗忘与保留效果，从而提升去学习的可靠性与迁移性。实验表明，SMU在多个数据集和基线上均取得最佳性能，显著提升了遗忘与保留的平衡性。

item.title: 基于热-机械融合的锂离子电池热失控早期预警框架
item.content:encoded:
提出一种融合热-机械信号的锂离子电池热失控预警方法。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于物理引导的热-机械融合框架，用于锂离子电池在受控机械滥用条件下的热失控早期预警。该框架整合温度、电压、力、形变和荷电状态等多源数据，通过轻量卷积分类器识别安全、预警或危险状态，并结合因果时间卷积网络进行时序建模与预测。实验验证表明，该方法在30次不同荷电状态和加载协议的机械滥用测试中表现出色，F1分数达0.89，高温预测误差为12.3°C，平均预警提前时间为15.6秒，检测成功率高达0.92。去除力信号后预警时间显著下降，证明机械前兆的重要性。该方法为提升电池安全性提供了新思路。

item.title: 基于执行反馈的代码代理预算控制方法
item.content:encoded:
文章提出一种代码代理的预算控制策略，提升执行效率。



摘要： 本文研究了在可执行环境中运行的代码代理，通过利用执行失败后的反馈信息，优化计算资源的分配。传统系统将失败视为级联决策，而本文提出在失败后仍可尝试低成本模型恢复，从而实现更高效的预算管理。作者训练了一个监督路由器，并引入Conformal Risk Control（CRC）层以适应不同预算需求。实验表明，该方法在多个代码基准测试中优于固定策略和二进制级联基线，其中一个配置在保持高成功率的同时，仅使用35%的平均恢复成本。相关代码已开源。

item.title: 基于视觉语言代理的实时到仿真转换框架
item.content:encoded:
提出Agentic Real2Sim框架实现机器人与物体交互的自动化仿真转换。



摘要： 文章介绍了一种名为Agentic Real2Sim的框架，用于将现实世界中机器人与物体的交互记录转化为可运行的物理仿真环境。该框架通过视觉语言代理实现场景几何、物体状态和物理参数的自动恢复，无需依赖人工调整。其在刚体操作、柔性物体交互和人形运动等多个领域进行了评估，展示了跨领域的通用性。该方法成本较低且转换成功率高，为后续机器人策略学习与评估提供了基础。

item.title: The Price of Hidden Curvature: An $\widetildeΩ (d^{5/4} \sqrt{T})$ Lower Bound for Bandit Convex Optimization
item.content:encoded: We establish a $\widetildeΩ(d^{5/4}\sqrt T)$ lower bound on the minimax expected regret of stochastic bandit convex optimization of $1$-Lipschitz functions on the Euclidean ball. This presents the first nontrivial regret lower bound that grows faster than $d\sqrt{T}$ for this problem, establishing that stochastic bandit convex optimization is fundamentally harder than linear bandits. The hard class of convex functions we construct takes the following form in dimension $2d$: for an action $a = (a^1,a^2) \in \mathbb{B}^{2d}_2$, each function is the scaled soft maximum of a "tube", $r^{-1} \| W^\star a^1 - \frac{r}{8\varepsilon} a^2 \|_2$ (hyperparameterized by $\varepsilon,r$), and a squared distance function, $\frac12 \| a^1 - u^\star \|_2^2 - \frac12 \| u^\star \|_2^2$. Here, $W^\star \in \mathbb{R}^{d \times d}$ is an unknown linear transformation, and $u^\star \in \mathbb{R}^{d}$ is an unknown vector which must be learned to minimize the function. Observations are informative about $u^\star$ only when the learner's action lies near the tube determined by $W^\star$, satisfying $a^2 \approx \frac{8\varepsilon}{r} W^\star a^1$: thus the learner must either find this tube without knowing $W^\star$, or spend observations learning useful directions of $W^\star$. Formally, our regret analysis exploits this tradeoff by bounding the posterior spread of Fisher information matrices obtained under an adaptive sequence of actions. Together, these ingredients give a sample complexity lower bound of $\widetildeΩ(d^{5/2}/\varepsilon^2)$ to find an $\varepsilon$-optimal action, which translates to an $\widetildeΩ (d^{5/4} \sqrt{T})$ regret lower bound. We also extend this lower bound to the unconstrained setting where the action space is $\mathbb{R}^d$.

item.title: Fluid-SDF：一种高效的显式几何表示框架
item.content:encoded:
Fluid-SDF通过显式几何基元实现高效、可编辑的3D形状建模。



摘要： Fluid-SDF是一种基于显式几何基元的压缩、可微分的构造实体几何（CSG）框架，它使用平滑最小函数融合几何元素，以少于100个参数重建复杂非凸拓扑结构，性能优于传统神经网络。该方法对高频数据噪声具有更强的鲁棒性，并允许用户直接编辑形状特征而无需重新训练，适用于移动AI和资源受限环境。

item.title: 基于编辑条件的3D场景检索方法CR-Refiner
item.content:encoded:
提出CR-Refiner提升3D场景检索精度。



摘要： 本文提出CR-Refiner，一种无需训练的重排序器，用于改进基于自然语言编辑条件的3D场景检索。该方法通过冻结的大型语言模型解析编辑指令，并利用非平衡最优传输问题对候选场景进行评分。此外，引入轴条件结构先验和语言模型验证模块，提升几何与空间编辑的检索效果。为评估该方法，作者还发布了3D-CER数据集，包含4,963个编辑条件查询，覆盖23,381个室内场景，涵盖多种编辑维度。

item.title: 基于多时间尺度强化学习的无人机城市感知系统
item.content:encoded:
提出TSRL框架提升无人机城市感知效率。



摘要： 本文研究了利用无人机进行城市环境监测的问题，指出现有方法忽视了风等环境因素对无人机性能的影响。针对大规模无人机群在动态环境中面临的可扩展性和多时间尺度决策异质性问题，作者提出了SensUAV和两阶段强化学习框架TSRL。该框架分为宏观任务调度层和微观速度控制层，分别解决任务分配和风扰适应问题。实验表明，TSRL在杭州和上海的数据集上分别提升了20.1%和46.6%的系统效益。
