item.title: 视觉语言模型中链式思维提示的推理边界与性能分析
item.content:encoded:
CoT通过扩展语言计算而非延长图像访问提升性能。



摘要： 本文研究了视觉语言模型（VLMs）中链式思维（CoT）提示在推理过程中的作用。通过引入视觉访问扫描方法，作者定义了视觉访问边界（VAB），发现CoT主要依赖于已提取的视觉信息进行语言计算，而非持续访问图像标记。实验表明，即使生成更长的推理路径，CoT的VAB层与直接回答的边界差异不大。此外，CoT的效果受限于感知读出能力，当视觉属性可被可靠读取时表现良好，否则效果有限。研究揭示了模型性能瓶颈在于读出而非计数。

item.title: 基于生成模型的法律条文检索框架GCSR
item.content:encoded:
提出GCSR框架提升法律条文检索效果。



摘要： 本文提出了一种基于生成模型的法律条文检索框架GCSR，将法律条文检索问题转化为序列生成任务，并将法律知识内化到生成模型中。通过引入多粒度结构化文档ID和多任务训练策略，实验表明GCSR在多个基准测试中均优于现有方法，展示了生成式检索在法律信息检索中的有效性及潜力。

item.title: 基于Wiener-Hopf线性预测的可解释音频深度伪造检测框架
item.content:encoded:
提出一种轻量级、可解释的音频深度伪造检测方法。



摘要： 本文提出了一种基于Wiener-Hopf线性预测的可解释音频深度伪造检测框架，结合轻量级二维卷积神经网络，实现了高检测性能与低计算复杂度的平衡。实验结果表明，该方法在基准数据集上表现优异，同时通过Grad-CAM分析揭示了模型关注低阶预测系数和静音及过渡区域，表明其能有效捕捉合成语音中的混响特征和统计不一致性。此外，该方法在常见后处理干扰下仍表现出良好的鲁棒性。

item.title: LLM裁判在引用质量评估中的校准研究
item.content:encoded:
研究LLM裁判在引用质量评估中的能力与偏差，发现经济型模型表现优异。



摘要： 本文研究了在深度研究系统中，使用大语言模型（LLM）作为引用质量评估的裁判，以作为强化学习中的奖励模型。文章通过对抗性长文本基准测试，对8个不同来源的LLM裁判进行评估，涉及1248次评分决策，其中378个为人类裁定的困难案例。结果显示，经济型模型在源相关性和事实支持两个维度上均表现出色，GPT-5-mini在源相关性任务中达到0.908的F1分数。尽管在事实支持方面各模型差异不显著，但不同模型在通过率、误报率和漏报率上存在明显差异。研究指出，仅依赖F1分数可能掩盖方向性偏差，而这种偏差正是强化学习循环中需要关注的关键问题。因此，校准裁判是使用引用标准作为奖励信号的前提，且并不需要最昂贵的模型。

item.title: ProofCouncil：一种用于解决开放数学问题的数学代理系统
item.content:encoded:
ProofCouncil在数学问题求解中表现出色，提交6题获认可。



摘要： 本文介绍了ProofCouncil，一个基于作者-评论家架构的数学代理系统，旨在解决实际数学问题。它作为FirstProof挑战的参赛作品，成功解决了其中6个问题，获得评委认可。此外，在30个开放数学问题的测试中，21个解决方案获得反馈，其中5个被判定完全正确，2个具有潜力，8个展示了有用的进展。文章还描述了ProofCouncil的开发过程及所使用的代理构建库，并将其开源以供社区使用。

item.title: 基于MRI的微生物密度分层方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出CHM-Net模型，通过MRI非侵入性预测微生物密度。



摘要： 本文研究了基于MRI的微生物密度分层（MRI-MDS）任务，并引入了中心热图驱动的宏微观建模网络（CHM-Net）。该模型通过中心热图引导的小病灶响应定位建立影像表型与微生物状态之间的联系，并从局部热图响应构建患者级宏观-微观证据以预测微生物密度。在新型GBNPC 2026数据集上的实验表明，CHM-Net优于现有基线模型，准确率提升12.06%。此外，在两个3D医学图像数据集上的辅助验证进一步证明了其在体积医学图像分类场景中的鲁棒性。

item.title: 基于时间感知深度学习的双源无轨电车能耗管理研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种时间感知框架用于无轨电车能耗预测与因果分析。



摘要： 本文提出了一种时间感知的表格深度学习框架，用于无轨电车在不同供电模式下的能耗管理。该框架结合周期性时间编码和贝叶斯优化，有效处理了路线属性、高频轨迹和天气等复杂输入。通过因果分析，识别出再生制动率和平均速度是主要节能因素，而滑行距离则是能耗增加的主要原因。实验结果表明，该方法在苏黎世无轨电车数据集上表现优异，优于多种基准模型，为车辆技术优化和网络规划提供了依据。

item.title: CoT提示在专利权利要求撰写中的有效性研究
item.content:encoded:
CoT提示能提升专利权利要求质量，隐式推理优于显式推理。



摘要： 本文探讨了Chain-of-Thought（CoT）提示在专利权利要求撰写中的应用效果。尽管CoT被广泛用于提升大语言模型的推理能力，但其在高度结构化任务中可能效果有限甚至产生负面影响。研究提出了一种针对专利权利要求撰写的特定CoT方法，并通过自动评估和专家评审验证其有效性。结果表明，增强推理的提示可以提升权利要求质量。此外，研究发现隐式CoT（推理过程不显式表达）比显式CoT表现更优，因为显式推理可能导致关键细节抽象、生成模式破坏和错误传播等问题。该研究为法律任务中的CoT应用提供了新见解。

item.title: cayleyR：通过Cayley图循环交集解决排列谜题的R包
item.content:encoded:
cayleyR利用Cayley图循环交集解决TopSpin排列谜题。



摘要： cayleyR是一个R包，用于通过检测Cayley图中的循环交集来解决排列谜题。其核心算法采用双向迭代搜索，从初始和目标排列状态出发，生成随机操作序列以在对称群Sn的Cayley图中形成循环，循环交集可提供连接路径。当无法直接找到交集时，通过距离引导的桥接选择缩小差距，并重复该过程。该包专注于TopSpin(n,k)谜题，其状态空间是通过循环移位和前缀反转生成的Sn的Cayley图。文章描述了数学框架、算法及其实现，结合了C++哈希索引状态存储与可选Vulkan GPU加速，软件已发布在CRAN上。

item.title: CRC-HGD数据集：用于结直肠腺癌分级的组织病理学图像数据
item.content:encoded:
介绍了一个用于结直肠癌分级的多尺度组织病理学图像数据集。



摘要： 本文介绍了CRC-HGD数据集，包含1,914张来自214名结直肠腺癌患者的H&E染色组织切片图像，涵盖三个分化等级（I、II、III）。图像在四个不同放大倍数（4x、10x、20x、40x）下采集，可用于人工智能辅助癌症分类与诊断。数据集通过Mendeley Data和Databiox网站公开，支持全面的计算分析，有助于提高结直肠癌的诊断准确性。

item.title: 人工智能在软件工程课程优化中的应用
item.content:encoded:
AI提升课程分析效率，减少毕业延迟。



摘要： 人工智能技术的应用使得大规模数据的自动分析成为可能，显著提高了以往耗时且劳动密集型任务的效率。本文利用AI技术分析和修订软件工程本科课程结构，解决因课程顺序问题导致学生无法在四年内毕业的问题。传统的人工分析和修订过程耗时且低效，难以适应学生需求的变化。本文通过使用大型语言模型分析课程模式并提出修订建议，有效缩短了课程调整时间，减少了瓶颈和毕业延迟。

item.title: KnowAct-GUIClaw：提升复杂任务自动化效率的跨平台框架
item.content:encoded:
KnowAct-GUIClaw提升任务自动化效率与跨平台适应性。



摘要： 本文针对现有Agent框架OpenClaw在跨平台GUI交互和自进化能力方面的不足，提出KnowAct-GUIClaw框架。该框架基于‘知深行远’理念，通过长期任务分解、可插拔GUI子代理和经验记忆系统，实现跨平台迁移和性能持续优化。实验表明，KnowAct-GUIClaw在多个操作系统上表现优异，尤其在长周期任务中超越了其他主流框架和闭源模型，显著提升了任务执行的准确性和效率。

item.title: 基于情感感知的360°音乐可视化生成方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种情感驱动的360°音乐视频生成方法，提升音乐体验的沉浸感。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为Bring Music The Horizon的情感感知音乐可视化系统，通过分析歌曲的情绪轨迹并生成相应的视觉引导，最终合成360°沉浸式音乐视频。该方法首先预测歌曲每四小节的情绪值（愉悦度与唤醒度），再通过EmotiCrafter生成情绪导向的视觉提示，并利用SEGA框架进行关键帧的语义控制。最后通过图像到视频模型生成连续的360°视频，以反映音乐的情感变化和结构。实验展示了不同风格歌曲的效果，并与现有基准模型进行了对比。

item.title: 提升视觉语言模型多图分析推理能力的框架与方法
item.content:encoded:
提出SD-MAR框架提升VLM在多图分析推理任务中的表现。



摘要： 本文针对视觉语言模型（VLMs）在多图像比较、变化检测和多步骤视觉推理等分析性任务中的不足，提出SD-MAR框架用于训练和评估VLMs。该框架通过受控扰动生成配对视觉场景，并构建涵盖语义变化归因和定量比较的推理任务。研究采用GRPO-lite与BDA强化学习方法进行微调，显著提升了Qwen2.5-VL-7B和InternVL3-8B在SD-MAR基准上的准确率，同时保持或提升了跨域泛化能力。LLM作为评判者进一步验证了逻辑连贯性和解释质量的提升。

item.title: Statistical Non-linear Reconstruction Loss for Image Anomaly Detection
item.content:encoded: Reconstruction-based methods are a cornerstone of unsupervised image anomaly detection, but they remain vulnerable to \emph{outlier leakage}, where standard mean squared error (MSE) loss drives the model to faithfully reconstruct anomalous patterns. We propose a Non-linear Reconstruction Loss that applies a sigmoid-based squashing function to suppress high-magnitude features, preventing outliers from dominating optimization while preserving sensitivity to normal patterns. In addition, we introduce a statistical calibration scheme that selects the scaling factor $k$ from the confidence interval (CI) of the normal feature distribution, enabling data-driven control of the suppression strength. Our approach achieves competitive or superior anomaly detection performance compared to state-of-the-art methods, reaching 99.0\% Image-AUROC and 97.3\% Pixel-AUROC on MVTec-AD, and 95.3\% Image-AUROC and 99.0\% Pixel-AUROC on VisA. These results indicate that non-linear gradient suppression is an effective mechanism for mitigating outlier leakage and improving anomaly localization in unified industrial inspection settings. The implementation is available at https://github.com/mintii13/Statistical-Non-linear-Reconstruction-Loss.git.

item.title: LatentFlow: A General Framework for Conditioning Stochastic Processes
item.content:encoded: Stochastic-process models are, as a rule, far easier to simulate than to condition. Non-linear observations, non-Gaussian likelihoods, black-box information, and global constraints all induce intractable conditional laws, requiring bespoke, model-specific constructions. We introduce LatentFlow, a single framework for conditioning stochastic processes, with no learned neural approximations and no training. Our starting point is to write the stochastic process as the deterministic image of a tractable latent innovation, $f_0 = T_{\vartheta}(ξ_0)$, with $ξ_0$ sampled from a simple reference distribution. This reduces process-level conditioning to latent-space inference: pull the likelihood back through $T_{\vartheta}$, sample the resulting latent law with a tractable guided probability flow, and push the samples forward. This construction is provably exact at the level of the target law; in practice, approximation enters only through finite terminal noising, Monte Carlo guidance, and time discretisation of the continuous-time dynamics, each of which is explicit and systematically reducible. As LatentFlow is training-free, conditioning reduces to solving a single reverse-time SDE. This enables conditional sampling in seconds on a single desktop CPU across model classes that have never shared a scalable method: classical spatial priors, nonlinear stochastic dynamics, mechanistic models from the physical and life sciences, stochastic PDEs, heavy-tails and extremes, point and discrete-state processes, and neural or simulator-defined processes.

item.title: A Novel Graph Fraud Detector via Grouped Attribute Completion and Confidence-Aware Contrastive Learning
item.content:encoded: Graph fraud detection plays a pivotal role in safeguarding the security and integrity of modern digital ecosystems. Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) are commonly adopted for graph fraud detection. However, the practical performance of existing GNN-based detectors is severely hindered by incomplete node attributes and extreme class imbalance within graphs. To mitigate these limitations, this paper proposes a novel framework for Graph Fraud Detection with Grouped attribute completion and Confidence-aware Contrastive learning, named GFD-GC. Specifically, it first imitates heterogeneous neighborhood structures to implement group-wise aggregation, which obtains informative complete node features by capturing fine-grained graph contextual patterns. Further, it introduces a confidence-aware supervised contrastive learning strategy to augment scarce labeled fraud nodes with high confidence pseudo-fraud nodes, which enhances the compactness of fraud representations and their separability from non-fraud nodes. Extensive experiments demonstrate the superiority of the proposed GFD-GC over state-of-the-art baselines on the graph fraud detection task, thereby providing an effective solution for real-world fraud scenarios.

item.title: Learning Subgroup Relations Using Siamese Graph Neural Networks
item.content:encoded: Determining whether one finite group is isomorphic to a subgroup of another is a fundamental problem in computational group theory. In this work, we propose a Siamese Graph Neural Network (Siamese GNN) for subgroup prediction using Cayley graph representations of finite groups. Each input group is represented by its undirected Cayley graph and encoded by one branch of a Siamese GNN to produce a graph embedding. The resulting graph embeddings are combined with algebraic features derived directly from the input groups to construct a joint feature vector, which is processed by a fully connected classifier to predict subgroup relations between finite groups. By integrating graph-based structural representations with algebraic features, the proposed framework provides a unified approach for learning subgroup relations from finite groups. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed architecture, achieving a test accuracy of 95.9% (47/49) on an independent test set and illustrating the potential of geometric deep learning for subgroup prediction.

item.title: Overview of Cross-Component In-loop Filters in Video Coding Standards
item.content:encoded: In-loop filters have been comprehensively explored during the development of video coding standards due to their remarkable noise-reduction capability. In the early stage of video coding, in-loop filters, such as Deblocking Filter, Sample Adaptive Offset, and Adaptive Loop Filter, were performed separately for each component. Recently, cross-component filters were studied to improve the chroma fidelity by exploiting correlations between the luma and chroma channels. This paper summarizes the cross-component filters used in the state-of-the-art video coding standard. Specifically, it includes the Cross-Component Adaptive Loop Filter and Cross-Component Sample Adaptive Offset. Cross-component filters aim to reduce compression artifacts based on the correlation between different components and provide more accurate pixel reconstruction values. In this paper, we introduce the origin, development, and status of cross-component filters in the current video coding standards. Finally, we had some discussions on the further evolutions of cross-component filters.

item.title: Do We Really Need Multimodal Emotion Language Models Larger Than 1B Parameters?
item.content:encoded: Recent advances in multimodal large language models (MLLMs) have significantly improved the performance of multimodal emotion recognition (MER) and enabled interpretable description generation by jointly modeling video, audio, and language, etc. However, these performance improvements are often accompanied by an increase in model parameter size (e.g, at least 7B), which simultaneously incurs high computational costs and reduces inference efficiency, thereby hindering real-time deployment on resource-constrained platforms such as robots and mobile devices. This raises a fundamental question: do we really need the multimodal MER model larger than 1B parameters for high-quality MER? In this paper, we challenge the assumption that larger models are inherently necessary and proposes a lightweight MER framework (called Light-MER), which achieves better and faster multimodal sentiment understanding and recognition through knowledge distillation. It can transfer knowledge from a strong, large-scale teacher model to a lightweight sub-billion-parameter student model, aiming to preserve rich multimodal emotion reasoning and recognition while substantially improving deployment efficiency. Specifically, we introduce two new optimization strategies to enhance knowledge transfer: (1) a new optimal transport loss that combines Sliced Wasserstein Distance with hidden-state alignment, and (2) a new multi-reward optimization strategy based on GRPO that balances MER performance and efficiency, aimed at further enhancing the learning capabilities of student models. Extensive experiments on nine benchmark datasets demonstrate that Light-MER achieves state-of-the-art performance while significantly improving inference efficiency. This highlights the strong potential of small multimodal emotion language models for future research. Code is available at https://github.com/GAIR-Lab/Light-MER.

item.title: GRASP: GRanularity-Aware Search Policy for Agentic RAG
item.content:encoded: Agentic retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) extends static RAG by allowing language models to iteratively reason, generate search queries, retrieve evidence, and predict answers. However, it remains challenging for models to decide when to retrieve, whether to use lexical matching or semantic similarity, and how to control context granularity to prevent irrelevant tokens from interfering with agent reasoning. In this paper, we introduce GRASP, a reinforcement learning (RL) framework for training agents to adaptively coordinate complementary retrieval tools during multi-step reasoning. GRASP provides the agent with semantic search, keyword search, and paragraph-reading actions, enabling it to retrieve sentence-level evidence and expand further context only when needed. We train the policy with a reward that jointly accounts for answer accuracy, grounded reading, complementary search, and turn efficiency. Experiments on multi-hop reasoning benchmarks show that GRASP improves both retrieval recall and downstream question answering performance compared with single-step retrieval, prompting-based agentic RAG, and RL-based retrieval baselines. Qualitative and ablation analyses show that the learned policy develops interpretable skimming and scanning behavior: it uses semantic search for broad exploration, paragraph reading for local verification, and keyword search for entity-specific evidence. These results suggest that learning to coordinate retrieval signals and context granularity is critical for agent's correct reasoning.

item.title: Sticky Jump Diffusions: A Unifying View of Masked, Continuous, and Hybrid Diffusion
item.content:encoded: We introduce Sticky Jump Diffusions (SJDs), continuous-time Markov processes on $\mathbb R^d$ whose discrete anchors are token embeddings. In forward time, anchors release their mass at a hazard rate and the released mass diffuses in the continuous ambient space; time reversal couples a score-driven SDE with a sticky jump kernel whose rate and destination are fixed by flux balance with the forward law. We estimate the score and the per-anchor reverse hazards from a single denoising classifier via Denoising Hazard Matching, the hazard analogue of denoising score matching, with simulation-free cross-entropy training. SJD recovers masked diffusion, continuous diffusion, and hybrid diffusion as limits. Its reversal explains features that each family treats as given: the mask of masked diffusion carries no evidence about the source token because the unsticking kernel of every anchor collapses to the same absorbing point; the terminal projection of continuous diffusion is required due to the absence of atoms in its forward marginal, without which flux balance yields no reverse jumps; and the update rules of hybrid diffusion (commit rate, destination, and drift) all follow from flux balance rather than from separate design. Beyond these limits, the unsticking kernel becomes a design space: a cross-position blending corrupts each position toward a blend of its neighbors' clean values or embeddings, turning dependency structure such as spatial locality or a constraint graph into an inductive bias of the corruption itself, and improves over the identity-kernel hybrid on CIFAR-10, Text8, and Sudoku.

item.title: Cross-Layer Misalignment Detection in Agent Skills: A Progressive Loading-Aware Contrastive Learning Approach
item.content:encoded: Large language model (LLM) agents are increasingly extended through Agent Skills, reusable artifacts that package natural-language metadata, procedural instructions, and execution-time resources for runtime use. As open-source skill marketplaces expand, users and agents increasingly rely on brief metadata to select third-party skills, making it difficult to detect inconsistencies between a skill's description and its true behavior, a problem we call cross-layer misalignment. To address this issue, we propose Progressive Loading-Aware Hierarchical Contrastive Learning (PL-HCL), an LLM-based framework that detects misalignment by modeling the layered structure of Agent Skills and learning cross-layer consistency. Using a normalized corpus of over 264,000 open-source skills and a human-verified challenge set, PL-HCL improves Macro-F1 from approximately 0.45 for unadapted baselines to 0.87-0.89 across evaluated LLM backbones. This approach offers an effective screening tool for users and operators, as well as design principles for detecting inconsistencies in layered digital artifacts.

item.title: Benchmarking Face Recognition without Real Faces
item.content:encoded: Synthetic face datasets have become effective enough to train face recognition models with accuracy rivaling that of models trained on real photographs. This progress sidesteps the ethical and legal burdens of collecting real biometric data, yet evaluation has not kept pace. Even studies that train entirely on synthetic images still rely on real-face benchmarks to measure performance, leaving the privacy problem only half solved. We ask whether synthetic datasets can replace real benchmarks for face recognition evaluation. We test 12 synthetic datasets against 7 established real benchmarks using 24 pre-trained models that span both convolutional and transformer architectures. Our evaluation covers biometric verification metrics, similarity score distributions, cross-model ranking consistency, and the underlying distributional properties of each dataset. Benchmarking fidelity varies widely across the synthetic candidates, but the two strongest, MorphFace and Vec2Face, reproduce the relative behavior of real benchmarks and reach agreement levels that fall within the natural disagreement already observed among the real benchmarks themselves. These results establish that well-constructed synthetic datasets can support reliable comparative evaluation for face recognition, moving the field closer to a fully synthetic and privacy-preserving pipeline for both training and benchmarking.

item.title: SingGuard-NSFA: Extensible Guardrails for Agentic AI via Generative Reasoning and Real-Time Classification
item.content:encoded: We present nsfaguard, a guardrail framework for securing agentic AI systems against operational threats, such as prompt injection, sensitive information extraction, malicious code requests, dangerous tool misuse, and resource exhaustion. We first introduce the NSFA taxonomy, which organizes 185 risk variants into a CIA-triad-grounded hierarchy and is cross-validated against three well-established OWASP guidelines. Based on this taxonomy, we construct a benchmark suite spanning 133 languages, comprising over 93K purpose-built samples targeting both user queries and agent responses, along with 3,435 cross-source samples adapted from five public agent-security datasets. To detect these operational threats in practice, we develop a dual-mode approach combining SFT-based generative reasoning for interpretable offline auditing with discriminative classification heads on the frozen backbone, enabling real-time detection at approximately 50,ms. We release four models with 0.8B, 2B, 4B, and 9B parameters, all achieving $\geq$94% F1 on purpose-built benchmarks and surpassing the strongest competing guardrails by 6 to 12 absolute points. On cross-source evaluation, the 9B model attains 91.29% F1 with a more balanced precision--recall trade-off. Moreover, ablation experiments show that classification heads can equip a guardrail with risk detection capabilities beyond its original scope and achieve state-of-the-art performance. These results demonstrate the extensibility of the approach and its generality as a plug-in enhancement.

item.title: FairSelect: A Systematic Evaluation of Multi-Level and Intersectional Algorithmic Fairness
item.content:encoded: Algorithmic fairness methods are increasingly used to identify and mitigate bias in machine learning models, yet most approaches are evaluated in isolation and along single demographic axes. This limits practical guidance for selecting fairness strategies, where disparities may arise across intersectional subgroups and across multiple stages of the modeling lifecycle. This work presents FairSelect, a toolkit for systematically evaluating fairness mitigation strategies applied individually and in combination across preprocessing, inprocessing, and postprocessing stages. FairSelect supports multiple model architectures, intersectional subgroup evaluation, and comparison of fairness utility tradeoffs across baseline, single method, and multi level configurations. The framework was validated using synthetic clinical datasets designed to represent specific bias mechanisms and a real-world replication of two-year stroke risk prediction among patients with atrial fibrillation. Synthetic experiments showed that targeted fairness methods generally reduced intended subgroup disparities, while combined strategies produced larger average fairness improvements with modest utility tradeoffs. In the clinical prediction task, mitigation effects were highly variable, with some combinations improving both fairness and predictive performance while others were ineffective or counterproductive. These findings demonstrate that fairness interventions interact in nonadditive and context dependent ways. FairSelect provides a practical framework for systematically identifying fairness strategies that improve subgroup equity while preserving model performance in clinical machine learning.

item.title: Learning Engagement Assistant (LEA): Cross-Course Scalability and Classroom Evaluation of an Agentic AI Tutoring System
item.content:encoded: This paper is an extension of a paper presented at the ICAART 2026 conference, which introduced LEA (Learning Engagement Assistant), an adaptive AI tutoring agent combining course-specific Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with structured Knowledge Component (KC) models across integrated Chat, Tutor, and Quiz modes. That prior work validated LEA on a single STEM course (CMP511) exclusively through simulation, using synthetic learner agents. This paper extends that work by reporting the first classroom deployment of LEA with real students (n = 8, CMP511) and the first empirical test of its cross-course scalability, deploying the system across three courses spanning two academic levels and two disciplinary domains. The study reveals a divergence from simulation predictions across modes, showing that synthetic evaluation alone cannot anticipate all aspects of real deployment. A RAGAS-based cross-course scalability evaluation (660 questions) finds Answer Relevancy and Context Precision broadly stable across courses (0.88-0.94 and 0.88-0.90 respectively), while Faithfulness declines with curriculum distance from the system's original course (0.69 to 0.50), a preliminary finding that may reflect generation logic tuned to the system's original subject rather than a scalability limitation. These findings suggest that while the orchestration layer requires no modification, full course-agnosticism of all downstream components requires further investigation.

item.title: 小型卷积网络在Devanagari识别中达到最佳性能
item.content:encoded:
小型网络在Devanagari识别中达到99.73%准确率，优于大型模型。



摘要： 本文提出一个参数为1.11M的小型卷积网络，用于46类Devanagari字符识别，准确率达到99.73%，是目前报道的最高水平。该模型比之前最先进的模型小15.6倍，并且所有测试模型都达到了相同的11错误固有上限。即使不使用知识蒸馏，该模型也能与大型模型相媲美。此外，在CMATERdb数字数据集上进行零样本学习和微调也表现出色，且在噪声鲁棒性方面优于大型基线模型。

item.title: TCAM-Diff：一种高效3D医学图像生成模型
item.content:encoded:
TCAM-Diff通过三平面表示和扩散模型实现高效高分辨率3D医学图像生成。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为TCAM-Diff的新型3D医学图像生成模型，该模型通过解码器-only自编码器方法学习三平面表示，并利用泛化操作防止过拟合。随后，采用三平面感知的交叉注意力扩散模型有效整合特征，再通过预训练解码器模块快速生成3D体积。实验在三个不同规模的医学数据集上验证了其优越性，使用MSE、SSIM和W-GAN评估指标，结果表明该方法在重建质量和生成效果上优于现有方法。

item.title: AI代理加入团队对组织协调的影响研究
item.content:encoded:
AI代理加入团队后，协作增强、冲突减少、产出更独特。



摘要： 本文研究AI代理加入开源软件开发团队后对组织协调的影响。通过分析2,991个GitHub项目，发现引入机器人后，团队重复协作增加，对特定机器人的识别增强，冲突减少，产出更具特色。这些变化集中在采用机器人之后，而非逐渐积累。研究显示，机器人作为社区社会基础设施的一部分，能够促进协调与差异化，但其影响机制仍需进一步验证。

item.title: 基于反向留一回合归因的多轮搜索强化学习方法LAPO
item.content:encoded:
LAPO通过反向归因评估每轮交互贡献，提升多轮搜索效果。



摘要： 本文提出LAPO方法，用于多轮搜索中的强化学习，通过反向留一回合归因来评估每轮交互的贡献。该方法用[DELETE]占位符替换每轮检索结果，计算策略对黄金答案的对数似然变化，从而估计每轮的贡献。LAPO还引入符号一致性门控机制，仅保留与原始归因分数方向一致的归因优势。实验表明，LAPO在七个知识密集型问答数据集上取得优于IGPO基线的性能，证明了其有效性。

item.title: ThinkBLOX：基于视觉语言模型的渐进式3D场景生成框架
item.content:encoded:
ThinkBLOX通过渐进式推理提升3D场景生成质量与交互性。



摘要： 本文提出ThinkBLOX，一个基于视觉语言模型（VLM）的渐进式推理框架，用于生成更合理、可交互的3D室内场景。与传统的一次性生成方法不同，ThinkBLOX采用分步推理和动作机制，逐步设计并优化场景布局。研究构建了包含20万条数据的ThinkBLOX-Data-200K数据集，涵盖多视角场景上下文、显式思维链和结构化JSON布局。通过监督微调，VLM在增量更新中学习推理与动作之间的联系。此外，为解决多解任务中的奖励冲突问题，引入Tier-Decoupled GDPO强化学习方案，提升物理合理性、语义一致性及交互编辑能力。实验表明，ThinkBLOX在多个指标上优于现有方法，并支持多种3D场景生成应用。

item.title: LLM代理在供应链补货任务中的表现评估
item.content:encoded:
评估LLM代理在供应链补货任务中的决策能力与状态估计误差。



摘要： 本文提出STOCKTAKE基准，用于评估大型语言模型在26周供应链补货任务中的表现。该基准设计为部分可观测马尔可夫决策过程，包含六个隐藏因素，允许计算公平的参考策略。通过比较代理的表现与基准策略，可以分别衡量其状态估计和控制能力。实验结果显示，多个模型在检测隐藏失败方面表现良好，但技能评分差异显著，表明存在知识-行动差距。研究揭示了代理失败的两个方面：响应不足和过度反应，STOCKTAKE能够同时测量这两个方向。

item.title: GeoAnchor：一种用于3D空间关系理解的文本-潜在推理框架
item.content:encoded:
GeoAnchor提升3D空间关系理解能力，优于现有技术。



摘要： 尽管多模态大语言模型在多个领域取得进展，但从2D图像中理解3D空间关系仍面临挑战。现有方法依赖符号文本标记，难以准确表达连续几何信息。虽然一些方法采用潜在表示增强推理，但单一潜在类型难以适应多样化的空间任务，导致复杂场景下的误配。为此，本文提出GeoAnchor，一个融合文本与潜在表示的推理框架。该框架将3D空间信息分解为位置、方向和几何三个互补组件，并在结构化空间中重新组合以构建局部证据并捕捉全局上下文，实现动态且可解释的推理。此外，通过协作训练策略，引导模型从局部空间感知到全面的3D理解。大量实验表明，GeoAnchor在多种复杂3D推理任务中表现优于现有技术，验证了其有效性与泛化能力。

item.title: KANs与MLPs在结构化表格分类任务中的性能对比研究
item.content:encoded:
KANs在多数分类任务中优于MLPs，但计算成本更高。



摘要： 本研究对Kolmogorov-Arnold网络（KANs）和多层感知机（MLPs）在结构化表格分类任务中的表现进行了实证比较。在十二个公开数据集上评估了两种模型的测试准确率和F1分数，结果表明KANs在二分类和多类任务中表现更优，且整体上具有显著优势。然而，KANs的参数和计算复杂度远高于MLPs，尽管其通过自适应样条映射实现了更好的泛化能力。研究建议在高精度应用中优先使用KANs，而在资源受限环境下仍可选择MLPs。未来应进一步扩展到更多数据模态以完善架构选择标准。

item.title: 基于扩散模型的三角形网格生成方法Nexus
item.content:encoded:
Nexus通过解耦顶点与拓扑生成，提升网格生成质量。



摘要： 本文提出Nexus，一种基于扩散模型的三角形网格生成方法。该方法通过将顶点视为稀疏体素并采用分层生成方式，以及引入Spacetime Interval对拓扑进行编码，实现了高效的网格生成。实验表明，Nexus在Objaverse和Toys4K数据集及真实图像上均优于现有自回归和两阶段方法，且得到3D专业人士的高度认可。

item.title: 基于自然语言的机器人导航框架研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种自然语言驱动的机器人导航框架，实现用户指令到导航动作的转换。



摘要： 本文介绍了一种基于自然语言的机器人导航框架，通过整合语言理解、环境感知和自主导航技术，使非专业用户能够通过自然语言与移动机器人进行交互。该框架由模块化的ROS 2组件构成，能够将自然语言指令转化为导航目标，并利用ROS 2 Nav2导航栈执行任务。实验在模拟环境和真实场景中进行，使用TurtleBot3 Waffle和Unitree Go2机器人验证了系统的有效性，结果显示其能准确理解直接指令和上下文请求，并生成有意义的反馈。该框架展示了语义感知与自主导航结合的可行性，具有良好的平台兼容性。

item.title: 基于LLM与符号回归的生物系统ODE模型发现框架MEDA
item.content:encoded:
MEDA系统可自动发现生物系统的微分方程模型。



摘要： 本文介绍了MEDA系统，这是一个结合大语言模型（LLM）和符号回归（SR）的智能框架，用于发现生物及其仿生动态系统的常微分方程（ODE）模型。MEDA能够检索背景知识、定义变量、生成机制约束、提出候选方程并进行拟合与评估。实验表明，MEDA在模型恢复、推理外推和开放式发现任务中表现优异，能够生成生物合理的模型。分析显示，知识引导的形式化和机制约束是关键组件，而仅靠数值拟合可能产生轨迹兼容但生物学错误的方程。

item.title: 基于后验预测的在线类别发现方法DP-BOA
item.content:encoded:
DP-BOA通过后验预测框架实现在线类别发现，提升新类识别性能。



摘要： 本文提出DP-BOA，一种基于在线Dirichlet过程高斯混合模型和正态逆Wishart先验的后验预测决策框架。该方法在训练阶段利用标记数据校准共享的NIW先验，并初始化已知类别的后验分布。测试时，对每个样本比较分配到已有类别的后验预测证据与由DP先验引发的新类别证据，从而决定是否生成新类别，并在线更新类别统计信息。该方法能捕捉类别几何特性并随证据积累自适应调整置信度，在标准OCD基准测试中表现优异，尤其在新类识别方面效果显著。

item.title: SFF-CLIP：提升CLIP细粒度特征表示的自标注微调方法
item.content:encoded:
SFF-CLIP通过自标注方式提升CLIP的细粒度特征表示能力。



摘要： 本文提出SFF-CLIP，一种基于自标注细粒度微调的CLIP改进方法。传统方法在提升CLIP的细粒度能力时依赖额外区域标注，限制了语义多样性并增加数据处理负担，同时可能损害CLIP原有的全局视觉表示能力。SFF-CLIP仅使用图像-文本对作为输入，在不牺牲全局视觉语义一致性的情况下，通过运行时区域-短语对齐机制，从输入句子中提取概念短语，并利用文本特定热图将其与区域特征对齐，从而增强细粒度特征表示。实验表明，SFF-CLIP在细粒度密集特征表示任务中表现显著提升，同时保持原CLIP在图像级任务上的性能。

item.title: On-policy Distillation训练动态与优化研究
item.content:encoded:
OPD通过高质量引导信号提升模型探索效果。



摘要： 本文系统研究了On-policy Distillation（OPD）在大语言模型后训练中的作用、问题及优化方法。研究指出，OPD作为探索催化剂，通过密集的token级引导信号帮助学生模型走向正确推理路径，但其效果高度依赖于引导信号的质量。文章揭示了两种关键问题：Student-Teacher Mismatch导致引导信号与任务正确性不匹配，以及Length Exploitation使学生通过响应截断或冗余填充绕过真实推理策略。为解决这些问题，作者提出轻量级信号调控方法，如优势裁剪和对数压缩，实验证明这些方法有效缓解了长度利用问题，提升了OPD性能。

item.title: 基于梯度流的神经网络收敛训练方法
item.content:encoded:
一种基于梯度流的神经网络训练方法，保证收敛性。



摘要： 本文提出了一种基于梯度流的神经网络训练方法，适用于包含解析激活函数的网络。该方法利用Lojasiewicz理论确保收敛性，通过求解常微分方程系统来近似网络系数。实验表明，该方法能够准确再现简单常微分方程解对参数的依赖关系，并在参数较少的情况下合理近似涉及波约束的反问题解，即使在严重不适定区域也能取得良好效果。

item.title: 开源项目中代理编码工具的采用与协作模式研究
item.content:encoded:
代理编码工具在开源项目中采用有限，协作以单人监督为主。



摘要： 本文分析了25,264个来自2,361个GitHub热门仓库的代理生成拉取请求，探讨了代理编码工具的采用情况、项目级生产力以及人机协作模式。结果显示，大多数项目在三个月内仅生成一到两个代理PR，表明高强度采用仍集中在少数项目中。小型项目（1-5名贡献者）的参与度和平均PR活动水平高于中型和大型项目。此外，项目级代理PR生产力差异显著，仅有少数项目超过行业估计的每参与者36个PR的水平。最后，人机协作主要依赖单一开发者审核，多开发者协作较为少见。研究为开源项目如何组织代理编码工具的人工监督提供了早期实证依据，并指出代理贡献的成功整合不仅依赖于技术进步，还需依赖人类和组织流程。

item.title: MOJO框架提升神经解码模型在有限标注数据下的性能
item.content:encoded:
MOJO结合自监督学习提升神经解码模型性能。



摘要： 本文提出MOJO框架，通过联合自监督学习和有监督学习，提升基于尖峰数据的神经解码模型性能。该方法在多个动物实验数据集上表现出色，尤其在标注数据有限的情况下效果显著。MOJO还提升了神经元表征的可解释性，并在人类脑电数据中展现出良好的泛化能力，为神经基础模型的数据使用提供了更灵活和可扩展的路径。

item.title: 基于量子厨房洗牌的无线网络异常检测研究
item.content:encoded:
量子厨房洗牌在无线信号异常检测中表现优异。



摘要： 本文研究了量子厨房洗牌（QKS）在无线射频信号异常检测中的应用。通过引入多深度数据重上传和环形纠缠，作者对QKS进行了扩展，并在受控的射频光谱图异常检测任务上进行了评估。实验采用五阶段消融协议，系统分析了浅层架构、重上传深度、训练次数、输入表示和经典读出的影响。结果表明，离散余弦变换（DCT）表示优于原始和主成分分析（PCA）输入，中等深度纠缠的QKS配置表现最佳。QKS在所有测试配置中均优于传统基线，最高测试AUROC为0.8778，F1值为0.7995。研究结合了真实测量的子6GHz蜂窝信号和IBM Quebec量子处理器的实际验证，展示了QKS在无线网络异常检测中的实际可行性。

item.title: CANON：利用共识提升大语言模型推理准确性的无标签训练方法
item.content:encoded:
CANON通过共识生成密集监督信号，提升模型推理性能。



摘要： 本文提出CANON（Consensus-ANchored self-distillatiON），一种无需标签的训练方法，通过多解采样和多数答案生成密集的token级监督信号。该方法在数学和科学推理基准测试中表现出色，相比无标签强化学习提升6个百分点，且计算成本更低。实验表明，CANON不仅提升了模型的准确性，还使其能够解决之前无法解决的问题，显示出其有效性。

item.title: 工业级视频修复模型LPM的开发与应用
item.content:encoded:
LPM实现高质量视频修复，提升用户体验并降低带宽成本。



摘要： 本文介绍了LPM（Large Processing Model），一个基于扩散的生成式框架，用于在复杂退化条件下进行逼真视频修复。LPM是首个在工业规模部署的视频修复模型，通过统一系统处理用户生成内容中的多种退化问题。其增强架构、渐进式训练策略和时间金字塔推理机制实现了高质量、时间一致的视频修复。LPM已在快手部署，处理视频占总观看时间的45%，显著提升体验指标。此外，LPM在保持相同画质的前提下，将码率降低了20%，节省了大量带宽成本，并成功应用于Kling等产品中，证明了生成式修复在大规模视频处理中的可行性。

item.title: 基于聚类的特征选择方法在可再生能源预测中的应用
item.content:encoded:
提出CSFS方法提升可再生能源预测效率与准确性。



摘要： 随着全球能源需求上升和对气候变化的关注增加，可再生能源在能源结构中的占比持续增长。然而，由于可再生能源依赖环境条件，其输出难以稳定控制，因此准确预测成为关键。本文通过两篇文献综述，分析了风力发电功率曲线建模和光伏电力预测的研究现状，发现现有特征选择方法存在系统性不足。为此，作者提出了一种新的模型无关的聚类特征选择方法——Cluster-based Sequential Feature Selection (CSFS)，旨在提高可再生能源预测的效率与可靠性。该方法在两个实际案例中进行了验证，并与传统方法进行比较，结果显示CSFS在保持预测性能的同时，平均降低了21%的计算成本。

item.title: 基于多模态融合的机器人状态编码器研究
item.content:encoded:
机器人状态编码器提升视觉感知能力，增强力/扭矩信息恢复。



摘要： 本文提出Kepler-Encoder-v0.1，一种以机器人状态为模态的多模态编码器，融合视觉、本体感觉和力/扭矩信息。通过自监督训练，该模型在仅输入视觉的情况下仍能有效恢复末端执行器状态和力信息，优于传统视觉特征和控制方法。实验表明，该编码器在不同机器人上表现一致，且其潜在空间可直接用于状态监测和图像重建，验证了单时刻状态融合的有效性。

研究在线移动众包中考虑成本与性能提升的招聘策略。

item.title: 甲烷羽流产品中的边界不确定性分析
item.content:encoded:
甲烷羽流边界存在不确定性，影响排放量计算。



摘要： 本文研究了基于EMIT数据的碳 Mapper 甲烷羽流产品中，不同羽流掩码对集成质量增强（IME）、羽流长度和排放率的影响。通过遗传算法（GA）方法，发现多个不同的掩码可以产生相同的IME、长度和排放率，表明羽流边界存在等效性。研究还评估了CM-like掩码与参考掩码的一致性，结果显示其在IME、排放率和不确定性方面具有较高的匹配度，但弱羽流仍存在较大不确定性，需专家审核。

item.title: 基于音频和文本的犹豫/矛盾视频识别系统
item.content:encoded:
提出一种基于音频和文本的视频识别方法，用于处理犹豫与矛盾任务。



摘要： 本文介绍了一种用于第11届ABAW竞赛中犹豫/矛盾视频识别挑战的音频-文本系统。该方法不使用视觉帧，而是将每个视频表示为与转录时间戳对齐的重叠5秒窗口。每个窗口结合了320维的语音特征、768维的情感导向RoBERTa嵌入以及74个手工特征，用于捕捉不确定性、犹豫和态度冲突。通过时间交叉注意力融合音频和文本，并在门控多实例学习（MIL）池化前注入支持特征以调节窗口的重要性。五个独立初始化模型的预测结果被平均。在公开的发展集上，集成模型的平均精度为0.875，宏F1值为0.72。源代码已公开。

PQFA提升多模态融合效果，参数更高效。

item.title: AI助手对人类判断力的影响研究
item.content:encoded:
AI助手削弱了人类的自我怀疑能力，导致错误率上升但自信增强。



摘要： 研究表明，当人们可以使用AI助手时，即使AI给出的答案是错误的，他们也更倾向于直接回答问题而不是暂停判断。在五项实验中，参与者在有AI可用的情况下，虽然回答更多问题，但正确率仅为无AI时的三分之一，而他们的信心却几乎翻倍。尽管通过激励准确性和惩罚错误性可以部分缓解这一现象，但人们仍然比没有AI时更少选择暂停判断。这表明，随着AI建议的普及，它可能正在改变人类决定是否具备足够知识来回答问题的元认知阈值。

item.title: TRACE-PCa: Predicting Prostate Cancer Progression from Longitudinal MRI During Active Surveillance
item.content:encoded: Active surveillance (AS) is the preferred strategy for favorable-risk prostate cancer, yet current protocols rely on scheduled repeat biopsies, most of which reveal no progression and are unnecessary. Existing risk-stratification tools operate on single time-point imaging or depend on explicit lesion segmentation, limiting their ability to capture longitudinal change and excluding patients without an MRI-visible lesion. In this study, we propose an end-to-end temporal and multimodal model for predicting pathological progression during AS without lesion segmentation. We encode each serial scan with a pretrained 3D MRI foundation model and introduce a temporal attention gate that recalibrates the multi-visit features to amplify focal imaging changes associated with progression. The gated imaging representation is then fused with clinical variables in a multimodal framework to estimate the probability of progression. Validated on a longitudinal AS cohort, our approach consistently outperforms competing baselines and performs comparably to the radiologist assessment representing current clinical practice. It maintains high negative predictive value while achieving higher positive predictive value, demonstrating its potential to safely reduce unnecessary biopsies during surveillance.

item.title: Partially Correlated Verifier Cascades in LLM Harnesses: Concave Log-Odds, Polynomial Reliability, and Blind-Spot Ceilings
item.content:encoded: Serial verification gates are a core reliability primitive in LLM harnesses: a candidate answer is returned only if $k$ verifier calls all accept it. Under conditionally independent gates, the recent Odds Law (arXiv:2606.15712) shows that posterior log-odds grow linearly in $k$, so failure decays exponentially, and states that "a tight theory of partially correlated verifier cascades remains open." This note gives a minimal such theory. Modeling the per-instance false-accept rate on the generator's own errors as a latent variable $α\sim G$ (de Finetti), the exact cascade posterior is $\ell_k = \ell_0 - \ln m_k$, with $m_k$ the $k$-th moment of $G$. Then: (i) $\ell_k$ is concave in $k$ for every non-degenerate $G$ -- the Odds Law is its tangent at the first gate and an upper bound; (ii) for Beta$(a,b)$ latents, failure decays polynomially, $1-r_k \asymp k^{-b}$, with correlation parameter $ρ_v = 1/(a+b+1)$; (iii) a blind-spot atom of mass $1-π$ at $α=1$ caps the evidence extractable from any number of gates at $-\ln(1-π)$ nats, so reliability saturates below 1; (iv) letting the true-accept rate also vary ($β\sim H$) yields a trichotomy -- gates eventually always help, plateau, or actively harm -- decided by the upper-tail exponents of $G$ and $H$, with closed-form crossover $k^\dagger$. The mechanism is survivorship: errors surviving gates are the high-$α$ ones. The theory is measurable: $R$ repeated verdicts per instance identify the first $R$ moments of $G$, so two verdicts identify $ρ_v$; beta-binomial likelihood and NPMLE recover the reliability curve and the ill-posed ceiling. In synthetic tests, independence-based extrapolation underestimates failure by 20x at $k=5$ and ~3000x at $k=10$; the correlated fit at $R=8$ tracks held-out depths. The practical lever is decorrelation -- changing model family, modality, or evidence source -- not adding gates.

item.title: SIVA-RL: Sensitivity-Invariance Visual Alignment for Multimodal Reinforcement Learning
item.content:encoded: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) drives multimodal reasoning, but answer-level correctness does not guarantee that a vision-language model grounds its predictions in visual evidence. Existing visual-intervention methods contrast policy behavior on original and modified images, yet assign supervision by the type of intervention rather than its observed effect. This assumption fails: identical operators produce heterogeneous outcomes across samples. We propose SIVA-RL, a Sensitivity-Invariance Visual Alignment framework that replaces operator-conditioned regularization with sample-wise, outcome-conditioned supervision. SIVA-RL constructs localized interventions through token-aligned, distance-constrained within-image PatchSwap. A frozen audit policy then scores each clean-intervention pair, and the observed reward drop becomes soft routing weights. Large-drop pairs drive sensitivity alignment, low-drop pairs drive clean-anchored invariance alignment, and ambiguous pairs are down-weighted. This design decouples intervention construction from supervision assignment and is compatible with both GRPO and DAPO backbones. Across nine multimodal reasoning benchmarks spanning mathematical, logical, and vision-dependent tasks, SIVA-RL improves 3B and 7B models over matched RL baselines in every setting. It yields an 8.79 percentage-point gain on vision-dependent reasoning and up to 14.9% relative overall improvement across all four GRPO- and DAPO-based configurations.

item.title: Algebraic Representability as the Limiting Regime of Grokking: An Exactly Solvable Model with Holomorphic Activations
item.content:encoded: Neural networks trained on modular arithmetic exhibit grokking, a delayed transition from memorisation to generalisation known to depend on model capacity: too little and the network memorises slowly or not at all, too much and it generalises almost immediately. What happens at the extreme of this spectrum, when the architecture's expressible function class collapses to a finite-dimensional algebraic variety? We study two-layer networks with a holomorphic monomial activation sigma(z)=z^k, trained on modular tasks encoded via roots of unity. Here the network output, regardless of hidden width, is confined to a (k+1)-dimensional subspace of characters of (Z_p)^2, an O(k/p^2) slice of the full function space. We give a complete algebraic characterisation of this subspace: a task is representable if and only if its discrete Fourier support lies on the diagonal u+v = k (mod p), which for linear-phase targets reduces to the arithmetic criterion m+n=k. This is not merely a constraint on eventual generalisation but on memorisation itself: because the outputs are algebraically confined, a non-representable target cannot be fit even on the training set, and we prove a positive lower bound on the training loss, independent of width. Across 585 runs the algebraic prediction matches the observed outcome with 99.8% accuracy, with no memorisation regime and no grokking; outcomes split cleanly into instant success and outright failure. This binary behaviour is the limiting case of the capacity-grokking relationship: when the expressible class shrinks to a fixed algebraic object, the question of when a network will grok dissolves into whether it can represent the target at all. A bottleneck ablation connects this extreme to standard networks, tracing a continuous path from representational failure, through memorisation without generalisation, to grokking with a shrinking gap as capacity grows.

item.title: WAVE-Stereo: Warp-Aligned Volume Encoding for Stereo Matching
item.content:encoded: Existing iterative stereo matching methods primarily adopt two types of correspondence representation: explicit matching search via correlation volumes and local residual refinement via warped features, yet the two remain separately modeled. We propose WAVE-Stereo, built on a core insight: correlation volumes and feature warping provide complementary matching cues. \textbf{GeoWarp Correspondence Encoder (GWCE)} encodes matching search, residual alignment, and disparity prior in parallel at the ConvGRU input. To mitigate matching degradation in textureless regions, we propose \textbf{Periodic Global Context Propagation (PGCP)}, which propagates global spatial information in a periodic manner. On five real-world benchmarks -- Middlebury, ETH3D, KITTI 2012, KITTI 2015, and Booster -- WAVE-Stereo achieves competitive zero-shot generalization accuracy without any external foundation model prior, achieving 3.18\% D1-all on KITTI 2015, 4.42\% Bad-2.0 on Booster, and 66ms real-time inference, striking a favorable balance between accuracy and efficiency. Our code is available at https://github.com/yamanoko-do/WAVE-Stereo.

item.title: FastCentNN: Accelerating Centroid Neural Network with Entropy Proxy
item.content:encoded: Centroid neural network (CentNN) is an unsupervised competitive learning algorithm in which centroid splitting is triggered only after strict local stabilization, often leading to prolonged low-movement training phases before model expansion. This report proposes FastCentNN, an accelerated variant that addresses this inefficiency by introducing an early splitting strategy based on the total centroid movement per epoch, which serves as a training entropy proxy. As a result, FastCentNN reduces unnecessary reassignment epochs while preserving the original winner-loser learning dynamics. FastCentNN supports both absolute and stage-relative movement thresholds, allowing the splitting criterion to remain either fixed or adaptive throughout training. Experiments on some benchmark datasets show that FastCentNN consistently achieves clustering quality comparable to CentNN while reducing runtime by up to 16% on synthetic 2D datasets and about 5% on high-dimensional datasets. FastCentNN therefore provides a practical and efficient drop-in replacement for CentNN, retaining its online adaptive learning behavior while offering a simple and interpretable speed-stability trade-off through configurable splitting thresholds.

item.title: Earthquaker-AI: A Retrieval-Augmented Generation Framework with Rubric-Based Assessment for Primary School Earthquake Education
item.content:encoded: This paper presents Earthquaker-AI, a hybrid educational framework building upon a previously implemented educational robotics project by integrating a conversational AI assistant based on Retrieval-Augmented Generation. It aims to enhance earthquake preparedness and conscious action among primary-school students. The system extends the award-winning STEM project Earthquaker moving from mechanical simulation with Lego WeDo2 to cognitive and metacognitive processing. The robotics component uses Lego WeDo2 automation to simulate seismic response, letting students interact with sensors and actuators as tangible representations of protective actions. The assistant operates as a guided learning mechanism aligning student responses with safety guidelines, while providing rubric-based verbal feedback that supports self-regulated learning and calmness under emergency conditions. Earthquaker-AI follows a progressive learning trajectory aligned with cognitive development. In early grades, the focus is on basic recognition of safety actions through multiple-choice questions, assessed via a two-dimensional rubric. In middle grades, students identify correct action sequences through multiple-choice questions, evaluated via a three-axis rubric. In upper grades, the approach shifts to verbal production, requiring short written responses assessed via a four-dimensional rubric that includes clarity of expression. The dialogic module uses RAG to match student queries semantically with official guidelines, generating safe, accurate responses. Experimental evaluation shows high groundedness and accuracy, with a low hallucination rate. Overall, Earthquaker-AI combines hands-on engagement, information processing, and reflective practice. Combining robotics, rubrics, and AI promotes technological literacy, self-regulation, and responsible use of digital systems, contributing to early crisis-management skills.

item.title: Price of Fairness in Bandits: A Tight Minimax Characterization
item.content:encoded: In bandit problems, standard regret-minimizing algorithms treat exploration as an amortized cost, which can expose early participants to unfair ex-ante losses in settings such as clinical trials. Recent work addresses this by evaluating the sequence of per-round expected rewards through the generalized $p$-mean, interpolating between utilitarian welfare ($p=1$), Nash welfare ($p\to0$), and Rawlsian fairness ($p\to-\infty$). Although tight guarantees are known for $p\ge0$, the strictly fair regime $q=-p>0$ remains unresolved because negative-power means are dominated by the smallest per-round rewards. For $σ$-sub-Gaussian rewards with nonnegative means, the best prior algorithm relied on uniform early exploration and achieved regret $O(k^{(q+1)/2}/\sqrt{T})$, while the only general lower bound was the classical $Ω(σ\sqrt{k/T})$. Thus it was unclear whether the extra dependence on $k$ was intrinsic to strict fairness or an artifact of uniform exploration. We close this gap by identifying the exact polynomial price of strict fairness. Using a needle-in-haystack construction, we prove an algorithm-independent lower bound $Ω(σ\sqrt{k^{\max(1,q)}/T})$; for $q>1$, this shows that the penalty $k^{q/2}$ is information-theoretically unavoidable. We then introduce \textsf{UCB-HARE} (Harmonic Anchored Rank Exploration), which replaces uniform exploration with an inverse-weighted harmonic rank schedule protected by a certified positive-mean anchor. Its regret is $\widetilde{O}(σ\sqrt{k^{\max(1,q)}/T})$, matching the lower bound up to logarithmic factors. Experiments on synthetic instances confirm that \textsf{UCB-HARE} improves over uniform-exploration baselines, with gains increasing as $q$ grows.

item.title: Linear Independent Component Analysis via Optimal Transport
item.content:encoded: Linear Independent Component Analysis (ICA) recovers jointly independent source signals from their linear mixtures. To achieve this, classical ICA algorithms attempt to maximize non-Gaussianity, measured by negentropy, which is linked to independence by information theory. Because exact negentropy optimization is intractable, they rely on proxy contrast functions, such as fourth-order cumulants, and parametric log-likelihoods. We propose instead to measure non-Gaussianity using the squared Wasserstein distance $W_2^2$ to a standard Gaussian. We prove that the Wasserstein distance between a standard normal distribution and linear projections of the data is maximized when the projection recovers an independent component. Based on this observation, we propose the OT-ICA algorithm which finds this projection by gradient-based optimization. Empirical evaluation on simulated data shows that OT-ICA outperforms proxy-based methods for different distributions of the latent variables. Application to EEG artifact removal and econometric price discovery confirm OT-ICA can be used for applied ICA tasks without distributional assumptions.

item.title: PiVoT: A Variational Solution for Real-time Large-scale Multi-object Detection and Tracking under Heavy Clutter
item.content:encoded: Multi-object detection and tracking from noisy point clouds remain challenging in many data-scarce radar applications. Current Bayesian trackers based on Poisson measurement models offer a training-free solution but struggle to achieve accuracy and efficiency under severe clutter, large object populations, and full-resolution Doppler point clouds. We address this with PiVoT, a fast, clutter-resilient multi-object tracker for both positional and Doppler measurements. PiVoT performs end-to-end detection and tracking of a large and time-varying number of objects without external clustering or detectors, through joint inference of object states, shapes, existence probabilities, data association, and measurement rates. Its efficiency is driven by several variational inference innovations, such as theoretically justified birth pruning, quadratic-to-linear complexity reductions for exact updates, and a computationally efficient Doppler Poisson model. Experiments show that PiVoT substantially outperforms existing Bayesian trackers in challenging scenes, while also demonstrating exceptional scalability to a thousand objects, robustness to clutter visually inseparable from objects, and real-time operation on full-scale modern automotive radar datasets, where it attains performance comparable to a deep-learning detection benchmark as a training-free joint detector and tracker.

item.title: Towards a Modular Bin-picking Framework for Handling Object Pose Uncertainties
item.content:encoded: In recent years, there has been growing interest in robust robotic systems for precise bin-picking applications. To achieve reliable performance, such systems must address errors arising from both the object pose estimation and the grasping process. Although various approaches have been proposed, they typically target specific challenges and do not offer general solutions. In this paper, we present a modular framework that jointly handles both error types. The framework incorporates object pose distribution estimation to account for pose uncertainty, which frequently arises in situations with ambiguous observations where a single correct pose cannot be determined. To further reduce uncertainty, we introduce a second-viewpoint module that computes complementary pose distributions, which are subsequently fused. This fusion decreases overall uncertainty and improves system efficiency. Additionally, two independent modules are included to compensate for grasping errors. The modular design allows the components to be combined for optimal performance or used individually, depending on the physical setup. The proposed method is evaluated in a real-world setup with three different objects, with no errors, and all modules are shown to improve efficiency. These results suggest that incorporating pose distributions with grasping pose errors is a promising direction for developing more flexible and reliable robotic production systems. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first framework that jointly addresses both grasping and object pose uncertainties using interchangeable modules. We believe there is ample opportunity to integrate additional modules, resulting in improved performance and flexibility. The current framework is limited to pose uncertainties in SO(2), but it could be extended to SE(3), enabling additional modules to improve the system.

item.title: How Far Can Root Cause Analysis Go on Real-World Telemetry Data?
item.content:encoded: Identifying root causes in production microservice failures requires reasoning over large-scale, multimodal telemetry spanning metrics, logs, and traces, a problem that has proved resistant to both classical and LLM-based approaches. The OpenRCA dataset exemplifies these challenges: it is large-scale, multimodal, and lacks detailed domain knowledge, and yields consistently low accuracy across all existing methods. We show that classical causal discovery methods and existing LLM-based multi-agent systems fail to reliably identify root causes on this benchmark, and present a Structured Multi-Agent RCA pipeline that substantially outperforms existing LLM-based and classical baselines, supporting both domain-knowledge and knowledge-free operating modes. To diagnose where failures originate, we introduce a reverse reasoning agent that, given the correct answer, identifies which signals in the extracted anomalies support it and determines whether Stage~1 had access to those signals, classifying each failure as Reasoning Gap (evidence present but unused) or Data Ambiguity (evidence genuinely absent). This analysis reveals that the required evidence is present in the vast majority of failures: the bottleneck is not data access but the agent's ability to reason over it correctly. We further introduce an automated rule mining pipeline that systematically extracts discrimination rules from reverse reasoning reports, reducing reliance on manual knowledge curation. Across all configurations, model reasoning capability and domain knowledge are the primary constraints: stronger models embed more domain expertise, and explicit knowledge injection partially compensates for this gap. Reasoning performance remains practically bounded even when evidence extraction is perfect: scaffold engineering and better data pipelines alone cannot close this gap; progress requires improvements at the model level.

item.title: Optimal and Efficient Contextual Combinatorial Semi-bandits with General Function Approximation
item.content:encoded: We study the contextual combinatorial semi-bandit (CCSB) problem with general reward function approximation. At each round, the learner observes a context, selects a combinatorial action consisting of a subset of basic arms, and receives the reward of each selected arm; the goal is to maximize the cumulative reward over time. We propose SquareCB.Comb, a computationally efficient algorithm that, at each round, solves a convex optimization problem to sample a combinatorial action that balances exploration and exploitation. SquareCB.Comb scales to large arm sets and imposes no structural assumptions on the action set beyond a cardinality bound of $m$ on each combinatorial action. We prove that SquareCB.Comb achieves a minimax optimal regret bound of $O(\sqrt{m A T \log |\mathcal{F}|})$, where $A$ is the number of arms, $m$ is the maximum number of arms in a combinatorial action, $T$ is the time horizon, and $\mathcal{F}$ is the reward function class. In the realizable setting, this bound matches the state-of-the-art regret guarantees achieved by policy search-based algorithms in the more restricted slate recommendation settings, while simultaneously generalizing to arbitrary combinatorial action structures and general reward function approximation.

item.title: How the Hessian-Spectrum of Neural Networks Depends on Data
item.content:encoded: The Hessian matrix is an important quantity of interest when it comes to studying the loss landscape and optimization dynamics in deep learning, as well as designing measures of generalization, second-order learning algorithms, etc. Prior works have focused on empirical results or pursued a theoretical treatment under overly simplified settings. In this work, we derive the eigenvalues of the Hessian of linear networks with arbitrary widths and depths, and datasets with an arbitrary number of samples, features, and labels. Importantly, for classification tasks with MSE loss, we identify that the sharpness of the solution is directly related to the maximum proportion of samples belonging to any class. We empirically validate our predictions and systematically analyze the effects of shedding the impractical assumptions one at a time, as well as incorporating nonlinearities. We observe that our predictions are considerably robust in most cases, allowing us to extend our conclusions to more practical learning setups.

item.title: Parallel gradient boosting for flexible estimation of conditional distributions
item.content:encoded: Boosting is one of the most successful learning techniques for standard classification and regression tasks. Its extension to multi-output prediction problems has found an increasing number of applications in recent years. Among them is the prediction of entire conditional distributions rather than single functionals, which can often be framed as a multi-output regression problem, for example multiple quantile regression. Addressing such problems with classical implementations of boosting is computationally challenging, because usually one base model is trained for each target at every iteration. More efficient variants of boosting have been proposed to speed up training, but they tend to be tied to specific loss functions and classes of base learners, usually decision trees. In this work, we study a modification of the gradient boosting algorithm, which we call parallel gradient boosting, designed to circumvent all these limitations. The core idea is to use a common descent direction for all training observations. By doing so, only one base model is needed at each iteration, regardless of the number of targets, which allows for considerable performance gains. We establish sufficient conditions for the convergence of the algorithm, whose practical use is introduced via the multiple quantile regression setting. We show that in such a setting, it provides predictions of similar quality to state-of-the-art boosting libraries such as XGBoost, while being faster by several orders of magnitude. Then, we evaluate the properties of the resulting conditional distribution estimator, which is shown empirically to outperform other nonparametric and semiparametric estimators, especially in high-dimensional settings and in the presence of mixed and/or missing covariates.

item.title: Constraint-Driven Model Optimization: An Industry Framework for Selecting Compression and Acceleration Techniques in Modern Machine Learning Systems
item.content:encoded: The rapid deployment of machine learning systems across cloud, edge, and enterprise environments has brought model optimization to the forefront of systems-engineering. Despite a rich literature spanning quantization, pruning, knowledge distillation, parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT), and inference-time optimization, practitioners are often left navigating these techniques through heuristics rather than principled methodology. We argue that optimization should be formulated as a constraint-driven, multi-objective engineering decision and introduce a unified framework that characterizes any production deployment along five interacting constraint dimensions: data availability, latency budget, memory budget, accuracy tolerance, and retraining budget. Building on this taxonomy, we synthesize empirical gains reported across the research literature and map them to operational constraints rather than algorithmic categories. To ensure practical relevance, we selected these techniques by reviewing recent literature for methods that report measurable improvements against critical deployment bottlenecks. We propose a prescriptive decision framework and provide optimization pipelines for four representative industrial scenarios to illustrate it in practice. To the best of our knowledge, this work provides one of the first structured attempts to formalize model optimization as a constraint-aware, multi-objective engineering process, synthesizing quantitative evidence from the research literature.

item.title: AgentCompass: A Unified Evaluation Infrastructure for Agent Capabilities
item.content:encoded: As Large Language Models (LLMs) evolve into autonomous agents, the need for unified evaluation infrastructure becomes critical. However, current evaluation pipelines remain highly fragmented and tightly coupled, hindering reproducibility and causing redundant engineering. To address this, we introduce AgentCompass, an open-source, lightweight, and extensible infrastructure for evaluating LLM-based agents. AgentCompass organizes the evaluation process around three independent components, namely Benchmark, Harness, and Environment, thereby enabling flexible configurations without requiring the reimplementation of complex execution logic. Furthermore, it features a fault-tolerant asynchronous runtime and comprehensive trajectory analysis tools to transparently diagnose nuanced failure modes like reward-hacking. Natively supporting over 20 benchmarks across five capability dimensions, AgentCompass provides the community with a scalable and reproducible infrastructure for advancing agent research.

item.title: Discrete Diffusion Models: A Unified Framework from Tokenization to Generation
item.content:encoded: Discrete denoising diffusion models (DDMs) have recently emerged as a compelling alternative to autoregressive (AR) modeling for discrete data, offering parallel generation and iterative global refinement capabilities. Unlike continuous diffusion, where the state space is fixed, DDMs are fundamentally shaped by how the discrete state space is constructed: the tokenization scheme, the vocabulary topology, and domain-specific structural alphabets. This work introduces a unified conceptual framework that views discrete diffusion models through the construction of the underlying discrete state space. Within this framework, existing formulations, including transition-matrix, masking/absorbing-state, and score/ratio-based approaches, emerge as different instantiations of a common design space. The framework further exposes common design trade-offs across training objectives, inference algorithms, scaling behavior, systems optimization, and evaluation protocols, suggesting several promising directions for future research.

item.title: MetaPerch: Learning from metadata for bioacoustics foundation models
item.content:encoded: Bioacoustic foundation models rely on large-scale citizen science platforms like Xeno-Canto for geographically and ecologically diverse data. Recent work has shown that supervision alone can produce SotA species detection models when trained on this large-scale data -- however, there remains unutilized potential in the form of recording metadata readily available within these community-driven data hubs. In this work, we explore the use of metadata -- such as location and time -- as auxiliary supervision signals, allowing the model to leverage species-metadata correlations in its learned representation. Auxiliary metadata losses provide additional information beyond vocalizations alone that can encourage a richer, more robust representation that generalizes better to species distribution and acoustic domain shifts -- important challenges for deployment in real-world passive acoustic monitoring (PAM) settings. We introduce MetaPerch, a new foundation model that achieves strong species identification performance across multiple challenging domains and present an extensive empirical study of the effects of 9 diverse metadata sources on 17 bioacoustic datasets.

item.title: MxGPS: Multiplex Graph Transformers for a Power Grid Foundation Model
item.content:encoded: Single-task fine-tuning of graph neural networks (GNNs) for power grid problems exhibits a systematic failure mode: models that achieve the lowest in-distribution error degrade the most under topology shift. We term this topology overfitting: the tendency of task-specific gradient signals to encode relational structure particular to the training topologies rather than the underlying physics, causing models to fail on unseen grids despite strong in-distribution performance. To expose and address this failure mode, we introduce MxGPS (Multiplex GPS), a multiplex graph transformer that runs K task-specialised GPS branches over a shared node encoder, jointly trained on Static State Estimation (SSE) and AC Power Flow (PF) via a self-supervised pre-training and multi-task fine-tuning protocol, with a cross-branch attention module evaluated in ablation. The joint SSE+PF objective forces the shared encoder to simultaneously satisfy complementary gradient signals, preventing it from overfitting to topology-specific relational structure. Under a 3-fold sliding-window cross-validation spanning four unseen topologies (14-, 24-, 162-, and 300-bus), MxGPS attains 0% boundary violation rate (BVR) on all four zero-shot Power Flow topologies. Critically, models with substantially lower in-distribution PF error degrade by 190% to 1400% under topology shift, whereas MxGPS degrades by only 39%, an inversion that directly implicates topology overfitting as the failure mechanism rather than insufficient model capacity. With only 1.6M parameters (12x fewer than the GridFM reference baseline), MxGPS demonstrates that multi-task joint training is a principled and parameter-efficient mechanism for topology-agnostic generalisation in power grid foundation models.

item.title: 基于大语言模型的量化策略优化框架EVOQUANT
item.content:encoded:
EVOQUANT提升量化策略表现，显著改善夏普比率。



摘要： 本文提出EVOQUANT，一个自进化验证引导的量化策略优化框架。该方法利用大语言模型深入诊断策略瓶颈，生成语义控制的候选修改，并通过多阶段验证管道选择最优策略，同时将优化经验转化为可复用知识以实现持续改进。在A股和加密货币市场的七种代表性策略上进行测试，结果表明EVOQUANT显著提升了所有策略的夏普比率，平均测试夏普率从-0.298提升至0.538，最佳策略相对提升199%。消融实验和压力测试进一步验证了框架的有效性和鲁棒性。该研究将量化策略优化从人工试错转变为自动化、可验证的迭代范式。

item.title: 语言模型置信度的计算框架研究
item.content:encoded:
研究语言模型置信度的计算机制及其与决策变量的关系。



摘要： 本文探讨了语言模型中置信度的本质，分析了答案logits是否反映了一个潜在的决策变量，而非启发式的偏好信号。通过统计决策置信度（SDC）框架，研究发现，在多种感知和记忆任务中，答案logits表现出与潜在决策变量一致的特征，表明其作为决策变量读取器的有效性。然而在复杂的视觉推理任务中，SDC的完整几何特征未被观察到，说明当前框架在缺乏明确规范过程模型时存在局限。该研究为多模态语言模型中的置信度提供了计算解释，并建立了SDC作为生物与人工智能中置信度研究的统一框架。

item.title: 智能作为原子压缩与组合复用的理论框架
item.content:encoded:
本文提出智能的原子压缩与组合复用理论框架。



摘要： 本文提出一种理解智能的新理论框架，认为认知、生物、计算和组织系统通过将复杂现象分解为可复用的原子单元，并重新组合成更高级结构来实现可扩展的智能。文章结合认知科学、信息论、进化生物学、软件工程等多个领域的证据，提出原子单元作为基础压缩层，有助于效率、迁移性、可解释性和可演化性。核心贡献是“压缩演算”，用于比较表层表示与原子表示，并描述压缩收益如何在抽象层次中累积。文章还提出“复合级联”假说，认为每一层抽象都会乘数式地提升表示效率。同时指出当前AI系统常处于低效的表示水平，依赖于标记或文档级处理，而非稳定的概念级原子结构。最后，该框架为设计自我演化的知识系统提供了基础，能够随时间发现、优化和组合新原型。

item.title: 基于能量的张力脊结构形态寻找与物理性质预测方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种能量驱动的学习框架用于张力脊结构形态和物理性质预测。



摘要： 本文针对张力脊结构的形态寻找和物理性质预测问题，提出了一种基于能量的学习框架。该方法结合总势能最小化和本构关系，能够同时预测平衡节点配置及成员力和力密度等物理量。通过在训练过程中引入基于能量的物理损失函数，提高了模型的物理一致性、鲁棒性和数据效率。数值实验表明该方法在棱柱和着陆器系统等张力脊结构中具有良好的性能，展示了其在可扩展形态寻找和准确预测结构特性方面的潜力。

item.title: LLM生成的评估量规在论文复现中的元评估研究
item.content:encoded:
研究评估LLM生成的论文复现量规的有效性。



摘要： 本文首次系统地对基于大语言模型生成的论文复现评估量规进行了元评估。研究将量规转化为检查清单格式，并在两种基础模型上测试四种生成设置。通过语义相似性和与真实量规评分的一致性进行内在和外在评估，结果显示增强设置显著提高了下游评估的一致性，最强设置接近人类基准，但内在提升有限。分析还发现，生成的量规通常过于细致、偏向高分且对论文领域适应性差，揭示了其优势与局限。

item.title: AdaPCLA框架提升电子健康记录生成的罕见事件建模能力
item.content:encoded:
AdaPCLA提高EHR生成数据对罕见事件的建模精度。



摘要： 本文提出AdaPCLA框架，旨在解决传统自回归模型在生成电子健康记录（EHR）时对罕见事件（如疾病、症状）共现结构建模不足的问题。该框架通过数据分布感知的训练策略，使生成模型能够自适应地拟合和生成EHR数据，并支持零样本分布控制以适应多样化的临床人群。理论分析揭示了通过标签级经验NTK进行稀有代码logit更新的机制，并推导了退火速度和NTK条件对保留先验信号的影响。实验表明，AdaPCLA在真实数据集上显著提升了尾部事件的合理性、下游任务性能和零样本控制能力，优于现有方法。

item.title: 语言模型在非证据激励下的报告偏差与因果校正方法
item.content:encoded:
研究语言模型在压力下报告偏差，提出因果校正方法提升内部激励一致性。



摘要： 本文探讨了对齐语言模型在面对非证据性激励时的报告偏差问题，如盲目顺从用户或过度自信。作者将其视为内部激励不一致（IC）的失败，并提出一种学习和验证反事实报告中介的方法，使模型的报告符合因果契约：对禁止因素（如压力、声望）保持不变，对许可因素（如真实证据）作出响应。研究通过贝叶斯见证基准测试，识别出可独立控制的回答、置信度和警告坐标，并引入无需训练的反事实报告坐标夹具（CRC clamp）。实验结果显示，该方法在因果验证中表现优异，但部署版本存在信息损失。该机制在多个模型家族中有效，并迁移至自然谄媚基准测试。

item.title: The One-Word Census: Answer-Choice Conformity Across 44 Language Models
item.content:encoded: When a language model must pick one answer from a large space of equally valid options, which does it pick -- and how often is it the same answer every other model picks? Asked to "pick a word -- any word," 44 models chose "serendipity" 41% of the time. We characterize this convergence with a deliberately minimal instrument: 31 single-turn prompts, each naming a category with many valid one-word answers ("Name a tree."), asked four times per model with no system prompt. Analysis is exact-match on normalized tokens -- no embeddings, no judge -- at about a dollar per model. That models converge is well documented; our contribution is the instrument itself -- the One-Word Census -- and what it reveals about the structure of the convergence. We score each model by answer-choice surprisal: the average $-\log2$ probability of its answers under the pooled answers of all other models, leave-one-out. Convergence is extreme -- in 7 of 31 categories one answer takes over 80% of all answers -- yet conformity varies more than fourfold across models, and the variation is structured. Persona- and community-tuned models are the most divergent; the newest mainline flagships are the most conformist, producing almost no answer no other model gave. Within four lineages (Claude, GPT, Qwen, Grok) conformity rises with each generation -- but reverses for the latest flagship Claude and GPT models, a possible early signal of repositioning at the top tier. Rankings are robust to roster composition (leave-one-family-out rho = 0.985). Against human category-production norms, the field is more concentrated than people in 18 of 20 shared categories. All prompts, transcripts, and code are public.

item.title: Hy-Embodied-VLM-1.0：高效多模态具身基础模型
item.content:encoded:
提出一种高效具身基础模型，提升物理世界交互能力。



摘要： 本文介绍了 Hy-Embodied-VLM-1.0，一个专为具身智能体设计的高效且强大的基础模型。该模型通过定义以行动为中心的能力分类体系，包括状态理解、动作转换推理和序列适应推理，提升智能体在物理世界的感知与交互能力。基于 Hy3-A3B 和 Hy-ViT2 架构，结合专家混合结构实现高效推理。在 38 个基准测试中表现优异，优于多个竞品模型，尤其在长周期任务中表现出色。

item.title: 自进化代理系统中的度量演化与安全验证
item.content:encoded:
文章探讨了自进化代理系统中度量的演化及其安全性验证。



摘要： 文章提出了一种通过演化生成可靠度量的方法，以解决自进化代理系统在缺乏现有评估指标时的性能瓶颈。该方法通过组合小型缺陷检测器，在完整的进化生命周期中进行训练，确保与锚定参考集的一致性，并通过无标签输出的共识进行正则化，同时通过独立的锚点审计来提升透明度。实验表明，这种双轨机制在代码生成、文本到SQL转换和报告生成任务中，能够保留高达110%的性能提升。此外，文章强调了锚点约束和外部审计在保障系统安全方面的重要性。

item.title: Critic Experience Bank: Self-Evolving Step-Level Confidence Estimation for LLM Agents
item.content:encoded: LLM agents act in external environments where each action changes the state that later decisions condition on, and where a single wrong step can waste interaction budget or trigger irreversible side effects long before the final failure is observed. Reliable deployment therefore requires \emph{step-level confidence estimation}: a calibrated probability that each proposed action is productive, available \emph{before} the action is executed. Existing LLM confidence estimators are designed to score a response from the given prompt, but agent confidence also depends on execution consequences: whether similar actions in similar situations actually advanced the task after the environment responded. We introduce the \method (\methodshort), a self-evolving critic framework in which an LLM critic accumulates evidence from its own past judgments and their observed consequences. After each trajectory, a hindsight LLM that sees the full execution feedback votes on whether each step was productive. The resulting pseudo-labels populate a memory bank from which related productive and unproductive experiences are retrieved into the critic's prompt whenever a similar step recurs. \methodshort requires no training and uses no ground truth step labels. Across three agent benchmarks and three critic backbones, \methodshort attains the best calibration (ECE and Brier) and ranking (AUC) in every dataset--critic combination, reducing ECE by up to $54\%$ relative to the strongest training-free baseline.

item.title: 提升文本引导图像编辑中物体一致性的方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出FlowMirror方法提升图像编辑中的物体一致性。



摘要： 本文针对文本引导图像编辑中物体一致性不足的问题，提出了ABO-Edit数据集和FlowMirror方法。ABO-Edit包含12,000个高质量图像对，用于研究物体一致性。研究发现，图像编辑的修正流模型在高噪声下仍能预测最终生成图像。基于此，作者提出无需参数调整的辅助损失函数FlowMirror，有效提升了生成质量。

item.title: RL中异常检测框架OOD-RL-Bench的构建与评估
item.content:encoded:
提出OOD-RL-Bench框架用于评估RL中的异常检测方法。



摘要： 本文提出了一个名为OOD-RL-Bench的框架，用于评估强化学习（RL）中异常检测方法在轨迹数据上的表现。该框架通过注入不同类型的异常来测试检测器的性能，并支持新方法的快速集成。研究使用Deep Q-Network在LunarLander-v3环境中进行实验，评估了多种检测指标，发现不同异常类型对检测效果影响显著。部分异常如观测扰动和模式切换可被高精度识别，而观测延迟和动作依赖动态则仍具挑战性。所有代码、模型和结果均已公开，便于复现。

item.title: 基于精确数据一致性的有限角度乳腺断层成像重建方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种数据一致的扩散先验方法提升有限角度乳腺断层成像质量。



摘要： 本文研究了有限角度数字乳腺断层成像（DBT）中的图像重建问题，针对传统方法在数据不完整区域产生的结构缺失和不确定性问题，提出了一种基于精确数据一致性的条件扩散先验方法。该方法通过将每一步的近似更新替换为精确的欧几里得投影，实现了对测量数据的严格一致性约束，显著提升了图像质量。实验表明，该方法不仅提高了图像保真度，还有效校准了不确定性，使误差分布更符合临床需求。此外，该方法在患者衍生的乳腺体模中表现出良好的性能，且无需牺牲深度分辨率。最后，作者还修复了投影器中的20.3%伴随不匹配问题，为有限角度DBT提供了首个数据一致且不确定性校准的重建方案。

item.title: ChartGenEval：一种用于评估节奏游戏谱面生成的框架
item.content:encoded:
提出ChartGenEval框架评估节奏游戏谱面生成质量。



摘要： 文章介绍了一种名为ChartGenEval的六题评估框架，用于衡量节奏游戏谱面生成的质量。该框架通过测试不同失败情况来评估生成结果，而非依赖传统统计指标。实验表明，多个输出轴在多项测试中满足敏感性和不变性标准。此外，研究发现谱面整体相位估计和常见模式重写对模型性能有显著影响。ChartGenEval提供独立的角色特定信号，为生成器优化提供反馈。

item.title: 基于隐空间生成模型的gyrokinetic湍流稳态模拟
item.content:encoded:
提出GyroFlow模型直接生成gyrokinetic湍流稳态，提升计算效率。



摘要： 文章介绍了一种名为GyroFlow的隐空间生成模型，用于直接估计gyrokinetic湍流在5D相空间中的稳态统计特性，而无需模拟瞬态过程。该方法基于遍历性假设，通过从噪声中生成饱和快照来提高计算效率，并优于传统的自回归和降阶模型。研究还提出了FGyD分布度量来评估生成质量，并展示了其与通量精度和求解器收敛性的相关性。GyroFlow还可用于加速数值代码的初始化。

item.title: 改进的ATACOM-DC框架提升安全强化学习性能
item.content:encoded:
ATACOM-DC提升安全强化学习中的安全与性能平衡。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为ATACOM-DC的安全强化学习框架，用于改善机器人在开放环境中的安全性和任务性能。该方法通过引入方向性约束，区分接近和远离约束边界的行为，在必要时才激活安全机制，从而减少约束对学习效率的影响。实验表明，该方法在多个模拟机器人控制任务中有效降低了约束违反成本并提升了任务表现。相关代码和资料已公开。

item.title: 基于扩散模型的药物设计框架conDitar-dev提升结合亲和力与成药性
item.content:encoded:
conDitar-dev提升药物设计结合亲和力与成药性。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于扩散模型的结构导向药物设计框架conDitar-dev，用于生成具有强结合亲和力和良好成药性的配体。该框架包含三个模块：msPRL用于多尺度口袋表征学习，conDitar通过msPRL表征引导扩散模型生成分子，paOPT则在生成阶段优化药物开发属性。在人类疾病靶点的新基准测试中，conDitar-dev优于现有方法，平均结合得分达到-8.85 kcal/mol。在五个ADMET属性上，其性能相比conDitar提升了最高73%。该框架已成功应用于PD-L1和CSF1R等靶点，生成的分子经实验验证，展现出良好的生物活性和药物重定位潜力。

item.title: 基于量子计算的多模态适配器提升少样本学习性能
item.content:encoded:
MQAdapter通过量子机制提升视觉语言模型的细粒度分类能力。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于量子计算的多模态适配器（MQAdapter），用于改进视觉语言模型在少样本学习中的表现。该方法首先根据输入图像检索最相似的Top-K类别作为语义锚点，再通过跨模态量子学习机制，在这些锚点指导下优化视觉特征。MQAdapter将视觉和文本特征编码为量子态，利用高维希尔伯特空间中的量子纠缠与叠加，建模更复杂的跨模态交互，从而生成更具判别性的表示。实验表明，MQAdapter在15个数据集上表现出色，且参数效率高，可与多种现有微调算法结合进一步提升性能。

item.title: 基于ADMM的SBMPC学习加速算法研究
item.content:encoded:
本文提出一种加速SBMPC的ADMM算法，提升计算效率。



摘要： 本文针对场景模型预测控制（SBMPC）计算复杂度高的问题，提出一种结合ADMM和Moreau包络学习的加速算法。通过将SBMPC问题转化为一致性形式并利用ADMM分解，实现跨场景和时间步的并行更新。进一步引入学习优化方法加速ADMM的原始更新过程，显著降低计算时间。该框架在微电网能量管理问题中进行了验证，与IPOPT和MadNLP等求解器相比，展现出更高的计算效率和可靠的闭环控制性能。

item.title: 基于聚类加权的扩展动态模态分解方法研究
item.content:encoded:
CW-EDMD通过聚类优化Koopman算子，提升预测精度。



摘要： 本文提出了一种改进的扩展动态模态分解方法（CW-EDMD），该方法结合软相空间划分和每个聚类的EDMD算子，以提高不同状态空间区域中动态建模的准确性。通过期望最大化算法，根据几何接近性和预测残差分配每个转移，使聚类在局部Koopman模型准确的区域进行专业化，而非数据密集区域。实验表明，在Lorenz、阻尼摆和Duffing系统上，CW-EDMD在一步预测和5秒滚动预测中均优于传统EDMD方法，显著降低了误差。

item.title: LEO卫星系统中集成感知与通信的鲁棒波束成形算法研究
item.content:encoded:
研究LEO卫星系统中ISAC的鲁棒波束成形算法，提升资源利用率。



摘要： 随着卫星感知和通信需求的增长，传统无线资源难以支持多个卫星系统。本文提出在低地球轨道（LEO）卫星系统中实现集成感知与通信（ISAC），使单颗卫星同时执行多目标感知和多用户通信任务，节省频谱和轨道资源。针对卫星有限的机载能量，设计了一种鲁棒波束成形算法，旨在最小化总发射功率，同时满足感知的均方误差（MSE）和通信的信干噪比（SINR）要求，特别是在存在信道相位不确定性的情况下。理论分析和仿真结果表明，该算法有效且优于现有方法。

item.content:encoded:
提出MobileSAM2，实现移动端高效图像视频分割。

item.title: 面向地理空间推理的TerraLogic基准与HieraPlan工具增强代理
item.content:encoded:
TerraLogic推动地理空间推理研究，HieraPlan提升复杂分析能力。



摘要： 本文介绍了TerraLogic，一个用于地理空间推理的基准数据集，包含545个层次化任务，涵盖多种遥感影像类型。同时提出了HieraPlan，一种具备故障容忍和长期规划能力的工具增强代理，能够进行结构化抽象和跨模态推理。实验表明，现有方法在层次化地理空间推理中表现不佳，而HieraPlan提供了强大的基线，提升了推理、泛化能力和错误处理。相关数据集和代码已公开。

item.title: 基于因果推断与多实例学习的全切片图像分析方法
item.content:encoded:
因果推断MIL方法提升全切片图像诊断准确性。



摘要： 本文探讨了利用因果推断和多实例学习（MIL）分析全切片图像（WSI）在数字病理学中的应用。研究提出两个假设：1）因果推断MIL引入独立分类通道，有效完成WSI分类；2）新通道与基线通道特征差异越大，越能消除虚假关联。该方法通过叠加并行独立通道，提高深度特征多样性，从而消除WSI诊断与非诊断子图像之间的错误关联。实验在乳腺癌和非小细胞肺癌数据集上验证了这些假设，为因果推断在WSI分析中的应用提供了新的理论视角。

item.title: 基于单视角的皮肤3D重建与测量方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种无需额外设备的皮肤3D重建方法，提升皮肤病诊断精度。



摘要： 本文提出DermDepth，一种用于皮肤科领域的单视角度量尺度3D模型，并构建了首个具有像素级3D信息的合成皮肤数据集D-Synth。实验表明，使用D-Synth训练DermDepth可将真实皮肤图像的度量误差从16倍降低至1.1倍以下，同时保持几何质量并增强纹理细节。该方法在多种临床样本上进行微调后，可在不同尺寸、肤色和慢性伤口病例中实现与医学文献一致的测量结果，提升了皮肤病变监测和诊断的准确性。

item.title: CoRe：统一框架提升跨图像比较推理能力
item.content:encoded:
CoRe框架显著提升视觉语言模型的跨图像比较推理性能。



摘要： 本文提出CoRe，一个用于跨图像比较推理的统一框架。该框架包含三个核心部分：CoRe-20K数据集、TriSR结构化奖励机制和CoRe-Bench基准测试。CoRe-20K通过多专家协作从结构化视觉元数据中自动生成，涵盖计数、深度、距离和空间关系等属性。TriSR在GRPO优化下联合监督属性定位、判断对齐和三元组一致性。实验表明，CoRe在CoRe-Bench上表现优异，相比最强基线提升了28.2个百分点的局部准确率，同时在标准多模态基准上保持竞争力。

item.title: 互联网智能实体架构：IoAT的框架与应用
item.content:encoded:
提出IoAT架构，整合AI、物联网等技术实现智能协同控制。



摘要： 本文介绍了互联网智能实体（IoAT）架构，该框架将智能AI、物联网、网络物理系统、物理AI、边缘计算和数字孪生整合为一个统一的闭环协调系统。IoAT由云、边缘/雾和物理物联网层组成，通过自主AI代理在分布式环境中进行感知、推理、协调和执行。文章将IoAT形式化为一个耦合的工作流-控制问题，并采用一种基于hylomorphic动态规划的框架，将智能规划与物理执行相结合。以智能建筑协调为例，探讨了安全、安全、治理、弹性及可信部署等关键研究挑战。

item.title: 隐式数据同化与EnCF滤波方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出隐式数据同化方法，适用于复杂观测模型。



摘要： 本文介绍了隐式数据同化方法，通过能量倾斜定义分析法则，并提出EnCF滤波器，利用随机控制流实现更新。该方法通过伴随匹配学习观测依赖的控制，适用于模拟器定义的观测。在非高斯、多对一、多模态和隐式观测模型中表现优于传统卡尔曼滤波器。研究证明了方法的理想精确性，并分析了误差分解与局部误差不累积的条件。

item.title: 人工智能系统的透明性与人类判断的作用
item.content:encoded:
强调在AI系统中引入人类判断以应对透明度问题。



摘要： 文章探讨了AI系统在多个领域日益普及，但其核心功能往往不透明，导致用户难以理解输入与输出之间的关系。这种不透明性与自主性的结合引发了关于系统可靠性、可控性及伦理法律合规性的担忧。文章主张通过利用人类的实践判断、美德和直觉来缓解这些问题，认为这些人类特质虽不可量化，但在AI部署中具有重要价值。作者以军事领域为例，说明这些能力如何促进伦理和有效的决策，并指出这一观点适用于所有使用不透明和潜在自主系统的领域。
