item.title: 基于隐藏状态的LLM代理失败预测与计算节省方法
item.content:encoded:
通过分析隐藏状态提前预测LLM代理失败，显著节省推理计算。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于大型语言模型（LLM）代理内部表示的早期失败预测方法。研究发现，通过轻量级探针分析每轮隐藏激活状态，可以在第一轮交互中就预测出最终失败，而仅依赖代理行为的评分器效果接近随机。基于此信号，作者设计了一个无需分布假设的中止级联机制，通过联合搜索每轮召回预算，在保证用户指定全局成功率的前提下有效减少无效推理计算。实验表明，该方法在TextCraft任务中实现了90%至97%的召回率，并节省了47.1%和37.2%的推理资源。此外，研究还揭示了高召回目标的样本复杂度，为实践者提供数据支持的可行性判断。

item.title: AEGIS：动态追踪视觉同义攻击的生成时防御机制
item.content:encoded:
提出AEGIS防御机制，有效应对视觉同义攻击。



摘要： 本文针对文本到图像扩散模型在面对视觉同义攻击（VSA）时的安全漏洞进行研究。传统安全机制主要依赖于显式不安全概念的过滤，但无法有效应对通过隐式视觉关联引发的攻击。作者提出AEGIS，一种基于生成过程中不安全语义动态追踪的防御方法，通过识别并干预特定注意力头来抑制非法内容生成。实验表明，AEGIS在保持生成质量的同时显著提升了安全性，在多个基准测试中表现出色。

item.title: 双查询场景图生成方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出双查询场景图生成方法，提升预测效果。



摘要： 本文针对场景图生成方法中检测器驱动与查询驱动方法在预测行为上的差异进行系统分析。通过设计控制实验，发现两者具有互补性。基于此，提出Dual-SGG方法，通过双查询设计融合两种机制，从而提升预测性能。实验在Visual Genome、Open Images v6和GQA-200数据集上验证了该方法的有效性。

item.title: 基于MIIM假设的CPS异常检测方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出MIIM假设，提升CPS异常检测效果。



摘要： 本文针对工业控制系统（CPS）中异常检测的挑战，提出一种基于MIIM（Massive, Implicit, Imbalanced Multimodality）假设的检测方法。传统方法因正常行为的复杂性和稀有性难以建模，而该方法通过联合学习潜在表示和显式高斯混合聚类，在潜在空间中进行评分，避免依赖全局密度或重构残差。在三个真实CPS数据集（WADI、HAI、SKAB）上验证，该方法在整体和动态故障子集上均表现优异，AUROC分别达到0.831、0.726和0.610。实验还表明，该方法在多模态数据上效果更显著，优于USAD、TranAD和GDN等深度检测器。

item.title: 基于统一多模态生成的计算机视觉方法
item.content:encoded:
统一多模态生成提升计算机视觉任务处理能力。



摘要： 本文提出将计算机视觉任务统一为多模态生成问题，通过自然语言指令和可选视觉提示来定义任务，模型以文本、图像或混合形式输出结果。SenseNova-Vision通过大规模数据转换构建了涵盖多种视觉任务的语料库，并在预训练模型基础上进行训练，无需任务特定结构。实验表明，该模型在检测、OCR、分割等任务上表现优异，展示了统一多模态生成在整合视觉能力方面的潜力。

item.title: 神经网络与NTK极限在结构任务中的性能差异研究
item.content:encoded:
神经网络在结构任务中优于NTK极限，受傅里叶复杂度和架构复杂度影响。



摘要： 本文研究了神经网络在具有组合结构的任务中为何优于其神经切线核（NTK）极限。通过分析单位圆上的目标函数，作者区分了两种复杂度：傅里叶复杂度控制NTK核回归，而架构复杂度控制深度-L、宽度-w的ReLU网络的学习能力。研究揭示了架构类C_{L,w,R}的最小最大率，并发现当两种复杂度解耦时，NTK估计器的表现会显著低于最优下界。实验验证了理论结论，表明NTK在平滑目标上表现良好，但在稀疏奇偶性模型中，两层网络表现远超NTK。结果表明性能差距源于函数空间属性，而非通用核与网络现象。

item.title: 面向非编程用户的自然语言笔记本Plainbook介绍
item.content:encoded:
Plainbook让非编程用户也能进行数据计算与分析。



摘要： Jupyter Notebooks虽然广泛用于数据科学，但需要编程知识。为满足不熟悉代码的科学家需求，作者介绍了Plainbook，一种以自然语言为核心的笔记本系统。它通过自然语言描述生成代码，并采用线性执行机制确保执行顺序，同时提供基于值的验证机制，帮助用户验证计算结果。这些功能由快照内核支持，提高了执行和验证效率。

item.title: 基于物理信息神经网络的弹性波传播建模研究
item.content:encoded:
PINN框架有效模拟双材料弹性波传播。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于物理信息神经网络（PINN）的框架，用于模拟双材料系统中的瞬态弹性波传播。通过将线性弹性轴对称方程、初始条件、边界条件和界面条件直接嵌入损失函数，该方法能够准确预测波的透射与反射，并重现轴向和径向位移历史及应力应变演化。使用ANSYS Workbench进行高保真有限元仿真作为验证数据，训练后的网络可预测未见过的时间点和材料属性变化，展现出良好的泛化能力和计算效率。研究表明，PINN与显式有限元分析结合为非均质固体中的弹性波传播提供了一种高效且准确的代理模型。

item.title: 基于多级表征的细粒度食物图像检索方法RFHNet
item.content:encoded:
RFHNet提升细粒度食物图像检索性能。



摘要： 本文提出RFHNet，一种用于细粒度食物图像检索的级联分层哈希网络。该方法通过多级表征捕捉全局结构和局部细节，包含三个组件：细粒度关系建模（FRM）用于捕获相似食物成分的细微差异，多频调制融合（MFMF）提取多频特征，以及层次语义协同（HSS）生成判别哈希码。实验表明，RFHNet在六个食物基准数据集上均优于现有方法，mAP提升4.44%至17.20%。该研究为大规模视觉食物检索和智能餐饮应用提供了有效解决方案。

item.title: VaseMuseum：面向古希腊陶器的智能数字博物馆框架
item.content:encoded:
VaseMuseum提升古希腊陶器数字博物馆的可信度与准确性。



摘要： 本文提出VaseMuseum，一个轻量级、模块化的多模态代理框架，用于构建古希腊陶器的智能数字博物馆。该框架通过结合2D图像与3D文物的多模态感知、3D感知推理、外部知识检索和推理时的可靠性控制，解决传统视觉-语言模型在文化遗址领域中因证据不足或模糊而产生的错误回答问题。VaseAgent从权威来源检索证据，并通过源级控制选择多样且可验证的证据。同时，响应级控制确保生成的答案符合证据池，避免不支持的断言。实验表明，VaseMuseum提升了引用有效性，减少了知识密集型查询中的幻觉，并在模糊情况下产生更中立的回答。

item.title: RuBench 1.0：基于真实用户请求的代码修复基准测试
item.content:encoded:
RuBench 1.0评估代码修复代理在真实用户请求下的表现。



摘要： 本文介绍了RuBench 1.0，这是一个从五个活跃开源仓库中提取的25个任务组成的基准测试集，所有任务均以俄语原生编写，模拟实际用户请求。该基准测试不依赖英语任务描述，并通过上游维护者的回归测试进行评估。所有修复提交均发生在模型训练数据截止时间之后，确保无数据泄露。研究评估了多个产品级配置（如Claude Code和Codex CLI）的表现，结果显示最佳配置解决了78.7%的任务。此外，还发现产品在某些情况下会自动切换模型，表明实际测量的是产品而非模型本身。所有任务、元数据、代理轨迹和差异均已发布，但评分标准未公开。

item.title: 量子机器学习任务中的学习分离研究
item.content:encoded:
研究量子机器学习任务中的学习分离现象。



摘要： 本文探讨了量子计算机在模拟量子多体系统方面的优势，并提出了一个基于PAC学习框架的量子机器学习任务。该任务通过随机稳定态探测、演化时间以及可观测量期望值来训练模型，以学习未知哈密顿量。研究提出了一种高效的量子算法，在训练阶段学习哈密顿量，并在部署阶段结合哈密顿量模拟和经典阴影协议进行推理。同时，研究证明了该任务在经典计算下具有硬性，除非BQP包含于P/poly。此外，结果还展示了该任务在量子学习上的可解性，并讨论了其在辅助认证量子模拟中的应用。

item.title: Association Unlearning与功能快捷方式恢复测试研究
item.content:encoded:
研究提出ART测试以评估模型是否仍能利用已删除的关联特征。



摘要： 本文探讨了Association Unlearning方法，旨在消除模型中学习到的标签-属性快捷方式，同时保持任务性能。现有评估主要关注输出鲁棒性或冻结特征中的属性可读性，但未能检测原始分类器是否仍能使用这些关联。为此，作者提出了Association Restoration Test (ART)，通过估计类别条件下的关联方向、放大残留成分，并用原始分类器头评估修改后的特征，以诊断功能快捷方式的可恢复性。实验表明，输出指标、表示探测和ART分别反映了快捷方式缓解的不同方面，支持了面向关联而非单个类别的修复评估方法。

item.title: CurateEvo: Data-Curation Evolving for Agentic Post-Training
item.content:encoded: Large language model (LLM) agents require post-training methods that can improve long-horizon decision making from environment feedback. However, existing agentic post-training pipelines often treat data curation as a fixed preprocessing step, focusing mainly on data augmentation while neglecting filtering, refinement, and adaptation to downstream failures. We propose CurateEvo, a failure-driven dynamic evolution framework for agentic post-training data curation. CurateEvo represents the curation strategy as executable code and iteratively rewrites it using failed trajectories from a held-out development set. At each epoch, the evolved strategy transforms a fixed raw corpus into supervised fine-tuning data, reinforcement learning data, and an inference-time memory bank. The evolution process first improves effectiveness by diagnosing recurring failure modes and augmenting, filtering, or refining data accordingly, and then improves efficiency by pruning redundant or low-utility training turns under a cost-aware objective. Experiments on ACEBench-Agent, BFCL-V4, and τ^2-Bench under both labeled and wild-data settings show that CurateEvo consistently outperforms prior curation methods, improving average scores by 3.2 and 2.7 points, respectively. Further analyses demonstrate that CurateEvo is compatible with different post-training recipes and substantially reduces curation overhead.

item.title: Realistic Compound-Lens Defocus Blur Synthesis
item.content:encoded: Defocus blur degrades fine image structures and limits visual perception, which can adversely affect downstream vision tasks. Although recent deep learning deblurring methods have achieved strong performance, their effectiveness depends on training data and often degrades across cameras and lenses due to limited optical diversity and realism in existing datasets. In this paper, we propose a pipeline for synthesizing realistic defocus deblurring datasets for diverse compound lenses. It integrates efficient wave-optics PSF computation via Debye CZT propagation, depth-aware defocus rendering with occlusion handling, and blur synthesis in the radiometrically linear space with camera ISP simulation. This unified pipeline enables the scalable generation of photorealistic defocus datasets with diverse lens characteristics. Using our pipeline, we generate CLDefocus, a large-scale synthetic dataset containing lens-diverse defocus image pairs. We further analyze the limitations of real-captured defocus datasets and show that such imperfections can bias full-reference evaluation. Extensive experiments demonstrate that models trained on CLDefocus achieve improved cross-device generalization compared to models trained on existing real and synthetic datasets.

item.title: SpanUQ: Span-Level Uncertainty Quantification for Large Language Model Generation
item.content:encoded: Uncertainty estimation is essential not only for the trustworthy deployment of large language models (LLMs) but also as a foundation for self-refinement in LLM generation. However, existing approaches operate at suboptimal granularities: token-level scores lack semantic coherence, while sequence-level scores fail to localize errors. We formalize Span-Level Uncertainty Estimation (SLUE), a new task that targets the natural granularity for uncertainty: semantically coherent text spans, each conveying a single assessable unit of meaning. To address this task, we introduce SPANUQ, a lightweight probe that distills the uncertainty knowledge from expensive multi-sample inference into a single forward pass over LLM hidden states. SPANUQ employs a DETR-style span decoder to simultaneously detect spans and estimate their uncertainty via a Mixture of Beta distribution, trained with a principled combination of Beta NLL regression and contrastive ranking objectives. We construct SPANUQ-BENCH, the first span-level uncertainty benchmark comprising 20K prompts, 293K annotated spans, and continuous soft labels derived from multi-sample claim verification. Experiments on five LLM backbones show that SPANUQ consistently achieves the best span-level uncertainty quality, outperforming the strongest probe baseline and all sampling-based methods while being 10-20x faster. Its DETR-based span detector attains 0.910 F1, surpassing the best heuristic by 39.4%, enabling precise error localization that sequence-level methods cannot provide. The framework generalizes across five LLMs spanning two model families.

item.title: AbICL: In-Context Learning for Antigen-Specific Antibody Affinity Ranking
item.content:encoded: Accurate ranking of antibody candidates according to their binding affinity is essential for therapeutic antibody discovery. However, existing methods treat affinity comparisons independently and ignore the contextual information encoded in other labeled comparisons, limiting their ability to capture antigen-specific binding landscapes. For many target antigens, a small number of experimentally characterized affinity comparisons are often available. An important question is whether the model can exploit these existing comparisons to infer antigen-specific ranking patterns that facilitate subsequent affinity ranking. This form of learning from labeled demonstrations closely resembles the paradigm of In-Context Learning, motivating us to revisit antibody affinity ranking from an ICL perspective. To this end, we propose AbICL, an ICL framework for antigen-specific antibody affinity ranking. AbICL combines a pretrained structural encoder with a context ranking head and is trained with an episodic meta-training strategy that enables the model to leverage support demonstrations for test-time adaptation without gradient updates. Experiments on the AbRank benchmark demonstrate that AbICL consistently outperforms existing ranking baselines across almost all data splits and evaluation benchmarks. Further analysis shows that the value of contextual demonstrations depends on how well they match the target inference task, and becomes increasingly pronounced under distribution shift and fine-grained affinity discrimination. These findings highlight the potential of ICL as an effective paradigm for antigen-specific antibody affinity ranking, particularly in challenging settings where a single global ranking function is insufficient.

item.title: OBBSeg: Irregular Lesion Segmentation under Oriented Bounding Box Annotations
item.content:encoded: Pixel-level annotation remains a major bottleneck in medical image segmentation, making weak supervision an attractive yet under-constrained alternative. We propose OBBSeg, an intermediate supervision paradigm guided by Oriented Bounding Boxes (OBBs) that bridges the gap between full and weak supervision. By jointly encoding spatial extent and orientation, OBBs provide compact geometric supervision that better aligns with elongated or anisotropic lesions, reducing the ambiguity of coarse box annotations. To mitigate the inherent rectangular bias of OBBs, we introduce a Mask-to-OBB loss, a differentiable formulation that enforces geometric consistency between predicted masks and OBB regions. Furthermore, we incorporate prompt-driven semantic guidance through two complementary modules-PAFE and DBFE-which enhance foreground representation and suppress background interference. Extensive experiments on 13 datasets across 5 imaging modalities show that OBBSeg not only outperforms existing weakly supervised methods but also achieves performance comparable to fully supervised approaches, demonstrating its potential for efficient and scalable medical image segmentation. The code is available at https://github.com/StarLxc3/OBBSeg.

item.title: Token-Based Dual-view Fusion and Adaptation of Large Vision Models for Breast Cancer Classification
item.content:encoded: Accurate breast cancer classification from mammography requires effective integration of complementary information from craniocaudal (CC) and mediolateral oblique (MLO) views, which provide a more complete characterization of breast abnormalities. However, existing multi-view learning approaches typically rely on feature-level aggregation or single-stage cross-attention, which can entangle view-specific and shared representations and restrict interaction to limited network depths. To address these limitations, we propose a token-centric dual-view learning framework that unifies prompt-based adaptation and cross-view fusion within a frozen vision transformer backbone. The framework reformulates inter-view interaction as structured token-level communication, where dedicated fusion tokens explicitly encode bidirectional information exchange between CC and MLO views via cross-attention, serving as intermediate carriers of cross-view dependencies rather than relying on direct feature fusion. Unlike conventional methods that apply fusion at a single layer, fusion modules are inserted at multiple transformer depths, enabling progressive and repeated interaction across the encoder hierarchy. Fusion tokens are reintegrated into the token sequence and refined by subsequent transformer layers, facilitating hierarchical propagation of complementary information while preserving view-specific structure. Experiments on VinDr-Mammo and CMMD datasets demonstrate consistent improvements over linear probing, prompt-only adaptation, and conventional fusion baselines. On the VinDr-Mammo BI-RADS classification task, the framework achieves 50.40% F1-score and 0.8090 AUC, including a 0.10 AUC improvement over a dual-view fusion baseline in the binary setting. Ablation studies further validate the effectiveness of token-based fusion and multi-depth interaction design.

item.title: UI2App: Benchmarking Visual Interaction Inference in Executable Web Application Generation
item.content:encoded: Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated growing competence in web page generation. However, existing text-driven approaches rely on complex prompts that impose substantial demands on users and offer limited expressivity for page layout and cross-page visual coherence. Image-driven paradigms, which take UI screenshots as input, align more closely with real development workflows. However, current benchmarks focus primarily on visual fidelity and lack a systematic evaluation of the interaction capabilities in generated artifacts. To address this gap, we introduce UI2App, the first benchmark targeting interaction inference, the ability to recover application behavior from screenshots alone, without any textual or behavioral guidance. UI2App comprises 327 screenshots grouped into 45 state-coherent screenshot sets for runnable multi-route web applications. We design an end-to-end pipeline that evaluates each artifact along four dimensions: executability, navigation reachability, visual fidelity, and interaction inference. The interaction metric (IIS) assesses inferred interactions by functional correctness and state-management complexity, crediting any valid implementation rather than matching a single reference. Experiments on six frontier vision-language models reveal a marked capability mismatch between visual reconstruction and interaction realization: the visual-fidelity leader scores only 7.5 on IIS, ranking fourth and trailing the IIS leader by 5.2x. High-complexity interactions such as cross-page state remain a pervasive bottleneck, with half of the evaluated models scoring exactly zero on this dimension. Overall, the results indicate that inferring complete interaction behavior from static screenshots remains a key challenge for models.

item.title: MAC-XA: Multi-view Anatomy-Correspondence Fusion for Coronary Stenosis Reporting from X-ray Angiography
item.content:encoded: Multi-view reasoning in coronary X-ray angiography is inherently a cross-projection geometric problem, yet automated report generation in this setting remains largely unexplored. The 3D vascular topology leads to projection-dependent branch overlap and foreshortening, rendering single-view modeling fundamentally incomplete and unstable for lesion localization and stenosis grading. Although multi-view fusion appears promising, learning anatomically consistent fusion from real angiograms is impeded by a critical limitation: cross-view alignment is unobservable and cannot be explicitly supervised. Consequently, conventional fusion relies on implicit correlations rather than verified anatomical correspondence. We address this by reformulating multi-view stenosis reporting as an alignment-constrained aggregation problem. A controllable synthetic angiography generation strategy is introduced to expose geometry-derived patch-level correspondence supervision unavailable in real data. An anatomy-correspondence module learns cross-view correspondence matrices that explicitly align auxiliary features within the main-view coordinate space prior to fusion, thereby constraining evidence aggregation to anatomically consistent regions. Experiments on synthetic data and zero-shot transfer to real angiograms show that this alignment-constrained design improves correspondence consistency and structured stenosis reporting compared to single-view modeling and conventional multi-view fusion methods. The code will be publicly available upon publication.

item.title: Auto-DSM Under the Lens: A Black-Box Evaluation Framework for LLM-Based DSM Generation
item.content:encoded: This paper presents a black-box evaluation framework to systematically assess the ability of Large Language Models (LLMs) to generate Design Structure Matrices (DSMs) from structured technical documentation. Motivated by the closed-source nature of current Auto-DSM pipelines, the framework introduces a reproducible methodology that benchmarks generated DSMs (GEN-DSMs) against manually validated ground-truth matrices (GT-DSMs). The evaluation integrates both single-run and multi-run perspectives, combining structural metrics (Completeness, Correctness, Coupling Density), classification metrics (Selective Accuracy, Abstention Coverage), and stability measures (Entropy, Fleiss' $κ$). To synthesize these aspects, a Composite Quality Score (Q) is proposed. Controlled experiments are conducted on two datasets: a fictive abstract system and a real-world refrigerator decomposition, covering variations in phrasing, parameter-dataset alignment, and system complexity. Results show that LLMs can produce structurally plausible DSMs and achieve high reproducibility under well-structured inputs, but remain sensitive to ambiguity, inconsistent dependency definitions, and prompt formulation. The findings highlight systematic sources of hallucination and abstention failure, demonstrating both the potential and current limitations of LLM-driven DSM automation. The proposed framework provides a transparent benchmark for auditing Auto-DSM pipelines and establishes foundations for integrating LLM-based decomposition methods into model-based systems engineering (MBSE) workflows.

item.title: Two Sides of the Same Coin: Learning the Backdoor to Remove the Backdoor
item.content:encoded: The community has recently developed various training-time defenses to counter neural backdoors introduced through data poisoning. In light of the observation that a model learns poisonous samples responsible for the backdoor easier than benign samples, these approaches either use a fixed threshold of the training loss for splitting or iteratively learn a reference model as an oracle for identifying benign samples. In particular, the latter has proven effective for anti-backdoor learning. Our method, HARVEY, leverages a similar yet crucially different technique: learning an oracle for poisonous rather than benign samples. Learning a backdoored reference model is significantly easier than learning a reference model on benign data. Consequently, we can identify poisonous samples much more accurately than related work identifies benign samples. This crucial difference enables near-perfect backdoor removal as we demonstrate in our evaluation. HARVEY substantially outperforms related approaches across attack types, datasets, and architectures, lowering the attack success rate to the very minimum at a negligible loss in natural accuracy. The figure below shows an overview of our methods working principle.

item.title: Designing Maintainable Hybrid Generative Systems: A Quantum-Inspired Approach to Automated Music Harmony Generation
item.content:encoded: This paper presents the design and evaluation of a maintainable hybrid generative architecture for automated music harmony generation from melody. The proposed system combines quantum-inspired candidate exploration over overlapping melodic contexts with explicit rule-based optimization to balance generative flexibility and structural control. The architecture is evaluated using explicit and reproducible metrics covering structural coherence, functional agreement, harmonic similarity, and robustness. The results show that the proposed approach produces harmonizations that preserve tonal structure and cadential behavior while allowing multiple valid harmonic realizations. Furthermore, the optimization layer improves structural coherence, stability, and predictability without requiring a training corpus. The study demonstrates that transparent and controllable hybrid generative systems can be systematically designed and evaluated within the context of Information Systems Development.

item.title: Graph Convolutional Attention: A Spectral Perspective on Graph Denoising and Diffusion
item.content:encoded: Denoising graphs is a fundamental problem in graph learning and the core operation of graph diffusion models. Attention-based architectures like graph transformers have recently shown promise in denoising graphs. However, our principled understanding of attention-based graph denoising remains limited, making it unclear whether standard attention is the right mechanism for this task. Here we show that, under a denoising objective, linear attention is suboptimal and can only learn an average spectral denoising filter over the training distribution. This creates a fundamental limitation as graphs often vary spectrally across the distribution. To overcome this limitation, we introduce Spectral Attention, which directly utilizes the input graph spectrum and provably outperforms linear attention by a margin governed by the spectral diversity of the distribution. We then derive Graph Convolutional Attention (GCA), a practical and permutation-equivariant realization of this idea that implements spectral denoising through graph-filtered queries and keys. For stochastic block models, GCA provably matches the idealized Spectral Attention mechanism. We further show that the softmax operation, that follows the attention, provides additional denoising by approximately projecting noisy eigenvectors onto the clean eigenspace. Empirically, replacing linear attention with GCA consistently improves graph denoising and diffusion on synthetic and real datasets, with gains strongly correlated with spectral diversity. In DiGress, GCA matches standard graph-transformer performance without computing expensive structural features, and when combined with the recently proposed PEARL positional encodings, avoids explicit eigendecomposition computations resulting in faster inference without degrading quality. The code can be found here: github.com/shervinkhalafi/graph_conv_att

item.title: GaussFusion: Towards Multimodal 3D Gaussian Pretraining
item.content:encoded: 3D Gaussian Splatting provides an explicit representation that jointly models geometry and appearance, serving as a scalable foundation for 3D representation learning. Existing pre-training methods for Gaussian representations, such as masked Gaussian reconstruction, primarily capture local structures but offer limited semantic supervision. In this paper, we propose GaussFusion, a multimodal pre-training framework for 3D Gaussian representations. GaussFusion integrates image and text supervision into masked Gaussian modeling through cross-modal semantic alignment, enabling the Gaussian encoder to learn both visual and language-level semantic information during pre-training. To better adapt masked modeling to the non-uniform distribution of Gaussian primitives, we further propose Gaussian Salience-guided Multi-scale Hole Masking (GSHM). GSHM constructs spatially continuous masked regions based on Gaussian salience. By applying hole masks at multiple scales, GSHM encourages the encoder to capture both fine-grained local patterns and broader structural dependencies. Extensive experiments on downstream tasks demonstrate that GaussFusion improves the transferability of Gaussian representations. Notably, GaussFusion outperforms Gaussian-MAE on ModelNet40 and ScanObjectNN (PB-T50-RS) by 0.61\% and 3.85\%, respectively.

item.title: Umm... With Transformers? Insights from Filled Pause Use across Four Slavic Parliaments
item.content:encoded: Filled pauses (FPs) are a universal feature of spontaneous speech, yet most studies rely on small, single-language corpora, limiting the generalisability of their findings. We analyse ~4,000 hours of parliamentary speech across four related Slavic languages (Croatian, Czech, Polish, Serbian). FP occurrence is obtained via transformer-based automatic detection, while FP rate is modelled using Generalised Estimating Equations (GEE) with Mundlak correction to distinguish within- from between- speaker effects. We replicate a negative association of age and speech rate with FP rate, but find that gender effects are language-specific and directionally opposite to most prior literature. Novel analyses of sentiment, political orientation, and power status reveal a consistent positive association between sentiment and FP rate, alongside parliament-specific modulation by orientation and power status, with opposition speakers tending toward lower FP rates than governing coalition speakers.

item.title: CoPiT: Cognitive Pivot Translation for Digraphic Low-Resource Mongolian in the Traditional Script
item.content:encoded: Low-resource languages remain challenging for machine translation, and Mongolian is a representative case. As a digraphic language, Mongolian is written in both Cyrillic and Traditional scripts, which exhibit a severe imbalance in data availability. While the Cyrillic script is relatively well-resourced, the Traditional script remains extremely data-scarce and orthographically ambiguous, leading to substantial performance degradation in direct translation. We propose CoPiT, a cognitively motivated pivot-based translation pipeline that exploits this internal resource hierarchy by routing translation through the Cyrillic script. The pipeline explicitly resolves script-induced ambiguity in the Traditional script before translation, enabling more stable and accurate meaning transfer. Across multiple backbone models and target languages, CoPiT consistently outperforms direct translation, achieving substantial absolute BLEU improvements together with consistent 1.5-1.6x COMET gains. These gains allow strong open-source models to match or outperform GPT-4.1 under comparable evaluation settings. Beyond inference-time improvements, CoPiT enables the construction of synthetic parallel data directly from Traditional-script text, mitigating data scarcity in realistic low-resource scenarios. We release a new multi-script parallel dataset covering Mongolian in both scripts alongside English, Korean, and Russian. All datasets and code are publicly available at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/anonymous_project-76C7.

item.title: A Definition and Roadmap for World Models
item.content:encoded: World models -- internal simulators that learn the structure and dynamics of an environment -- have become one of the most actively debated concepts in AI. From model-based reinforcement learning and video generation to embodied robotics and ultimately, physical AI, researchers across AI subfields are building systems that they call "world models", yet there is no consensus on what a world model fundamentally is, what it should predict, or how it should be built. This perspective article provides a scientific definition of world models, discussions of their key technical aspects, and a staged roadmap for developing effective world models.

item.title: FADRA: Frequency-Aware Diffusion with Residual Adaptation for Video Face Restoration
item.content:encoded: Video face restoration (VFR) aims to recover high-quality and temporally consistent facial details from severely degraded video sequences; however, existing methods still struggle to balance spatial fidelity and temporal coherence under complex degradations. To address this, we propose FADRA, a frequency-aware diffusion framework with iterative residual adaptation specifically tailored for robust VFR. We first leverage the strong temporal consistency of a pre-trained text-to-video diffusion model and introduce lightweight LoRA adapters together with a Low-Quality (LQ) Pixel-Alignment Feature Fusion module to efficiently adapt the frozen generative prior to the VFR task. To further adapt the frozen diffusion backbone to the downstream VFR task beyond LoRA-based adaptation, we introduce a Repeated Residual Adaptation Head (RRAH) for step-wise residual refinement after the diffusion backbone. To make this refinement explicitly guided by the degraded observation, RRAH further takes the LQ latent together with the current velocity prediction as input, allowing the model to repeatedly revisit LQ cues and predict residual updates at each flow-matching step. This LQ-guided repeated residual adaptation helps recover fine facial details while preserving the inherent temporal priors of the pre-trained model. Furthermore, to ensure the structural integrity of perceptually important details, we introduce a Frequency-Aware Loss that provides explicit supervision across multiple spectral bands, emphasizing visually sensitive frequency components that are crucial for perceptual quality and prone to temporal jittering. Extensive experiments demonstrate that FADRA recovers better facial structures and produces more temporally consistent videos than state-of-the-art methods, leading to clear gains in both quantitative metrics and visual perception.

item.title: From RGB Generation to Dense Field Readout: Pixel-Space Dense Prediction with Text-to-Image Models
item.content:encoded: Large-scale text-to-image models are attractive backbones for dense prediction because RGB generation pretraining learns rich semantic, structural, and geometric priors. Existing generative and editing approaches reuse these priors by casting dense prediction as target generation: annotations such as depth, normals, alpha mattes, masks, and heatmaps are encoded into an RGB-trained VAE latent space and decoded back as image-like targets. We argue this inherits more of the generative output interface than dense prediction requires: unlike RGB synthesis, dense prediction asks for pixel-correct, task-native fields on the same image plane, not new RGB content to be rendered. Our key observation is that a pretrained DiT already organizes RGB inputs through a patch-to-token-to-patch lattice on the image plane, so each token indexes a fixed output patch whose channels can carry task-native quantities instead of RGB appearance. We instantiate this as ReChannel: we keep the VAE encoder for the DiT's input distribution but drop the target-side decoder, adapt the frozen DiT with task LoRA, and map each token to its p x p x K_t pixel-space patch through a shared token-local linear head--about 33K parameters, no spatial mixing. Using FLUX-Klein, we evaluate on six dense prediction tasks and over a dozen benchmarks. This minimal interface sets new state-of-the-art on trimap-free matting, KITTI depth, and referring segmentation, and stays competitive on normals, saliency, and pose. In a matched 4B setting it is more accurate and 2.48x faster than an edit-plus-latent-decode counterpart--dense perception can benefit from generative pretraining without inheriting its output interface.

item.title: WING: A Window-Prior-Based Generative Network with Gated Inception for Cross-Modality CT Synthesis
item.content:encoded: Generating CT volumes from MRI and CBCT can improve treatment planning in adaptive radiotherapy while avoiding additional radiation exposure. However, direct regression of CT intensities is challenged by the inherently high dynamic range and long-tailed distributions, thereby averaging out sparse yet clinically important structures. To alleviate this issue, we reformulate the regression target into multiple windowed representations, leveraging the inductive prior that CT intensities are structure-deterministic and window-separable. These windowed views exhibit smoother distributions and admit structured fusion back to the full-range CT. Building on this reformulation, we introduce WING, a WINdow-prior-based Generative network comprising: 1) a new Gated Inception Generator to produce multi-window predictions, enabling multi-shape kernel interactions to capture cross-modality correspondence; 2) a Fuse-and-Refine Transformer to aggregate the windowed outputs and learn residuals for detail refinement; and 3) a joint adversarial training objective to enhance window-conditioned realism. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our compact WING achieves state-of-the-art performance on the MRI-to-CT and CBCT-to-CT benchmarks, while supporting multi-anatomy synthesis with a single model.

item.title: High-Resolution Artwork Outpainting with Global Blueprint Guidance and Layout Control
item.content:encoded: Image outpainting extends an image beyond its original borders, requiring seamless style integration and globally coherent scene completion. Building on the success of diffusion models, recent methods have achieved substantial improvements in visual quality. In practice, however, high-resolution outpainting is commonly performed via progressive expansion around a fixed source image, particularly in artwork scenarios. Despite this progress, existing approaches still suffer from three key limitations: (i) the absence of a reliable global planning mechanism, which leads to structural instability and error accumulation at high resolutions; (ii) limited spatial controllability beyond text prompts, making it difficult to place objects at user-specified locations; and (iii) high inference latency caused by inherently sequential patch generation. To address these issues, we propose a global blueprint-guided two-stage diffusion framework for layout-controllable high-resolution outpainting with efficient parallel synthesis. In Stage 1, we generate a low-resolution global blueprint using a layout adapter that injects bounding-box conditions into a Stable Diffusion inpainting backbone, producing a globally consistent structural plan while extracting global guidance features. In Stage 2, we synthesize high-resolution local patches in parallel by injecting the blueprint-derived global guidance and initializing each patch from the blueprint using the low-frequency preservation property of forward diffusion. This design eliminates sequential dependency while maintaining global coherence. Extensive experiments on large-scale artwork datasets demonstrate improved visual fidelity, stronger semantic consistency, and substantially reduced inference time compared to prior baselines, while uniquely supporting explicit layout control for artwork outpainting.

item.title: SaMer：一种基于对象感知的多向量视觉语言检索压缩框架
item.content:encoded:
SaMer通过对象感知合并提升多向量检索效率与精度。



摘要： 本文提出SaMer，一种用于多向量视觉语言检索的高效图像侧令牌压缩框架。SaMer在不损失关键对象和区域信息的前提下，将图像侧令牌压缩为K个代表性中心点，从而显著降低存储和计算成本。该方法仅在训练阶段使用对象标注作为合并先验，推理时无需真实边界框或检测器，并且只微调共享投影层。实验表明，当K=64时，SaMer可减少93%以上的图像侧令牌，存储成本降低16.09倍，同时提升了Flickr30K和MSCOCO数据集上的R@1指标。其优势在于保留了查询选择所需的对象证据，优于传统压缩方法。

item.title: 基于3D数据的无监督像素级语义左右预测方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种无监督方法实现图像像素级语义左右预测。



摘要： 本文针对真实场景图像中像素级语义左右预测的挑战，提出了一种无监督学习框架。该方法结合3D形状数据和真实图像数据，提升单视角图像的语义左右预测效果。实验表明，使用中等规模的3D形状数据集与多样化的真实图像数据，即使面对全新3D物体类别如汽车或火车，也能实现高质量的预测结果。相比现有方法，该方法在渲染图像和真实图像数据集上均表现出色。

item.title: 基于归一化熵的轨迹感知强化学习方法
item.content:encoded:
提出STAPO框架，提升长序列任务中轨迹感知能力。



摘要： 本文针对强化学习在长序列任务中因稀疏奖励导致的轨迹忽视问题，提出了一种基于归一化熵的轨迹感知强化学习方法STAPO。该方法通过衡量代理在特定状态下的行为偏离度，增强对低质量动作与轨迹忽视的识别，并结合轨迹感知奖励和独立惩罚机制进行优化，有效提升了轨迹意识并保持训练稳定性。实验表明，STAPO在多个基准任务中表现优异，验证了其在智能体任务中的有效性与鲁棒性。

item.title: CARE：一种无需训练的概念擦除方法
item.content:encoded:
CARE实现精准概念擦除，减少对非目标概念的干扰。



摘要： 本文提出CARE，一种无需模型微调的闭式概念擦除算子。它通过一个小的保留概念锚点库计算保持子空间感知的方向，替代原始目标方向，在交叉注意力值空间中直接应用编辑。该方法仅需一个收缩参数控制擦除与保留的平衡，并具有最小扰动解释。实验表明，CARE在标准概念擦除协议下，能更准确地保留非目标概念，同时在实例、风格和名人概念上保持竞争力。代码已开源。

item.title: 基于大语言模型的疾病严重程度表型框架MOSAIC研究
item.content:encoded:
MOSAIC利用大语言模型生成临床有意义的疾病严重程度表型。



摘要： 本文提出MOSAIC框架，用于通过大语言模型从电子健康记录中生成疾病严重程度表型。以2型糖尿病为案例，MOSAIC在合成数据集上评估，与多个算法基准进行比较。结果显示，该框架能够涵盖传统方法未涉及的领域，如基于生物标志物的血糖分期和健康社会决定因素。MOSAIC在开放权重和专有管道之间表现出良好一致性，并与多个基准达成中等至良好相关性。此外，基于代理的分类方法在全因死亡率和新发并发症方面表现出显著差异，表明其超越固定规则的推理能力。研究证明了大语言模型在生成临床有用表型方面的潜力，未来可扩展至其他多维严重程度的疾病。

item.title: SAYRE：一种用于提升多模态文档信息抽取的场景感知合成框架
item.content:encoded:
SAYRE通过生成合成数据提升多模态KIE性能，适用于本地部署模型。



摘要： 本文提出SAYRE，一个场景感知的文档合成框架，用于生成可扩展的KIE训练数据，无需人工设计模板。SAYRE通过捕捉文档内容模式和布局规范，生成文档-模式-标注三元组，并引入错误驱动生成以增强训练样本。实验表明，SAYRE在受限和开放类别KIE任务中均优于现有方法，尤其对小模型和开放类别表现显著提升。合成数据的增加进一步提升了整体性能，减少了字段级错误，验证了该方法在多模态KIE中的有效性。

item.title: 基于模型合并的WSI生存分析持续学习框架
item.content:encoded:
MergeSurv提升WSI生存分析的持续学习效果。



摘要： 本文提出MergeSurv，一种用于全切片图像（WSI）生存分析的基于模型合并的持续学习框架。该方法通过在每个任务上独立微调病理视觉-语言基础模型，并将参数顺序合并到统一模型中，无需存储先前训练数据。研究还比较了两种推理策略：One-for-All（OFA）和Voting-Expert Aggregation（VEA）。实验在四个TCGA队列上验证了MergeSurv的有效性，结果表明其优于传统微调及代表性正则化和重放方法，同时有效减少了灾难性遗忘。研究显示模型合并是计算病理学中可扩展且隐私保护的持续学习有前景的方向。

item.title: AssemCAD：基于公理的文本到CAD装配生成框架
item.content:encoded:
AssemCAD通过公理化装配规范实现高效机械装配生成。



摘要： AssemCAD是一种基于公理的框架，用于从自然语言生成生产级CAD装配。与传统的单个零件生成不同，AssemCAD通过构建包含类型化部件、几何端口、可执行配合关系和工程公理的装配规范，实现了对多组件协调推理的支持。该框架还引入了基于端口和配合的CAD装配库，能够通过确定性配合变换执行符号装配关系，并利用具体的B-Rep几何证据验证接口。此外，AssemCAD支持按需合成可重用的参数化组件工厂，适用于标准和开放世界几何。实验表明，AssemCAD在装配保持率和物理有效性方面优于代码中心的CAD生成基线。

item.title: 2026 IEEE ICME工业缺陷检测与严重程度分级挑战赛
item.content:encoded:
2026年IEEE ICME挑战赛聚焦跨场景缺陷检测与精细严重度分级。



摘要： 本文介绍了IEEE国际多媒体会议（ICME）2026年的Grand Challenge，旨在解决工业缺陷检测系统在跨场景部署时性能下降以及缺乏严重度评估的问题。挑战分为两个赛道：Track 1关注跨场景的缺陷检测与分类，Track 2要求对检测到的缺陷进行精细的严重度分级。该挑战构建了一个包含3,800多张高分辨率显微图像的大规模工业数据集，涵盖七种缺陷类别，并吸引了86个参赛团队提交130份方案。最终有21个团队提交结果，12个团队提供模型和报告，推动了工业缺陷分析研究的新标准。

item.title: 大语言模型内部对生成长度的估计能力研究
item.content:encoded:
研究发现大语言模型能预测生成文本长度。



摘要： 该研究探讨了大语言模型是否具备对生成文本长度的内部估计能力。通过在三个7-8B参数的开放权重模型上训练线性探测器，研究人员发现模型的响应长度可以从提示的最后一个隐藏状态中线性解码出来。此外，自然语言数据集上的探测方向可以广泛迁移至合成数据集，并优于统计基线。在高损失完成任务中，探测器在模型重新开始部分解决方案时会调整其位置估计，表明模型可能具有类似计划的输出长度内部表示。

item.title: HunyuanOCR-1.5：轻量级端到端OCR视觉语言模型的优化与提升
item.content:encoded:
HunyuanOCR-1.5优化了OCR性能，提升推理速度与多任务处理能力。



摘要： HunyuanOCR-1.5是一款轻量级端到端OCR专用视觉语言模型，整合了文档解析、文本检测、信息提取等任务。基于前代模型HunyuanOCR-1.0的架构，通过引入DFlash技术显著提升推理效率，实现6.37倍Transformer推理加速。同时，提出Agentic Data Flow系统，增强长尾任务能力，如古文字OCR、图表解析、多图文本问答等。该模型在OmniDocBench v1.6基准中表现优异，并扩展了高分辨率、长上下文和多任务场景的应用能力。实验表明其具备更快的推理速度和更广的OCR覆盖范围，适用于实际应用。

item.title: PAST-TIDE：基于语义掩码语言模型的立场检测系统
item.content:encoded:
PAST-TIDE通过语义掩码语言模型实现高效立场检测，取得优异成绩。



摘要： PAST-TIDE 是一种用于处理 StanceNakba 共享任务的立场检测系统，该系统通过重新定义立场为掩码语言模型（MLM）任务，利用可学习的标签映射器进行分类，而非传统分类头。同时结合原型对比学习和主题条件层归一化，提升跨主题阿拉伯语立场检测效果。在官方排行榜上，PAST-TIDE 在子任务 A 和 B 上分别获得 0.75 和 0.74 的宏 F1 分数，表明在低资源环境下，对预训练模型进行最小修改仍可保持竞争力。

item.title: 结构化演示收集提升VLA模型的机器人操作能力
item.content:encoded:
结构化演示提升VLA模型在机器人操作中的表现。



摘要： 本文探讨了视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型在机器人操作中的应用，指出演示数据的组织方式对学习效率和泛化能力有重要影响。作者提出了一种从简单到复杂的结构化演示收集策略，通过分解任务、标准化环境和按复杂度排序演示，提升了VLA模型的学习效果。实验结果显示，该方法在块抓取与折叠任务中显著提高了成功率和训练稳定性，为高效技能获取和长时序操作提供了新思路。

item.title: Dashboard2Code：交互式数据看板生成的新任务与评估基准
item.content:encoded:
提出Dashboard2Code任务及评估框架，推动交互式数据看板生成研究。



摘要： 本文介绍了Dashboard2Code任务，旨在让模型通过交互探索生成可复现的交互式数据看板。为支持全面评估，作者构建了DashboardMimic基准，包含180个精心设计的看板-代码对，涵盖多种交互模式和难度级别。同时提出自动化评估框架，结合代码语义分析与动态交互测试，验证了模型在视觉和交互一致性上的表现。实验表明，现有模型在高复杂度看板上仍存在显著性能差距。

item.title: 基于大语言模型的脑电癫痫波自动检测方法研究
item.content:encoded:
利用大语言模型生成特征，提升脑电癫痫波检测准确率。



摘要： 本文提出EEG-SpikeAgent，一种基于大语言模型的闭环程序合成框架，用于生成用于脑电图（EEG）中癫痫波检测的信号处理特征。该系统通过迭代生成和优化特征模块，结合分类器评估性能，并反馈诊断信息进行模型优化。在VEPISET数据集上的实验表明，该方法在五折交叉验证中取得了较高的AUC值（0.935）、平衡准确率（0.699）和特异性（0.996）。此外，考虑伪影的特征生成方法进一步提升了模型性能，证明了LLM在可审计、可检查的代码驱动特征工程中的应用潜力。

item.title: 基于选择性重要性采样的强化学习后训练方法
item.content:encoded:
SIS通过减少离策略数据方差提升LLM强化学习效果。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为选择性重要性采样（SIS）的强化学习后训练方法，用于解决大型语言模型在后训练过程中因离策略数据导致的方差问题。SIS通过将离策略token转换为近似在线策略token，避免了传统重要性采样中因长序列导致的方差爆炸问题。该方法理论上能够缩小token级与序列级离策略梯度估计器之间的差距，并且仅需修改策略损失中的重要性比例，计算开销极小，可广泛应用于多种RL后训练算法。实验表明，SIS在密集型和MoE架构的LLM上均能提升多个指标表现，并增强了对离策略数据的鲁棒性。

item.title: 人工智能在精子分析中的应用与挑战
item.content:encoded:
AI提升精子分析准确性与可重复性。



摘要： 本文综述了人工智能在精子分析中的应用，涵盖计算机视觉、深度学习和多模态融合等技术。文章详细讨论了精子检测、运动性评估、形态分类、功能分析及遗传完整性评价等任务，并总结了公开数据集、评估指标和多模态策略。同时，文章指出实际部署中面临的数据稀缺、跨中心域偏移、标注不一致、可解释性等问题，并提出了从技术标准化到临床验证的翻译路线图。该研究强调了AI在辅助生殖技术中的潜力与尚未解决的挑战。

item.title: EvoAgentBench：评估长周期LLM系统中代理自我演化的基准
item.content:encoded:
提出EvoAgentBench，用于评估代理在多个领域中的自我演化能力。



摘要： 本文介绍了EvoAgentBench，这是一个用于评估长周期大语言模型系统中代理自我演化能力的基准。该基准通过提取代理执行过程中产生的可重用能力，并将其结构化为领域特定的能力图，以支持任务间的程序性迁移。与传统评估方法不同，EvoAgentBench关注的是经验编码、路由和吸收的细粒度诊断，而非单纯的准确率比较。实验表明，尽管能力内容可以在不同模型家族间可靠转移，但目前尚无自动方法能在所有设置中持续获得正向收益。该基准已公开供研究使用。

item.title: 视频监测在早产儿呼吸暂停检测中的应用研究
item.content:encoded:
视频监测可有效检测早产儿呼吸暂停，提升监测准确性。



摘要： 该研究探讨了基于摄像头的信号是否能够检测早产儿的呼吸暂停相关呼吸停止（COBE），并提供与常规生理信号互补的信息。通过分析30名早产儿的视频和临床记录，研究人员从动态跟踪的躯干区域提取呼吸运动，并使用ResNet架构训练模型。结果显示，仅使用视频的模型准确率为76.9%，而结合阻抗肺图（IP）等生理信号后，准确率提升至90.6%。这表明视频信号包含有价值的呼吸信息，有助于提高早产儿呼吸监测的可靠性。

item.title: HIEVI-RAG：一种面向封闭领域文档理解的分层多模态RAG框架
item.content:encoded:
HIEVI-RAG解决多模态RAG中的检索与生成问题，提升文档理解性能。



摘要： 本文提出HIEVI-RAG，一种分层、以证据驱动的多模态RAG框架，用于封闭领域的文档理解。该框架通过四个阶段优化检索与生成流程：首先对复杂查询进行分层分解；其次利用多模态检索器获取候选页面；接着通过EVIAGENT进行细粒度页面验证；最后基于记忆引导的迭代生成实现多轮动态推理。实验表明，该框架在多个基准测试中表现优异，平均准确率超过现有基线8.05%。

item.title: 统一音频-文本大模型Audex的构建与应用
item.content:encoded:
Audex是基于Nemotron-Cascade的统一音频-文本大模型，具备强大多模态生成能力。



摘要： 本文介绍了Audex，一个基于Nemotron-Cascade-2-30B-A3B的统一音频-文本大模型。该模型采用单一Transformer解码器架构，将音频输入编码为文本嵌入空间，并在生成过程中统一处理文本和音频输出。通过精心构建的157.4B音频和320.5B文本数据集进行多阶段训练，Audex在音频理解、语音识别、翻译、文本转语音、音频生成等方面表现优异，同时保留了原始文本模型的强大推理、对齐、知识和长上下文能力。

item.title: 大语言模型中可解性知识与表述的分离研究
item.content:encoded:
研究分离了大语言模型中的可解性知识与表述，揭示其可独立操控。



摘要： 尽管大语言模型在数学推理方面取得了显著进展，但判断数学问题是否可解仍然是一个基础而具有挑战性的能力。本文通过分别探究模型中可解性知识和表述的表示，实现了两者的解耦分析。研究发现，知识和表述在模型隐藏状态中以不同的线性可解表示形式存在，而伪造行为主要与表述变化相关。通过引入不可解提示，可以主要通过调整表述来减少伪造行为，激活引导实验进一步表明这些表示可以被机制性操控以提高模型的拒绝回答能力。

item.title: Platonic Projection Structures: Operator-Induced Observability in Representation Learning
item.content:encoded: We characterize observability in representation learning through Platonic Projection Structures (PPS), an operator-theoretic framework for analyzing representation accessibility under partial observation. Rather than treating observable outputs as direct reflections of latent representations, PPS models observation through a self-adjoint positive semidefinite operator acting on a latent representation space. A system is represented as a triple $(H, Π, O)$, where $H$ is a latent representation space, $Π\succeq 0$ is an observation operator, and $O(v)=\langle v,Πv\rangle$ defines an induced scalar observable. Observability is characterized by the quotient geometry $H/\ker(Π)$, representing equivalence classes of latent states indistinguishable under observation. We show that quantum measurement and representation inference under linear observation models share this operator-theoretic structure while differing in the algebraic properties of their observation operators; the correspondence is structural rather than physical. Representation transfer and knowledge distillation can likewise be interpreted as approximate preservation of observable geometry through $ΦΠ_T \approx Π_S Φ$. PPS also reveals a structural limitation of output-based interpretability: latent components in $\ker(Π)$ are inaccessible from induced observables, imposing intrinsic constraints on attribution and explanation methods. Controlled empirical validations demonstrate kernel-invariant observability, projection-induced attribution gaps, and rank-controlled observable geometry in latent representation spaces. PPS thus provides an explicit characterization of observability through operator-induced quotient geometry and a unified perspective on representation accessibility, interpretability, and projection-mediated inference.

item.title: 基于仿真训练的节能代码生成模型研究
item.content:encoded:
通过仿真训练提升代码能量效率，实现更优节能效果。



摘要： 本文提出Green Tea，一个包含350万次评估的C++代码数据集，用于训练能量感知的代码生成模型。通过监督微调和闭环强化学习（GRPO）方法，结合仿真反馈提升代码的能量效率。研究引入CARET指标，以确保代码在保持功能正确性的同时提高能效。实验表明，该方法在143个测试问题中实现了12.63%的CARET提升，并优于58.4%的人类专家参考结果。同时，研究揭示了传统性能指标如IPC无法准确反映真实能效，强调直接能量仿真的必要性。论文还公开了数据集和基础设施，助力社区构建高效节能的代码生成模型。

item.title: 基于记忆增强的类别级物体位姿估计方法 MemPose
item.content:encoded:
MemPose通过引入外部记忆缓冲区提升类别级位姿估计性能。



摘要： 本文提出 MemPose，一种基于记忆增强的类别级物体位姿估计框架。与传统方法依赖固定形状先验或静态参数不同，MemPose 引入外部记忆缓冲区，动态存储和更新先前观察实例的结构表示，使模型能够利用积累的经验支持当前感知。在 REAL275、CAMERA25、Housecat6D 和 Wild6D 四个基准数据集上的实验表明，该方法优于现有最优方法，展现出更强的泛化能力和适应性。

item.title: 基于SMC的生成模型后验条件分析及其误差界研究
item.content:encoded:
本文分析SMC方法在生成模型中的后验条件误差，提出非渐近误差界。



摘要： 本文研究了序列蒙特卡洛（SMC）方法在预训练生成模型后验条件中的应用，重点分析了由于使用近似转移核导致的误差。在正向平滑遗忘条件下，作者将总误差分解为核偏差和有限粒子蒙特卡洛误差，并通过扩展Doeblin条件和Lyapunov漂移论证，实现了对偏差的合理控制。随后，该框架被应用于基于得分的扩散模型的条件采样，首次提出了同时控制初始化误差、时间离散化、得分近似及有限粒子误差的非渐近误差界。

item.title: STEELBENCH：工业监控中的视觉语言模型评估基准
item.content:encoded:
提出STEELBENCH基准，用于评估工业监控中的视觉语言模型性能。



摘要： 现有视频基准主要针对消费级视频、第一视角记录或模拟工业环境，未涵盖真实工业闭路电视（CCTV）中的复杂视觉和操作条件。为此，研究者引入STEELBENCH，这是一个用于工业监控的诊断性基准，同时评估个体工人活动识别、安全规则推理和标注来源的可追溯性。该数据集包含1,345个密集标注的片段，来自149小时工厂操作视频，通过时间去重、类别平衡和可见性感知分层采样筛选。每个片段包含动作标签、个人防护装备属性、空间上下文和安全规则标注。为避免模型辅助标注对评估结果的影响，STEELBENCH还包含一个来源可追溯的审计协议，用于衡量标注影响并评估对真实标注来源的敏感性。实验发现，未经审计的模型标注可能导致同一类模型准确率提高最多17个百分点。九种不同架构的视觉语言模型中，最佳模型仅达到42.6%的动作识别准确率，而人类基准为84.6%。模型在识别、鲁棒性、校准和安全推理方面表现不一，即使预测正确动作，仍有37-58%的情况得出错误的安全判断，且没有模型能通过超过2项诊断测试。数据集已公开于Hugging Face。

item.title: 基于多视角框架的患者特异性药物反应预测方法
item.content:encoded:
PREDIKTOR通过多视角框架提升患者特异性药物反应预测准确性。



摘要： 本文提出PREDIKTOR，一种基于患者中心的多视角框架，用于预测个体对药物的治疗反应。该方法结合个性化基因调控网络和模拟的转录组扰动视图，通过共享潜在空间对齐两种视图，并在TCGA数据集上表现出优于现有方法的性能。PREDIKTOR在多种评估条件下均表现优异，并在I-SPY2试验中实现零样本迁移，显著提升了预测准确率。其对齐嵌入还能稳定地恢复已知机制，支持可解释的精准肿瘤学应用。

item.title: 基于机器学习与蒙特卡洛模拟的数字电路可靠性分析框架
item.content:encoded:
SMART框架提升数字电路可靠性分析效率与精度。



摘要： 随着CMOS技术进入深纳米尺度，数字电路可靠性面临Bias Temperature Instability（BTI）和Process Variation（PV）的双重挑战。传统方法因计算复杂度高而难以适应大规模设计。本文提出SMART框架，结合机器学习与蒙特卡洛模拟，通过随机森林回归预测门延迟分布，并利用贝叶斯优化进行超参数调优，显著提升分析效率与准确性。实验表明，SMART在ISCAS85基准电路中相比现有方法节省94.54%分析时间，误差仅1.63%，为可靠数字系统设计提供高效解决方案。

item.title: CanniUplift：解决多卖家环境下激励分配的双重侵蚀问题
item.content:encoded:
CanniUplift框架有效应对多卖家环境中的激励侵蚀问题，提升平台GMV。



摘要： 在电子商务中，个性化激励分配对提升平台增长至关重要。然而，在多卖家环境中，传统模型因违反SUTVA假设而面临挑战。本文提出CanniUplift框架，通过Platform-level Global Alignment（PGA）捕捉跨店铺替代效应，并利用Redemption-based Decomposition Denoising（RDD）减少激励噪声。此外，Treat-Attention机制用于建模用户行为与激励选项之间的复杂交互。实验表明，该框架在合成数据和真实工业数据上均优于现有方法，成功提升了平台增量GMV和ROI。
