item.title: AIFS-SUBS: Extending Data-Driven Forecasting to Sub-Seasonal Timescales
item.content:encoded: Data-driven models now rival numerical weather prediction in the medium range, but extending them to sub-seasonal lead times raises challenges absent at shorter horizons. Errors accumulate over long autoregressive rollouts, systematic biases grow with lead time, and several years of data must be held out for independent verification, even though machine-learning models otherwise benefit from longer training records. To address these challenges, we adapt ECMWF's AIFS-CRPS medium-range model. AIFS-SUBS adopts a 24h autoregressive time step to reduce error accumulation, adds stratospheric levels and top-of-atmosphere thermal radiation as predictors, and reserves 2007--2011 as an independent verification window. We evaluate two config-durations: AIFS-SUBS, fine-tuned on operational analyses, and AIFS-SUBS-ERA5, trained on ERA5 alone. Across weeks 2--6, AIFS-SUBS matches the operational Integrated Forecasting System (IFS) in probabilistic skill while reducing systematic biases. For the convective (OLR) component of the Madden--Julian Oscillation (MJO), AIFS-SUBS extends skilful forecasts (correlation > 0.5) by eight days relative to the IFS, while matching or exceeding the IFS for the full multivariate RMM index. AIFS-SUBS also reproduces the observed MJO modulation of tropical cyclone activity comparably. Stratospheric skill is particularly strong with AIFS-SUBS reproducing sudden stratospheric warming (SSW) frequency and surface impact. In the AI Weather Quest, AIFS-SUBS-ERA5 attains a variable-averaged ranked probability skill score slightly ahead of the IFS at weeks 3 and 4. At inference, AIFS-SUBS uses about 200 times less energy than the IFS, opening the door to much larger real-time ensembles. AIFS-SUBS is ECMWF's first machine-learning model targeted at sub-seasonal time-scales.

item.title: Deform360: A Massive Multi-view Visuotactile Dataset for Deformable World Models
item.content:encoded: Predicting object dynamics (i.e., world modeling) is a fundamental challenge for robotic manipulation, and modeling deformable objects presents a particularly difficult case due to their high-dimensional state spaces and complex material properties. While current world models approach this through two distinct paradigms: learning the dynamics over the 2D pixel space or more explicit 3D geometric space. A systematic understanding of their relative strengths and limitations remains elusive due to the lack of diverse, large-scale real-world data. To address this, we present Deform360, a large-scale visuotactile dataset featuring 198 daily-life objects, 1,980 interaction sequences, and over 215 hours of observations from 41 surround-view cameras and bimanual tactile grippers to capture both global motion and contact-induced local deformations. Leveraging a novel markerless visuotactile 3D tracking pipeline to extract dense geometry and motion, we systematically evaluate current state-of-the-art world models, comparing 2D video models against 3D particle models. Finally, we provide a preliminary demonstration indicating the real-world applicability of our dataset by performing robot planning tasks on deformable objects. Our analysis reveals key insights into the trade-offs between structural priors and scalability, providing a solid benchmark for future research in generalizable deformable object-centric world modeling. Project website: https://deform360.lhy.xyz

item.title: TacReasoner: A Dynamic Tactile-Language Framework for Interactive Reasoning in Real-World Scenarios
item.content:encoded: Among the five primary human senses, tactile is arguably the most fundamental to survival, as it enables the perception of physical contact and interaction in real-world environments. In this paper, we explore two key challenges of integrating tactile sensing into intelligent systems for multimodal reasoning: (i) insufficient modeling of dynamic tactile signals, which restricts reasoning over temporally evolving properties, and (ii) hallucination in tactile foundation models caused by the absence of explicit reasoning mechanisms, leading to unstable real-world inference. To address these challenges, we propose TacReasoner, a dynamic tactile-language framework for interactive reasoning in real-world scenarios. First, TacReasoner incorporates a Dynamic-aware Tactile Encoder to enhance the perception and representation of dynamic tactile signals. More importantly, we introduce TouchCoT-10k, the first tactile chain-of-thought dataset for structured reasoning over tactile inputs. Upon it, we establish DynTac-Bench to systematically evaluate dynamic tactile perception and real-world commonsense reasoning. Experimental results demonstrate that TacReasoner achieves competitive performance against state-of-the-art models across multiple datasets. Notably, despite using only 7B parameters, TacReasoner outperforms the 14B VTV-LLM model on most subtasks, highlighting its effectiveness and efficiency in tactile commonsense reasoning.

item.title: Learning Only What Valid Adapters Can Express: Subspace-Constrained Adaptation Against Fine-Tuning Poisoning
item.content:encoded: Parameter-efficient fine-tuning still leaves a broad space of behavior-changing updates reachable, so a poisoned objective can be represented and optimized. We study an alternative: adaptation constrained to the subspace estimated from a trusted pool of existing task adapters. On flan-t5-large with 196 public LoRA adapters, we show that (1) the functionally relevant content of an adapter lies in a low-dimensional shared subspace, 30 to 38 percent of its weight norm being redundant under the evaluated task distributions; (2) gradient adaptation restricted to 128 coordinates on this subspace matches full LoRA fine-tuning on clean classification data, while under targeted label inversion LoRA collapses to 3-26 percent exact match and the constrained learner keeps 62-96 percent on the tasks the pool covers; (3) the constrained learner cannot fit corrupted data, its adaptation loss separating clean from garbage by two orders of magnitude (120x), an out-of-distribution signal without an extra detector; and (4) against an adaptive backdoor attacker who optimizes within the subspace, the attack is blocked (8 percent success versus 100 for LoRA) on the task where its target behavior is unlike anything in the pool, and only partially blocked (85 percent) when the target coincides with a common pool behavior. On these two tasks the outcome is consistent with how close the target is to the pool's directions, which suggests but does not establish a pool-relative boundary. The mechanism trades peak plasticity for these properties: on tasks the pool covers poorly, unconstrained fine-tuning wins, and the protection assumes the pool itself is trusted. Code and data are public.

item.title: HamQASBench: A Hamiltonian-Informed Diagnostic Benchmark for Evaluating Quantum Architecture Search
item.content:encoded: Quantum Architecture Search (QAS) automates the design of parameterized quantum circuits for variational quantum algorithms, yet existing benchmarks organize instances by molecular identity or qubit count -- criteria agnostic to Hamiltonian structure -- and rely solely on energy accuracy, which cannot detect structural failures such as over-parameterization on near-product ground states. We introduce HamQASBench, a Hamiltonian-informed diagnostic benchmark organizing 11 molecules into five structural tiers via fingerprints derived from the Pauli operator basis, computational basis representation, and ground-state entanglement. A post-hoc critical-structure extraction procedure identifies minimal circuits consistent with each tier's requirements, complementing energy-based evaluation with per-qubit entanglement analysis and pairwise state fidelity. Benchmarking five QAS methods across four paradigms reveals failure modes invisible to conventional metrics: over-parameterization in the minimalism regime, eigenstate commitment under degeneracy, a representation bottleneck in strongly correlated systems, topology-induced routing failure, and circuit search space growth as a scalability bottleneck.

item.title: TGRIP: A Text-Guided Approach to Vehicle Instance Prediction in Autonomous Driving
item.content:encoded: Bird's-Eye View (BEV) end-to-end instance prediction has emerged as a robust paradigm for autonomous driving perception, effectively mitigating the error propagation inherent in traditional modular pipelines. However, current state-of-the-art approaches rely predominantly on geometric supervision, such as occupancy regression and optical flow, effectively treating scene agents as generic moving obstacles. This absence of explicit semantic awareness imposes limitations on the capacity of the model to solve ambiguities in complex scenarios, particularly those where object-specific behavior is essential for accurate forecasting (e.g. overtaking, intersections). In this paper, we introduce Text-Guided Representation for Instance Prediction (TGRIP), a novel framework that bridges this gap by injecting rich semantic priors into the instance prediction loop. The proposed teacher-student pipeline employs Vision-Language Foundation Models to generate dense, semantic-enhanced BEV maps from multi-camera images. These maps serve as auxiliary supervision during training, guiding the network to learn spatio-temporal representations that are not only geometrically consistent but also semantically discriminative. To the best of our knowledge, this represents the first attempt to unify semantic guidance with the temporal task of future instance prediction. The experimental results demonstrate that TGRIP surpasses existing state-of-the-art models in nuScenes, validating the hypothesis that semantic enrichment is a fundamental element for robust, end-to-end motion prediction. Code is available on https://github.com/miguelag99/TGRIP.

item.title: DiCE-CIR: Direct Composition Learning for Efficient Zero-Shot Composed Image Retrieval
item.content:encoded: Zero-shot composed image retrieval (ZS-CIR) aims to retrieve a target image from a multimodal query consisting of a reference image and an edit text describing the desired modification. Recent ZS-CIR studies have relied on projection-based methods that map a reference image into pseudo-word tokens in the text embedding space. However, such methods require additional projection and re-encoding steps, increasing training complexity, reducing efficiency, and introducing a discrepancy between training and inference. In this paper, we propose DiCE-CIR, a direct composition learning method that predicts composed query representations by directly composing a reference image and an edit text. To enable scalable training without manually annotated triplets, we automatically construct compositional training samples from large-scale image-caption pairs using a large language model. Based on these samples, we train a lightweight composition module with objectives that promote alignment with the target, edit-consistent semantic transformation, and retrieval discriminability. We conduct extensive experiments on ZS-CIR benchmarks and show that DiCE-CIR achieves state-of-the-art performance on CIRCO and competitive performance on CIRR while maintaining high computational efficiency.

item.title: LLM-as-a-Verifier: A General-Purpose Verification Framework
item.content:encoded: Scaling pre-training, post-training, and test-time compute have become the central paradigms for improving the capabilities of LLMs. In this work, we identify verification, the ability to determine the correctness of a solution, as a new scaling axis. To unlock this and demonstrate its effectiveness, we introduce LLM-as-a-Verifier, a general-purpose verification framework that provides fine-grained feedback for agentic tasks without requiring additional training. Unlike standard LM judges that prompt LLMs to produce discrete scores for candidate solutions, LLM-as-a-Verifier computes the expectation over the distribution of scoring token logits to generate continuous scores. This probabilistic formulation enables verification to scale along multiple dimensions: (1) score granularity, (2) repeated evaluation, and (3) criteria decomposition. In particular, we show that scaling the scoring granularity leads to better separation between positive and negative solutions, resulting in more calibrated comparisons. Moreover, scaling repeated evaluation and criteria decomposition consistently lead to additional gains in verification accuracy through variance and complexity reduction. We further introduce a cost-efficient ranking algorithm for selecting the best solution among candidates using the verifier's continuous scores. LLM-as-a-Verifier achieves state-of-the-art performance on Terminal-Bench V2 (86.5%), SWE-Bench Verified (78.2%), RoboRewardBench (87.4%), and MedAgentBench (73.3%). Beyond verification, the fine-grained signals from LLM-as-a-Verifier can also serve as a proxy for estimating task progress. We build an extension for Claude Code, enabling developers to monitor and improve their own agentic systems. Finally, we show that LLM-as-a-Verifier can provide dense feedback for RL, improving the sample efficiency of SAC and GRPO on robotics and mathematical reasoning benchmarks.

item.title: ProCon: Projection-Consistency Memory for Training-Free Anomaly Detection
item.content:encoded: Memory-based anomaly detection is attractive because it localizes defects from normal images without training a decoder or synthesizing pseudo anomalies. However, most memory methods still use the memory bank as a nearest-neighbor lookup table: a test patch is treated as normal if it has one nearby normal anchor. This hard retrieval view is vulnerable to false-normal matches and does not test whether the patch is consistently supported by a local normal neighborhood. We propose ProCon, a training-free framework that turns memory retrieval into decoder-free reconstruction. ProCon softly projects each test patch onto nearby normal memory vectors and uses the projection residual as anomaly evidence. To stabilize this residual, it constructs seed-perturbed layer-wise memories, aggregates bank residuals by a median, and fuses depth-specific residual maps by layer consensus. ProCon requires no decoder training, backbone fine-tuning, learned fusion weights, or pseudo-anomaly supervision. Across MVTec-AD, VisA, and Real-IAD under the single-category evaluation protocol, ProCon achieves strong image- and pixel-level performance under seven standard metrics, including image AUROC scores of 99.8%, 99.2%, and 93.2%, respectively. Ablations show that the gains come from replacing hard retrieval with soft normal projection and stabilizing the residuals through memory and depth consensus. The code is available at https://github.com/jw-chae/Procon

item.title: The syntax of wh-agreement in Yemeni Ibbi Arabic
item.content:encoded: This article tackles an important phenomenon in the syntax of Yemeni Ibbi Arabic (YIA), viz., wh-agreement, a phenomenon common to several languages including Greek, Indonesian, Lubukusu, Irish, etc. In YIA, wh-agreement manifests itself via agreement inflections on the Wh-Op, C, T/V, v. To account for this phenomenon, we propose an Agree across phases (AAP) approach anchored in the mechanism of Feature Inheritance (FI) in which Agree as MATCHING (AM) is a bit separated from feature valuation (FV). AM concerns Cs/vs, but FV Ts/Vs. Analyzing the agreement patterns observed between Wh-Op(erators), functional heads (precisely C, (T), v), and verbal complexes, we argue that the suffixes -eh, -uh, -nen, -um, having undergone grammaticalization process from Stannard Arabic (SA) third person pronouns, function as morphological marking of wh-agreement. Findings indicate that YIA data offer a unique empirical contribution to generative syntax, specifically concerning wh-agreement in this dialect operating via MATCHING mechanism. Our proposal straightforwardly accounts for wh-agreement cross-linguistically. This study provides further evidence that incorporating under-investigated typology provides further support for the universality of Universal Grammar (UG) by revealing how specific I-language operations reflect deeper, invariant principles of human language architecture. It concludes that the wh-agreement mechanism in YIA is more morphosyntactically robust than in languages such as Greek, Indonesian, Palauan, and Irish, providing compelling evidence for AAP as a UG approach to long-distance dependencies.

item.title: Strategic Buying Agents
item.content:encoded: Agentic AI is shifting online shopping from search toward delegated purchasing, where autonomous buying agents monitor markets and decide when to buy on a consumer's behalf. We study the design of such strategic buying agents, which must decide when to purchase within a finite shopping window, translating price observations, the remaining time horizon, and beliefs about future price changes into a purchase policy. We formulate this problem across three information regimes: stationary, Bayesian, and robust, and treat the resulting optimal policies as a policy menu for implementation. In the stationary regime, price adjustments follow a Poisson arrival process with a known post-adjustment price distribution; the optimal policy is a dynamic purchase-threshold rule, with the threshold governed by an ordinary differential equation. In the Bayesian regime, the adjustment intensity is known, but the price-adjustment distribution is uncertain; the optimal rule remains threshold-based, now depending on posterior beliefs, and we bound the value of knowing the true distribution. In the robust regime, the agent has only price bounds and seeks worst-case protection; randomized threshold policies achieve optimal competitive-ratio and minimax-regret guarantees. We evaluate the proposed policies on Amazon price histories from Keepa (367 items, 48,933 timestamped observations) and examine their integration into language-model buying agents. The stationary and Bayesian policies perform competitively on mean normalized consumer surplus despite their stylized assumptions, while the robust policy performs best at the distribution's 10th percentile. Results suggest language models are better suited to selecting among regimes and calibration samples than to making buy-or-wait decisions directly.

item.title: Graph Sparse Sampling: Breaking the Curse of the Horizon in Continuous MDP Planning
item.content:encoded: Planning under uncertainty in continuous domains is essential for autonomous systems, yet computationally demanding. Tree-based search methods such as Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) remain popular, but their branching structure can require sampling budgets that grow exponentially with lookahead depth in the worst case. From a tree perspective, continuous state or action spaces become especially challenging, since the planner must decide where to search in an infinite branching hierarchy. We propose Graph Sparse Sampling (GSS), an online planning algorithm that shares sampled futures across many candidate decisions, rather than sampling separate successors for each candidate action. This branch-free graph exposes large GPU-friendly batches, while using heuristics to focus computation. We prove finite-sample performance guarantees for GSS covering full-rank or low-rank generative simulators via smoothed backups, and discrete or sampled continuous action spaces. Under suitable overlap, regularity, and action-coverage conditions, these bounds have polynomial dependence on the planning horizon, formalizing when shared futures can avoid the exponential horizon dependence of tree-shaped sparse sampling. We demonstrate continuous-control simulations where GSS substantially outperforms tree-based planners on long horizons or achieves near-optimal performance, supporting no-branching graph planning as a complementary design principle for online control.

item.title: RADIANCE: Relative Adaptive Denoising with IP-Adapter for Novel Concept Enhancement
item.content:encoded: Text-to-image (T2I) diffusion models have achieved striking progress but still struggle to synthesize rare concepts involving unusual attribute-object pairings, often resulting in concept omission or semantic drift where a dominant entity overwhelms the generation. Tracing these failures to a lack of compositional balance during the denoising trajectory, we propose RADIANCE, a training-free framework that treats inference as a closed-loop feedback process. RADIANCE augments pretrained backbones with three modular components: (1) a Compositional Similarity Monitor (CSM) that tracks the emergence of objects and attributes in intermediate latents via CLIP-based feedback; (2) a Bidirectional Scale Controller (BSC) that applies a reactive "restoring force" using positive and negative IP-Adapter scales to rebalance biased trajectories; and (3) a Feedback Guidance Scheduler (FGS) that coordinates these updates across timesteps without additional training. We further extend the framework to multi-object prompts via Delayed Adapter Activation (DAA) and Layer-wise Alternating Guidance (LAG) to prevent premature concept fusion. By overlapping monitoring and denoising through pipelined execution, RADIANCE maintains competitive latency while significantly enhancing the per-sample success rate and effective throughput. Experiments on RareBench and T2I-CompBench demonstrate that RADIANCE consistently enhances compositional alignment and perceptual quality over state-of-the-art baselines.

item.title: Fitted Occupancy-Ratio Evaluation without Bellman Completeness
item.content:encoded: Occupancy ratios correct distribution shift in offline reinforcement learning and are central to off-policy evaluation. Existing primal-dual and minimax methods typically estimate these ratios by enforcing occupancy-balance moments over a critic class. We propose fitted occupancy-ratio evaluation (FORE), a fitted fixed-point method that characterizes the discounted occupancy ratio through an adjoint Bellman recursion. At each iteration, FORE solves a single-level density-ratio objective on one-step-transition data, thereby projecting the adjoint Bellman image onto a log-ratio class in Kullback--Leibler (KL) divergence. Unlike analyses of fitted Q-evaluation, which typically require value-function realizability together with Bellman completeness or projected-operator stability, our central approximation condition is just realizability of the discounted occupancy ratio itself. Under this condition, the population KL-projected recursion contracts in relative entropy toward the true ratio by virtue of the adjoint Bellman operator being a KL-contraction. For the empirical recursion, we establish finite-sample regret bounds that yield convergence in KL up to log-ratio approximation error and a statistical error governed by the complexity of the ratio hypothesis class. The fitted ratio supports direct value estimation by reward reweighting, occupancy-weighted fitted Q-evaluation, and doubly robust estimation that combines the fitted ratio with a fitted Q-function. Together, these results identify discounted occupancy-ratio realizability as a sufficient condition for offline policy evaluation without any completeness assumptions.

item.title: InFlux++: Real and Synthetic Data for Estimating Dynamic Camera Intrinsics
item.content:encoded: Camera intrinsics are vital for recovering 3D structure from 2D video. However, most 3D algorithms assume fixed intrinsics throughout a video, an assumption that often fails for real-world in-the-wild videos. Consequently, estimating per-frame intrinsics from RGB images is critical for making 3D methods robust to videos with dynamic intrinsics. InFlux previously advanced this research direction by establishing the first real-world benchmark with per-frame ground truth intrinsics for dynamic intrinsics videos. Nevertheless, existing methods remain inaccurate due to two obstacles: (i) training data is scarce and lacks intrinsics diversity; and (ii) benchmarks, including InFlux, have limited scene and camera motion diversity, making it difficult to properly evaluate methods. To address both gaps, we present InFlux++, consisting of two components. InFlux++ Synth is a large-scale procedurally generated synthetic video dataset with 441K+ annotated frames from 1841 high-resolution videos, providing accurate per-frame ground truth intrinsics for training dynamic intrinsics prediction models; a subset also includes per-frame pose, depth, and normals. The videos feature rich intrinsics diversity through changes in camera zoom and focus, as well as dynamic objects and realistic rendering effects such as lens distortion and defocus blur. InFlux++ Real is a large-scale real-world benchmark that extends InFlux with 514K+ newly captured frames across 334 high-resolution videos, spanning a wider range of scenes and camera motions. Finetuning existing intrinsics prediction methods on InFlux++ Synth consistently improves focal length estimation across both InFlux++ Real and InFlux, suggesting that synthetic supervision is promising for RGB-based intrinsics prediction. For the dataset, benchmark, code, videos, submission instructions, and live leaderboard, please visit https://influx.cs.princeton.edu/ .

item.title: MRMS: A Multi-Resolution Memory Substrate for Long-Lived AI Agents
item.content:encoded: Long-lived AI agents require continuity across interactions, but continuity cannot be obtained by simply extending the prompt window. An agent must preserve useful prior experience, retrieve it selectively, distinguish personal context from external evidence, and revise memory when the underlying situation changes. We propose an architectural memory substrate organized along two orthogonal axes: a representational axis spanning structured records, vector representations, and graph relations; and a temporal axis spanning short-term traces, medium-term abstractions, and long-term semantic commitments. Its key design constraint is synchronized structured-vector-graph memory: structured records govern eligibility, vector representations support recall, and graph relations adjudicate support, contradiction, and supersession before gated context projection. Its central claim is that reliable personalization is a memory design problem: useful memory is structured, selectively exposed, continuously consolidated, and epistemically labeled rather than stored as undifferentiated conversation history. Beyond the framework, we instantiate MRMS as a lightweight prototype implementing structured records, vector retrieval, temporal policies, and graph-based revision. The prototype exercises the core substrate mechanisms through pre-generation memory selection, revision, boundary enforcement, and evidence attribution under controlled long-lived interaction scenarios with explicit evidence requirements.

item.title: Wavelet Scattering Transform for Interpretable Schizophrenia Biomarker Discovery and Classification from Resting-State EEG
item.content:encoded: Schizophrenia is a debilitating neuropsychiatric disorder characterized by profound cortical network dysregulation, for which objective, clinically translatable EEG based biomarkers remain underdeveloped. Existing automated classification pipelines rely predominantly on static power spectral density features inherently blind to amplitude modulation dynamics and cross-frequency coupling, phenomena central to schizophrenia pathophysiology, while adopting epoch level cross validation strategies that introduce temporal data leakage, artificially inflate reported performance. This study introduces a mathematically principled diagnostic framework integrating the multi-order Wavelet Scattering Transform(WST), strict Leave One Subject Out (LOSO) cross-validation, and SHAP explainability for simultaneous EEG classification and biomarker discovery. Hierarchical WST coefficients capturing multi-scale amplitude modulation structure were extracted from resting state multichannel EEG. Subject-level ANOVA with Benjamini Hochberg false discovery rate correction identified significant biomarkers, with Random Forest and SVM classifiers evaluated under strict LOSO cross validation and subject-level majority voting. Second-order scattering coefficients encoding cross frequency coupling dominated the discriminative biomarker set, with gamma-band features most prevalent, demonstrating that temporal amplitude modulation constitutes the primary electrophysiological signature of schizophrenia. Electrode P3 was identified as the single most discriminative site. Under rigorous subject independent evaluation, the Random Forest achieved 90.48% accuracy (AUC = 0.9339; sensitivity = 95.56%). The proposed WST framework establishes a rigorous, interpretable standard for EEG-driven psychiatric biomarker discovery that can also be applicable in the detection of schizophrenia subtypes in the future.

item.title: Three-Phase Evaluation of AI-Assisted Software Development Life Cycle
item.content:encoded: This paper presents an exploratory evaluation of how increasing levels of AI autonomy affect software development productivity, requirement adherence, and developer cognitive workload. A team of four developers reimplemented the same full-stack web application across three sequential phases: partial AI-assisted development using GitHub Copilot, an AI-exclusive workflow using GitHub Copilot, and an AI-exclusive workflow using AWS Kiro. Evaluation metrics included development effort (hours), requirement adherence (RITM score), AI-interaction efficiency, and NASA-TLX workload measures. Across phases, higher levels of AI autonomy were associated with reduced development effort, improved requirement adherence, and lower self-reported mental workload, while developer frustration increased modestly. The AWS Kiro phase achieved the strongest overall performance on most measured dimensions, suggesting that tooling architecture may influence outcomes independently of AI autonomy level.

item.title: Can temporal article-level credibility signals improve domain-level credibility prediction?
item.content:encoded: Web domain credibility evaluation is vital for combating misinformation. It is conducted by examining factors such as domain type, transparency, and overall reputation. However, assessing the credibility of newly emerging web domains remains challenging since they have no reputation yet. Expert fact-checkers evaluate the credibility of domains by analyzing the content of their articles, including the presence of misinformation, bias, or propaganda. Yet, the ease of large-scale content generation enabled by LLMs has accelerated the creation of new content, rendering manual assessment insufficient and underscoring the need for automated approaches to domain credibility evaluation. In this paper, we introduce our Domain Credibility Evaluation Framework (DCEF), a temporal framework for domain credibility evaluation grounded in expert ratings. DCEF enables us to investigate whether the credibility of web domains can be assessed from their published articles following the workflow of expert fact-checkers, without any prior knowledge of the source domains themselves.

item.title: SPEARBench: A Benchmark for Naturalness Evaluation in Streaming Speech-to-Speech Language Models
item.content:encoded: Streaming speech-to-speech language models aim to answer spoken queries directly with synthetic speech. However, standard speech and text benchmarks do not capture whether these systems behave naturally in conversations, where timing, turn-taking, prosody, interpersonal stance, language and dialect consistency, and relationship-aware appropriateness jointly shape perceived quality. We introduce SPEARBench, a benchmark for evaluating naturalness in speech-to-speech language models from question-answer interactions. SPEARBench constructs controlled dialogue prompts from the Seamless Interaction corpus, runs inference across multiple models, and evaluates generated answers using a multidimensional protocol that covers response latency, interruptions, speech quality, ASR robustness, language and dialect consistency, emotional naturalness, interpersonal stance, and explainable distributional baselines. The benchmark includes original human answers as a reference condition and reports results for several contemporary models. Results show that current models can achieve high signal-level quality and low ASR error while still differing from human conversational behavior in latency, overlap, dialect preservation, emotional adaptation, and interpersonal stance dynamics.

item.title: 基于验证期诊断的潜在世界模型闭环性能预测研究
item.content:encoded:
通过ROF指标提升世界模型闭环性能预测效果。



摘要： 本文研究如何仅通过验证期诊断来预测学习到的潜在世界模型在下游闭环任务中的表现。在LunarLander v3环境中，作者训练了一个RSSM世界模型，并使用CEM-MPC返回值作为闭环性能的基准。通过评估40个指标，发现奖励可观测性分数（ROF）是最强的单一预测指标。进一步结合三个结构正则化项，提出了复合奖励可观测性分数（CROF），用于离线选择最佳检查点。实验表明，基于CROF选择的世界模型在模型强化学习中优于无模型基线，且所需真实环境交互显著减少，同时还能驱动强大的零样本CEM-MPC策略。

item.title: AF3AD：一种用于3D点云异常检测的合成框架
item.content:encoded:
AF3AD通过生成伪异常数据提升3D点云异常检测效果。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为Anomaly Factory 3D (AF3AD) 的模块化框架，用于在3D点云中检测和定位缺陷。由于正常数据占主导，异常样本稀缺且多样，AF3AD通过从正常点云中合成多样化的伪异常来扩展训练数据。该框架采用基于局部PCA坐标系的参数化变形模型，结合空间衰减、各向异性、方向门控和法向/切向位移场，实现多种几何缺陷的模拟。实验表明，AF3AD可以与不同检测方法结合，并在AnomalyShapeNet和Real3D-AD数据集上提升了物体级和点级的检测与定位性能。AF3AD作为独立的合成工具，有助于推动多种3D异常检测方法的发展。

item.title: Device Passport：提升生物信号模型跨布局迁移能力的新通道嵌入方法
item.content:encoded:
研究新通道嵌入方法提升生物信号模型跨设备迁移性能。



摘要： 本文探讨了在缺乏大规模特定布局数据的情况下，如何通过改进通道嵌入技术来提升生物信号基础模型的跨布局迁移能力。作者提出了一种名为Device Passport的新方法，该方法结合通道的功能活动和元数据进行学习，与以往仅依赖功能信息或元数据的方法不同。实验结果显示，Device Passport在多个跨布局任务中表现优异，特别是在耳部脑电图应用中优于现有最强基线模型，表明通道嵌入设计对生物信号模型的再利用具有重要意义。

item.title: 基于知识蒸馏的3D大模型压缩研究
item.content:encoded:
通过知识蒸馏压缩3D大模型，提升在资源受限环境下的部署能力。



摘要： 本文研究了如何通过知识蒸馏压缩大型3D基础模型MASt3R，以适应如月球探测等计算资源受限的应用场景。实验中，从一个6.88亿参数的MASt3R教师模型出发，将其密集几何预测蒸馏到多种轻量学生模型中。为解决教师与学生之间的维度不匹配问题，提出了一种结构化SVD初始化方法，有效提升了收敛速度和性能。实验结果显示，压缩后的学生模型在保持高重建精度的同时，模型大小减少了7倍，甚至优于直接使用稀疏标注训练的基线模型。研究还揭示了关于几何基础模型蒸馏的关键原则和实践见解，如卷积编码器不如Transformer架构，保留编码器容量比扩大解码器更重要，特征级蒸馏优于仅输出监督，SVD初始化有助于优化稳定性。

item.title: 阿拉伯语-俄语科学翻译基准研究
item.content:encoded:
构建阿拉伯语-俄语科学翻译基准，提升科研交流效率。



摘要： 本文介绍了阿拉伯语与俄语之间的科学翻译基准，包含约27,000句对的混合平行语料库，涵盖科学摘要和通用领域文本。研究对三种多语言模型进行了LoRA微调，其中Qwen2.5-7B模型在QLoRA（秩8）下表现最佳，BLEU得分为23.15，BERTScore为0.906。实验表明，零样本基线需通过领域微调提升性能，而少量示例提示无效。研究释放了模型、语料库和评估代码，旨在降低语言障碍，促进阿拉伯语和俄语科研人员的知识共享，助力可持续发展和创新基础设施建设。

item.title: 基于医学先验的超声图像分类框架
item.content:encoded:
提出一种结合医学先验的双分支超声分类框架，提升诊断准确性和可解释性。



摘要： 本文针对超声图像分类中临床先验信息不足导致的泛化能力差和可解释性弱的问题，提出一种属性引导的双分支框架。该框架包含一个基础分支和一个引入领域无关医学属性的属性引导分支，通过自适应决策模块融合两者的输出，实现更准确和可解释的分类结果。实验表明，该方法可无缝集成到多种模型中，显著提升性能且开销低。代码已开源。

item.title: 基于多级分布熵的机器学习入侵检测系统研究
item.content:encoded:
多级分布熵方法提升入侵检测性能，无需原始数据。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为多级分布熵（MDE）的分析框架，通过流级别汇总统计信息，在三个层次上提取可解释的熵特征，包括流内高斯微分熵、跨方向Jensen-Shannon散度和TCP标志模式香农熵。该方法无需原始数据或训练集即可实现高效的入侵检测。在多个基准数据集上的实验表明，仅使用熵特征即可达到与传统特征相当的性能，且在操作指标报告中揭示了聚合F1值可能掩盖的失败模式。此外，研究还发现时间漂移下模型表现下降的问题，并通过SHAP分析验证了熵特征的可重复性和领域一致性。

item.title: 基于对称群的等变庞加莱残差网络研究
item.content:encoded:
等变庞加莱残差网络提升超平面优化效率与空间对齐性。



摘要： 本文提出等变庞加莱残差网络，结合双曲几何与离散对称群（C4和D4），解决传统超平面网络在空间变换中参数冗余和信号消失的问题。通过几何安全张量重塑、左正则排列和联合方向庞加莱中点批量归一化，有效减少优化空间，加速收敛，同时满足庞加莱球边界约束并保持空间-群等变性。实验表明该方法显著提升了模型性能。

item.title: DeadPool：高效容错的大型语言模型训练方法
item.content:encoded:
DeadPool实现零开销训练与快速故障恢复。



摘要： 本文提出DeadPool，一种用于大型语言模型（LLM）训练的高效容错机制。传统方法在无故障执行时有显著开销或在故障恢复时耗时过长，而DeadPool通过热切换技术，在不中断任务的情况下用备用节点替换故障节点。其核心包括非关键路径的内存检查点机制和运行时通信器重建协议，使检查点与计算并行执行，从而实现无开销的正常训练。在永久性节点故障时，利用内存检查点进行最小化重新计算，恢复时间小于40秒。实验表明，DeadPool在大规模GPU集群和大参数模型中均表现优异。

item.title: OPINE-World：基于对象中心程序模型的高效环境学习代理
item.content:encoded:
OPINE-World通过在线交互学习高效环境模型，提升任务适应能力。



摘要： 本文介绍了一种名为 OPINE-World 的 LLM 代理，它能够通过在线交互学习对象中心的程序化世界模型。该代理结合两个协作的智能体，一个在环境中行动，另一个通过回放验证和基于模型的规划生成代码模型，并利用一种称为“本体误差”的贝叶斯度量来引导探索。OPINE-World 在 ARC-AGI-3 基准测试中表现出色，无需每局单独训练即可解决 25 个游戏中的 20 个，动作效率得分为 78.4，优于人类基准。

item.title: 轻量级零样本异常检测框架LiZAD在工业生产中的应用
item.content:encoded:
LiZAD实现高效零样本异常检测，适用于边缘设备。



摘要： 在现代高通量工业生产线中，产品配置和视觉特征频繁变化，使得为每个新场景收集和标注数据变得不切实际。因此，零样本异常检测（ZSAD）成为一种理想方案，可在无需目标特定样本训练的情况下进行缺陷检测。然而，现有ZSAD方法计算成本高，难以部署在资源受限的设备上。为此，研究者提出LiZAD：一个专为边缘设备设计的轻量级ZSAD框架。该框架结合DINOv3的密集且空间感知的视觉特征与MobileCLIP2的高效文本嵌入，通过低内存可训练投影头将特征映射到共享潜在空间。相比六种最先进的ZSAD模型，LiZAD在内存、参数和延迟方面分别减少了61.5%、74.6%和3.02倍，同时仅比最佳模型平均P-AUROC下降6.4%。该方法已在NVIDIA Jetson NX和Jetson AGX设备上成功部署，并在维罗纳大学工业计算机工程实验室的真实生产线上进行了测试。

item.title: 基于Realsee3D数据集的全景3D重建方法Argus
item.content:encoded:
Argus实现高精度全景3D重建，提升相机姿态与深度估计性能。



摘要： 由于缺乏大规模全景RGB-D训练数据，全景数据的度量前馈3D重建仍处于探索阶段。本文提出Realsee3D数据集，包含10,000个室内场景（1,000个真实、9,000个合成）和299,000个全景视角，并引入Argus网络进行度量全景3D重建。Argus通过学习的共视模块选择最优参考视角以避免全局位姿漂移，并通过分解双向像素到世界映射来增强多任务学习中的几何一致性。在Realsee3D基准测试中，Argus在相机姿态估计、深度估计和点云重建方面均取得最先进的度量性能。

item.title: 文本嵌入相似性度量的几何条件研究
item.content:encoded:
嵌入空间的几何结构决定相似性度量选择。



摘要： 本文通过全面实验证明，文本嵌入的几何结构决定了哪种相似性度量更有效。当嵌入方差均匀分布时，余弦相似度表现最佳；而当方差集中在少数方向（即各向异性）时，基于排名或L1的度量效果更优。实验覆盖19种编码器和19种无参数相似性度量，结果显示，单一主方向的方差占比可预测替代度量的效果，相关性高达0.95。通过去除主方向，余弦相似度性能恢复，表明几何结构是关键因素。

item.title: 基于聚类的语义分块对RAG系统性能的影响研究
item.content:encoded:
研究比较不同分块方法对RAG系统性能的影响。



摘要： 本文评估了基于聚类的语义分块在长篇结构化学术论文中的检索和回答质量，与固定大小分块和递归分块方法进行对比。研究使用RAGAs框架进行测试，发现基于忠实度的评估指标在此设置中可靠性有限。固定问题与文档特定问题的表现差异较大，可能与文档格式和预处理有关。实验结果表明，在所测试配置下，基于聚类的分块方法并未优于更简单的策略。

item.title: 评估大语言模型在非对话规划中的心智理论能力
item.content:encoded:
研究大语言模型在非对话场景中理解他人信念的能力。



摘要： 本文提出一种新的评估方法NCP-ToM，用于测试大语言模型在非对话情境下通过行动影响他人信念的能力。研究采用新框架NCP-ExploreToM，让模型通过移动物体或引导角色完成任务目标。评估结果显示，GPT-5在80%的任务中表现优异，且优于人类参与者，但在不同情境下的鲁棒性仍不及人类。所有模型在处理真实信念任务时表现优于虚假信念任务，这为模型对齐提供了积极信号。研究强调了对自主社会代理进行代理评估的重要性。

item.title: 不确定性感知深度学习在汽车雷达到达方向估计中的应用研究
item.content:encoded:
比较两种不确定性建模方法在雷达DOA估计中的性能与适用性。



摘要： 本文研究了不确定性感知的深度学习方法在汽车雷达到达方向（DOA）估计中的应用，重点分析了基于圆统计学的冯·米塞斯（VM）集成模型与基于正态逆伽马分布的证据深度学习（EDL）框架。VM集成模型通过参数（mu, kappa）生成可解释的不确定性，适用于方向几何结构；而EDL则提供更平滑的不确定性变化和稍优的排名一致性。实验表明，在正常条件下，VM集成模型具有更低的不确定性，并对严重扰动更敏感，而EDL在不确定性分布上更为稳定。此外，VM集成模型可通过闭式VM似然直接整合到关联模块中，实现统一的目标检测与跟踪流程。研究揭示了几何一致性与统计泛化之间的权衡。

item.title: 基于动态支持区间的高斯直方图损失在强化学习中的应用
item.content:encoded:
提出动态学习支持区间的高斯直方图损失方法，提升RL性能。



摘要： 本文研究了高斯直方图损失（HL-Gauss）在深度强化学习中的应用，该方法将价值估计转化为分类问题。传统方法需要预先定义固定支持区间，但在非平稳和随机的RL任务中存在困难。本文提出一种动态学习支持上下界的方法，在学习价值分布的同时优化支持区间，理论分析表明该方法能更紧地约束均方贝尔曼误差。实验显示该方法在多数连续控制任务中表现优于传统HL-Gauss方法，且无需预设支持区间。

item.title: 基于双像素与视觉Transformer的深度估计方法
item.content:encoded:
FoundDP提升双像素深度估计的结构一致性和精度。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为FoundDP的统一框架，将双像素（DP）深度估计与单目深度基础模型的全局结构先验相结合。该方法通过DP获取度量尺度，并利用视觉Transformer（ViT）特征恢复弱视差区域的结构一致性。为确保在DP成像条件下可靠的度量引导，作者识别并缓解了ViT特征因DP散焦模糊导致的退化问题，从而实现更稳定的度量引导深度估计。实验表明，FoundDP在合成和真实DP基准数据集上均表现出色，特别是在视差可观察性较低的情况下，显著提升了结构保真度和度量准确性。

item.title: 多器官病理报告生成框架PriOrGen缓解长尾偏差
item.content:encoded:
提出PriOrGen框架解决多器官病理报告生成中的长尾偏差问题。



摘要： 本文针对数字病理学中自动化病理报告生成的挑战，提出了一种名为PriOrGen的新框架，以解决现有方法在多器官场景下的偏差问题。研究指出，现有方法存在视觉表示偏差和文本解码偏差，导致对尾部器官的诊断不可靠。PriOrGen通过两个创新模块：视觉原型锚定瓶颈模块和元报告锚定库模块，分别优化视觉特征提取和文本生成过程，有效缓解了长尾分布带来的影响。实验表明，该方法在多器官数据集上表现出色，优于现有先进方法。

item.title: 基于互信息的多目标探索与偏好优化方法
item.content:encoded:
提出MI-EPO框架，提升语言模型与多维偏好的对齐效果。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于互信息的多目标探索与偏好优化方法（MI-EPO），通过最大化生成响应、偏好反馈和偏好向量之间的联合条件互信息，统一多目标探索与对齐过程。该方法引入概率路由机制，自然分解目标对齐与偏好感知探索，鼓励模型生成与不同偏好条件相一致且可区分的响应。实验表明，MI-EPO在安全对齐和助手任务中显著提升了响应与偏好向量的对齐效果，增强了输出的可控性，并实现了多个目标间的稳定权衡。

item.title: WorldOdysseyBench：多维度评估交互式世界模型的基准测试
item.content:encoded:
提出WorldOdysseyBench，全面评估交互式世界模型的稳定性与真实性。



摘要： 本文提出WorldOdysseyBench，一个用于评估交互式世界模型（IWMs）长期稳定性的开放世界基准测试。该基准涵盖四个维度：动作、视觉、物理和记忆，并引入了针对每个维度的创新评估方法。例如，动作维度通过逐帧评估揭示轨迹级评估无法发现的失败；视觉维度通过分段漂移度量捕捉序列中非单调的崩溃现象；物理维度通过可控性门控评估机械、光学和3D一致性；记忆维度则通过解耦动作协议评估场景和主体记忆。测试案例覆盖自然、城市和室内场景，包含第一人称和第三人称视角，持续交互时间从10秒到60秒不等。评估显示，现有模型在所有维度上均表现不佳，表明IWMs仍需进一步发展以实现真实部署。

item.title: 评估前沿AI系统在儿童安全风险中的识别能力
item.content:encoded:
本文提出CAREBench基准，用于评估AI系统在儿童安全风险发生前的识别能力。



摘要： 文章探讨了如何评估前沿AI系统在儿童安全风险发生前的识别能力。现有的儿童安全评估主要针对儿童性虐待材料，但许多安全问题在AI帮助成年人操纵、冒充、追踪或孤立未成年人时就已经开始。为此，作者提出了CAREBench（Child AI Risk Evaluation）基准，涵盖12类风险，如诱骗、身份冒充、隐私侵犯等。该基准通过家长和临床专家的标注，评估AI是否能在风险行为显性化之前进行识别、拒绝、缓和或引导。对7个前沿模型的测试显示失败率从2%到58%不等，表明AI在儿童安全保护方面仍存在显著漏洞。

item.title: PedNStream：基于Link Transmission Model的大规模行人网络仿真框架
item.content:encoded:
PedNStream是一个高效且适用于控制的行人网络仿真工具。



摘要： 本文介绍了PedNStream，一个基于Link Transmission Model（LTM）的宏观行人网络仿真框架。该工具通过引入随机链接动态来捕捉扩散和活动引起的变异，并采用基于效用的路由选择方法以适应不确定和干预驱动的环境。PedNStream具备模块化结构和内置控制器接口，支持如闸门控制、流量分离和路线引导等干预措施。文章通过合成场景和真实网络实验验证了其性能，包括排队形成、溢流、拥堵消散和自适应重路由等功能，并展示了其在闭环控制中的应用和可扩展性。

item.title: 多秘书问题中加性后悔的下界分析
item.content:encoded:
研究多秘书问题中的加性后悔下界，证明对某些分布需至少 (log T)^2 的后悔。



摘要： 本文研究多秘书问题中加性后悔的下界，即在线策略收益与离线最优收益之间的差距。已有研究表明，在有界密度分布且支持集连续的情况下，后悔上界为 O(log T)，而在支持集存在间隙的情况下，上界为 O((log T)^2)。然而，是否需要额外的对数因子仍不清楚。本文证明在临界容量下混合两个分离均匀分布时，最优后悔至少为 (log T)^2，因此现有上界在最简情形下是紧致的。该框架还给出了支持集边缘密度趋于零的分布的匹配下界，证明过程使用了 Bellman 证书，将下界转化为显式证书构造，揭示了支持集间隙为何导致更大后悔。

item.title: CooperScene：面向真实世界部署的协同自动驾驶数据集
item.content:encoded:
CooperScene提供高保真协同自动驾驶数据，支持多智能体实际性能评估。



摘要： 本文介绍了CooperScene，一个高保真的协同自动驾驶数据集，旨在反映真实世界的C-V2X通信特性。该数据集涵盖多种场景，包括交叉口、高速公路匝道和停车场，包含三辆联网自动驾驶汽车和一个路侧单元，所有设备均配备多模传感器和商用C-V2X通信设备。数据集提供全局一致的3D标注，覆盖59,000帧，总计344,000个物体，并具备厘米级定位、精确标定和符合3GPP标准的通信协议。CooperScene为多智能体协同系统的实际性能评估提供了严格的基准。

item.title: Dynamo：无需训练的视觉语言模型适应框架
item.content:encoded:
Dynamo提升视觉推理性能，无需重新训练模型。



摘要： Dynamo是一个无需训练的框架，通过在少量标注数据上运行，使冻结的视觉语言模型（VLM）适应视觉推理任务。该框架通过分析自身正确与错误尝试，逐步发展出可复用的推理技能和可执行的视觉工具，并将这些能力存入持久化库中。实验表明，Dynamo在四个视觉推理基准测试中提升了所有20个模型-基准组合的直接推理准确率，且在已知工具集的情况下，能有效选择工具以进一步提升性能。相比任务特定的强化学习方法，Dynamo在计算成本更低的情况下显著缩小了性能差距，并可与强化学习方法结合使用。

item.title: 基于利润的反事实解释框架PBCE
item.content:encoded:
提出PBCE框架，以利润最大化替代传统反事实解释方法。



摘要： 本文针对现有反事实解释方法在目标设定和距离度量上的不足，提出基于利润最大化（PBCE）的反事实解释框架。该框架无需外生指定目标值，而是直接以利润作为优化目标，并将距离项重新解释为修改产品属性的成本，从而提供更具经济意义的解释。此方法适用于管理与营销场景，有助于提升模型可解释性并支持数据驱动决策。

item.title: 静脉识别数据增强评估与AGVBench基准研究
item.content:encoded:
静脉识别数据增强效果评估及安全性能分析。



摘要： 本文介绍了AGVBench，一个用于评估30种数据增强策略在五组公开手掌和手指静脉数据集上的表现的基准平台。研究涵盖了传统CNN、视觉Transformer和专用静脉识别模型。结果显示，多图像混合方法（如MixUp、PuzzleMix、StarMixup）在识别性能上表现最佳，但存在对抗攻击脆弱性问题。研究还发现几何变换常导致识别性能下降，且不同数据集间增强效果差异显著。这表明仅关注准确率不足以评估生物特征增强的有效性。AGVBench提供了标准化协议，支持可复现的研究和可靠、安全、鲁棒的静脉识别系统设计。

item.title: 自主网络中AI决策的运行时验证框架研究
item.content:encoded:
提出Guard Rail Validation框架以确保AI在自主网络中的安全决策。



摘要： 本文针对完全自主电信网络中AI/ML代理在无人工干预下做出实时决策的风险，提出了一种名为Guard Rail Validation (GRV) 的标准化运行时验证框架。该框架通过多维评估（如操作范围、服务关键性、可逆性等）确定决策的关键性等级，并根据等级应用不同级别的验证机制，包括执行日志记录、边界检查、独立代理验证或多代理共识。此外，该框架还支持跨代理冲突检测和关键性加权优先级解决，以及符合监管要求的运行时合规日志记录。文章展示了该架构、算法流程、O-RAN部署模型，并评估了其对已知AI/ML攻击的威胁覆盖能力。

item.title: LLM数学推理中的策略多样性研究
item.content:encoded:
研究提出策略级多样性，揭示传统度量的不足。



摘要： 本文探讨了大语言模型在数学推理中的多样性问题，指出现有度量主要关注表层变化，而非解题策略的差异。作者引入了策略级多样性概念，通过人工校准的LLM评判框架发现，传统度量无法准确反映策略多样性，且在多样性感知的强化学习中导致策略多样性下降。研究还发现，策略多样化的候选集有助于测试时的性能提升，但训练过程中优化评判器多样性奖励会导致模型利用特定偏好，而非真正拓展策略。该工作为实现更接近人类的多样化推理提供了新方向。

item.title: ReMAP-PET: Beyond Visual Understanding -- Learning Region-Guided Metabolic Alignment Semantics from Brain PET
item.content:encoded: Positron Emission Tomography (PET) reveals brain metabolism and is clinically central to neurodegenerative disease assessment, yet existing 3D brain foundation models treat PET as generic volumetric data, missing the structured regional metabolic information that distinguishes it from structural neuroimaging. To address these limitations, we propose ReMAP-PET, a framework that moves beyond visual encoding by supervising a partially-tuned MedicalNet 3D ResNet-50 with brain regional standardized uptake value ratio (SUVR) profiles through joint regression and contrastive objectives, enabling the encoder to learn the metabolic semantics underlying PET modality. On 1015 paired PET--SUVR samples, ReMAP-PET achieves 0.070 SUVR MAE and 77.8% PET SUVR Recall@1, substantially outperforming five frozen pretrained baselines. We further connect the metabolic embedding to clinical language via contrastive alignment with frozen BioClinicalBERT and demonstrate end-to-end PET-to-report generation through SUVR-constrained verbalization. Linear probing on diagnostic classification and cognitive regression tasks confirms that the embeddings retain clinically relevant information without task-specific fine-tuning. Our results show that grounding PET encoders in regional metabolic semantics -- rather than treating PET as generic volumetric data -- yields representations that are structured, interpretable, and language-compatible, pointing to a new direction for metabolic-aware PET understanding.

item.title: Embracing Intra-Class Heterogeneity for Semi-Supervised Medical Image Segmentation: From Diversity to Precision
item.content:encoded: Due to the scarcity of expert-annotated data, Semi-Supervised Medical Image Segmentation (SSMIS) has emerged as a promising approach. Many anatomical structures in medical images exhibit significant intra-class heterogeneity, with different regions showing heterogeneous intensity patterns within the same structure. However, existing methods inadequately exploit this intensity-manifested intra-class heterogeneity, resulting in uniform structural representations and imprecise segmentation. Furthermore, the scarcity of labeled data makes it more difficult to effectively capture such complex heterogeneity. To address this, we propose Multiple Prototype Contrastive Learning (MPCL), an SSMIS framework that possesses better diversity and better precision. It consists of three novel designs: First, we provide structural representations with better diversity and propose Intensity-aligned Heterogeneous Prototype Generation (IHPG) that effectively models intra-class heterogeneity by generating multiple prototypes aligned with intensity characteristics. Second, we further enhance more diverse structural representations and build a solid foundation for more precise segmentation through Prototypical Space Optimization (PSO) that systematically optimizes a more discriminative and generalizable prototypical space. Finally, we achieve segmentation results with better precision through Dual-branch Knowledge Alignment (DKA) that efficiently promotes intra-class heterogeneity knowledge transfer from prototypical space to the segmentation network. Extensive experiments on three medical image datasets with significant intra-class heterogeneity demonstrate that MPCL significantly outperforms existing methods, especially under extremely limited labeled data.

item.title: FacePlex: Full-Duplex Joint Speech-Facial Motion Generation for Conversational Avatars
item.content:encoded: Natural face-to-face conversation requires real-time speech generation together with synchronized facial motion. Existing systems only partially address this problem: speech-only full-duplex models can generate speech in real time but do not produce facial motion, while audio-driven facial motion models animate a face from already available audio rather than jointly generating speech and motion online. To bridge this gap, we first formalize full-duplex joint speech-facial motion generation, where speech tokens and facial motion tokens are produced together every step. Building on this formulation, we propose FacePlex, a unified streaming framework with two key components. First, Rolling Flow Matching adapts flow matching to online motion generation by committing new motion frames at each streaming step. Second, Rolling Cross-Attention couples the streaming audio queue with the motion queue, allowing speech and facial motion to condition each other as generation progresses. Through extensive experiments, ablation studies, and a user study, we show that FacePlex enables full-duplex joint speech-facial motion generation under online streaming constraints, while achieving stronger lip-sync quality and motion fidelity than audio-driven facial motion baselines.

item.title: HealthAgentBench: A Unified Benchmark Suite of Realistic Agentic Healthcare Environments for Challenging Frontier AI Agents
item.content:encoded: As AI agents become increasingly capable of complex, long-horizon reasoning, rigorous and holistic evaluation is essential for measuring progress toward real-world healthcare applications. We introduce HealthAgentBench, a suite of 54 agentic healthcare tasks across 7 categories each with its unique environment. The benchmark suite spans diverse workflows throughout the patient journey and a broad range of modalities. Each task is designed to replicate an end-to-end clinical workflow: given minimal instructions, an agent must explore raw healthcare data, operate within a complex environment, and execute multi-step solutions that go beyond naive prompting. A final task success rate is reported to provide a single, interpretable metric for HealthAgentBench overall performance for each agent. Evaluating frontier agents on HealthAgentBench, we find that overall task success rate remains low, underscoring the difficulty of the suite. The strongest and the most cost effective agent, Codex GPT-5.5, achieves only approximately 42% success rate. Beyond aggregate performance, HealthAgentBench reveals nuanced strengths and weaknesses across task categories. Frontier agents show promise in automatically developing research modeling pipelines over EHR data, but medical imaging remains especially challenging, particularly for Claude Code models, while Codex GPT-5.5 shows emerging capability. Tasks that combine large search spaces with compositional reasoning requirements remain difficult for all current agents. Together, these results suggest that HealthAgentBench provides a challenging and realistic benchmark with substantial room for future progress. We release our benchmark at https://github.com/microsoft/HealthAgentBench.

item.title: DL-VINS-Factory: A Modular Framework for Learned Visual Front-Ends in Visual-Inertial SLAM
item.content:encoded: Deep-learning features excel in visual matching, yet their practical value in tightly coupled visual-inertial SLAM (VI-SLAM) remains insufficiently characterized. We present DL-VINS-Factory, a unified framework that integrates learned feature extractors (ALIKED, RaCo, SuperPoint, XFeat) with either Lucas--Kanade (LK) optical-flow tracking or LightGlue (LG) descriptor matching. All front-ends share a sliding-window Ceres back-end, with optional AnyLoc DINOv2-VLAD loop closure, and 4-DoF pose-graph optimization. We benchmark the system across the four datasets covering indoor, unstructured outdoor, aggressive-motion, and visually degraded conditions. Results show that learned front-ends are viable for real-time embedded VI-SLAM, but are not universally superior to classical tracking. Relative to the corresponding GFTT+LK baseline, ALIKED+LG reduces EuRoC ATE by $5\%$ in monocular odometry and by $7\%$ in stereo with loop-closure. On NTU-VIRAL, where aggressive aerial motion increases inter-frame viewpoint change, ALIKED+LG stereo reduces loop-closed ATE by $12\%$. In Botanic Garden dataset, optical-flow tracking remains preferable, but learned keypoints still improve over the baseline GFTT, in which SuperPoint+LK reduces grayscale camera ATE by $29\%$, while RaCo+LK reduces RGB camera ATE by $38\%$. On SubT-MRS, learned front-ends display varying degree of improvement based on individual cases. With TensorRT acceleration on a Jetson AGX Orin, all valid configurations run in real time between $29$--$47$ FPS in monocular mode and $18$--$33$ FPS in stereo mode for the EuRoC and NTU-VIRAL datasets. AnyLoc further confirms roughly $2$--$7\times$ more valid loops than BRIEF+DBoW2. The implementation is open-sourced at https://github.com/limshoonkit/DL-VINS-Factory-ROS2/.

item.title: SHOVIR: A Benchmark for Evaluating Vision Shortcut Learning in Radiology Report Generation
item.content:encoded: Current evaluation protocols for Vision-Language Models (VLMs) in Radiology Report Generation (RRG) rely on report-level metrics that measure lexical overlap or aggregate clinical correctness. However, such metrics do not test whether individual diagnostic statements stem from the actual pathological evidence visible in the image. This allows models to achieve competitive scores by exploiting learned priors or spurious correlations, a failure mode we refer to as vision shortcut. We introduce SHOVIR, a benchmark for evaluating vision shortcut behavior in RRG. SHOVIR extends two spatially annotated chest X-ray datasets, MIMIC-CXR and PadChest-GR, with per-box CheXpert labels, and defines image-level and disease-level occlusion experiments that contrast baseline performance on clean images against localized, region-specific perturbations. Comparing predictions across these conditions isolates two failure modes at the disease-class level: direct shortcuts, where a finding persists after its visual evidence is removed, and contextual shortcuts, where detection degrades once co-occurring pathologies are occluded despite the target region remaining intact. Benchmarking eight state-of-the-art VLMs, we find that shortcut behavior varies substantially across architectures and datasets. Models achieving the highest baseline report quality do not necessarily rank highest in spatial grounding, revealing that clinically fluent generation can coexist with shallow reliance on visual evidence. These findings expose a blind spot in current RRG evaluation and motivate region-aware assessment protocols.

item.title: From SRA to Self-Flow: Data Augmentation or Self-Supervision?
item.content:encoded: Representation alignment has become an effective way to accelerate diffusion transformer training and improve generation quality. Recent self-alignment methods, such as SRA and Self-Flow, further remove the dependency on external pretrained encoders by constructing alignment within the diffusion model itself. However, the mechanism behind the improvement from SRA to Self-Flow, dual-time scheduling, remains under-examined: Self-Flow attributes its gain to interactions between tokens at different noise levels, where cleaner tokens help infer noisier ones. In this work, we revisit this explanation and ask whether the gain instead comes from data augmentation along the noise dimension. To disentangle these factors, we introduce Attention Separation, which preserves the same dual-timestep input as Self-Flow while blocking attention between tokens assigned to different noise levels. Surprisingly, removing such interaction does not degrade performance and can even improve it, suggesting that the improvement from SRA to Self-Flow mainly comes from data augmentation. Furthermore,We show that Attention Separation itself provides an augmentation effect by splitting a single image into multiple effective training parts to expand the training data. Based on these observations, we combine self-representation alignment with dual-timestep and attention-separation augmentation, and demonstrate the effectiveness of this design on ImageNet.

item.title: Generic Expert Coverage for Pruning SparseMixture-of-Experts Language Models
item.content:encoded: Sparsely activated Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) language models contain substantial structured redundancy among routed experts, but pruning them without downstream calibration data remains challenging. Existing expert-pruning methods typically rely on a single aggregated importance score, which can bias the retained set toward experts favored by dominant calibration patterns. We propose \textbf{Generic TB-Coverage}, a coverage-aware expert pruning method that uses only generic text corpora (WikiText2 and C4) for calibration. Instead of collapsing expert utility into one score, our method profiles per-expert utility separately on each corpus and enforces a fixed-budget coverage rule that preserves high-utility experts from each corpus before constructing the final pruning mask. Across Qwen1.5-MoE-A2.7B and DeepSeek-MoE-16B-Base at 25\%, 50\%, and 75\% retention budgets, our method improves average accuracy on six common zero-shot benchmarks over random pruning, REAP, and ExpertSparsity, while also reducing perplexity degradation on WikiText2 and C4. The gains are largest under aggressive pruning (25\% and 50\% retain), suggesting that preserving cross-corpus expert coverage is an effective generic-data prior for MoE pruning. Our improvements hold with fixed pruning budgets and no downstream calibration data.

item.title: Bi-NAS: Towards Effective and Personalized Explanation for Recommender Systems via Bi-Level Neural Architecture Search
item.content:encoded: Recommender systems are vital in helping users navigate vast amounts of information, offering personalized suggestions and effective explanations for these recommendations. While previous efforts have attempted to provide such explanations, evaluating their effectiveness across various scenarios remains a challenge. Enhancing these explanations is essential for improving user engagement, trust, and decision-making. To facilitate effective explanations within the recommender system, we propose a Bi-level Neural Architecture Search (Bi-NAS) framework to optimize explanations. This approach simultaneously refines cross-attention mechanisms and feature interaction functions by exploring both intra-layer and inter-layer design spaces. Furthermore, we integrate Large Language Models (LLMs) to enhance explanation generation, leveraging zero-shot prompting to produce more effective and personalized justifications. By aligning user feature preferences with item quality scores, our approach ensures that explanations reflect both user intent and item attributes, improving transparency and reasoning depth. Extensive evaluations on four real-world datasets demonstrate that Bi-NAS not only boosts recommendation accuracy but also significantly improves the effectiveness of explanations for recommender systems, providing users with clear and reliable insights into the suggestions they receive.

item.title: STEMGym: Benchmarking Sequential Decision-Making under Dose Budgets in Autonomous Electron Microscopy
item.content:encoded: A central premise of autonomous scientific imaging is that smarter navigation, whether Bayesian, RL-based, or otherwise adaptive, is the principal lever for sample-efficient acquisition. We present evidence to the contrary in scanning transmission electron microscopy (STEM), an atomic-resolution imaging modality whose every measurement deposits damaging electron dose. We introduce STEMGym, an open-source Gymnasium benchmark of 15 physics-simulated STEM worlds spanning five materials, three difficulty levels, and four characterisation tasks, scored by the Dose-Efficiency Curve area (DEC-AUC), a single scalar capturing the information-vs-dose Pareto frontier. Across 33 agent configurations under realistic dose budgets, the dominant determinant of dose efficiency is the analyst (perception) pipeline, not the navigator: pairing a trained CNN analyst with naïve raster scanning raises DEC-AUC by 5.5x over a CNN-free raster baseline (0.287 vs.\ 0.052), while substituting Bayesian or adaptive finite-state-machine navigation for raster yields no statistically significant further gain. Production-tier vision-language models further underperform task-specific CNNs by {\sim}13x on crystallographic defect analysis. By decoupling perception, navigation, and planning under a unified dose budget, STEMGym reframes where ML effort should be invested in autonomous electron microscopy and provides the measurement infrastructure to test it.

item.title: 多单位歧视性拍卖中投标者的重复竞价策略研究
item.content:encoded:
研究多单位歧视性拍卖中投标者在预算约束下的最优竞价策略。



摘要： 本文研究了在多单位歧视性拍卖中，单个投标者如何在每轮竞价中最大化累积效用，考虑了预算限制和资本成本参数的影响。通过将效用分解为各个单位的贡献，作者提出了基于最短路径的多项式时间学习算法，在全信息和仅知结果的反馈环境下均实现了次线性遗憾。在预算约束下，设计了耦合的原始-对偶算法，利用双重调整的边权重进行原始更新，并通过在线梯度下降更新对偶变量，实现了与预算相关的次线性遗憾。最后，提出了可扩展到大规模或无限上下文空间的实现方法。

item.title: 基于视觉-语言-动作模型的双臂家具装配系统研究
item.content:encoded:
提出FurnitureVLA，提升真实尺度家具装配成功率。



摘要： 本文提出FurnitureVLA，首个针对真实尺度双臂家具装配的系统性研究，采用视觉-语言-动作模型（VLAs）。研究构建了可扩展的仿真管道用于专家数据生成与评估，并开发了VR远程操作系统以获取高质量现实世界演示。为解决长达7个子任务、1550步控制的长时序装配问题，提出增强进度的VLA模型，通过语义化子任务微调，联合预测动作与连续进度信号，实现自动子任务切换并减少推理中的误差累积。研究还探讨了影响真实尺度装配精度的感知与控制设计因素，实验显示FurnitureVLA在三种家具类型中平均仿真成功率从48%提升至80%，设计因素研究进一步提升21%，在实际Kinova Gen3平台上仅下降16%。

item.title: 基于部分可见骨架的鲁棒动作识别方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出PartialVisGraph框架提升部分可见骨架动作识别性能。



摘要： 本文针对真实场景中由于视野限制导致的骨架关键点缺失问题，提出了一种名为PartialVisGraph的新框架。该框架通过引入可学习的虚拟超边构建高表达力的超图结构，捕捉超越传统成对关系的高阶依赖关系。同时设计了单头自适应Transformer模块，能够根据可见性先验信息动态聚合关节特征，有效防止遮挡或离视野关节干扰特征传播。在NTU RGB+D数据集上的实验表明，该方法在部分可见条件下表现优异，相比现有方法提升高达68.8%，同时在全可见情况下也保持优势，为实际部署提供可行方案。

item.title: EchoRisk数据集与心脏毒性预测挑战
item.content:encoded:
EchoRisk数据集用于乳腺癌治疗相关心脏毒性的早期预测研究。



摘要： 本文介绍了EchoRisk数据集，这是首个经过整理的多中心纵向超声心动图数据集，专门用于乳腺癌治疗引起的心脏毒性风险评估。该数据集包含422名患者，涵盖1,123次临床检查和2,159段超声视频，支持三个任务：左心室射血分数估计、左心室功能障碍分类以及治疗前基线超声的早期心脏毒性预测。研究团队使用R(2+1)D视频模型进行基准测试，结果显示在心脏功能评估和左心室功能障碍分类方面表现良好，但基于单次治疗前超声的早期预测仍具挑战性。该数据集和代码已公开，以促进心血管肿瘤学领域的进一步研究。

item.title: 多语言预训练语料库MultiSynt/MT的构建与应用
item.content:encoded:
多语言预训练语料库提升LLM性能，支持跨语言研究。



摘要： 文章介绍了MultiSynt/MT，一个包含约4.8万亿目标语言词元的开放合成平行语料库，涵盖36种欧洲语言。该语料库通过翻译1000亿高质量Nemotron-CC词元生成，使用Tower+和OPUS-MT/HPLT-MT系统。对于许多中等和低资源欧洲语言，这是目前最大的公开预训练资源。实验表明，基于MultiSynt/MT训练的参考LLM在多语言基准测试中达到HPLT 2.0的水平，且用更少的预训练词元实现更高性能。分析还指出评估盲点，如标准多项选择测试未能捕捉翻译质量差异，而基于流畅度的LLM评估能有效识别这些差异。文章还提到挪威语的特定任务仍需依赖本地数据。语料库已发布，支持多语言预训练数据和评估的可控研究。

item.title: 基于上下文嵌入预测普通话单字发音时长的研究
item.content:encoded:
上下文嵌入可有效预测普通话单字发音时长。



摘要： 本研究探讨了普通话口语中单音节CV词的发音时长是否能通过其上下文嵌入（CEs）进行预测。通过对7470个普通话单字词的分析，研究发现CEs不仅能以高于随机水平的方式预测发音时长，而且在词类层面和个体词层面均表现出显著的预测能力。此外，预测的时长足够精确，可以将归一化的频率轮廓转换为毫秒时间尺度上的轮廓，且预测结果优于置换基线。

item.title: 基于时变参数的动态模型预测框架研究
item.content:encoded:
时变参数模型结合深度学习实现复杂动态预测。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于离散时间动态模型的预测框架，其中模型系数随时间变化。通过深度学习框架恢复模型参数，在保持透明参数结构的同时，能够捕捉复杂的非平稳模式。分析涵盖了两种噪声过程：标准高斯噪声和拉普拉斯噪声，后者在重尾和尖峰波动情况下更具描述优势。针对两种情况，文章构建了预测方案并分析了不确定性量化，包括预测区间构造。结果表明，简单模型结合时变参数估计可在不同噪声假设下有效预测复杂动态，模型适用于TVAR(p)，但预测区间公式与实验集中在TVAR(1)情形。

item.title: 基于神经网络架构搜索的AI计算资源回收框架
item.content:encoded:
通过NAS技术将通用任务转为NN模型，提升边缘AI芯片利用率。



摘要： 随着AI在物联网场景中的广泛应用，AI芯片已成为边缘计算的重要组成部分。然而，这些芯片通常仅针对结构化神经网络设计，导致在处理非峰值负载时存在大量计算浪费。与此同时，通用处理器在面对高计算需求任务时又面临资源限制。为此，本文提出一种利用通用近似技术回收闲置AI计算资源的框架，通过神经网络架构搜索（NAS）将传统任务转化为神经网络模型，并在AI芯片空闲时段部署，从而减轻通用处理器负担。实验表明，该策略显著提升了边缘处理任务的性能。

item.title: 分析工作流生成中的语义缺失问题研究
item.content:encoded:
研究发现代理生成的分析工作流存在语义缺失问题。



摘要： 本文通过跨领域的实证研究，分析了由智能代理生成的分析工作流中出现的153次操作失败。尽管工作流生成和执行成功，但语义信息不足导致了五类常见错误：比较基础、流程推理、量化推理、角色混淆和政策基础。这表明用户分析概念与系统可用信息之间存在语义差距，未来数据系统需更丰富的语义表示以弥合这一差距。

item.title: 解耦神经近似下的FBSDE后验误差分析框架
item.content:encoded:
提出一种FBSDE解耦神经近似的后验误差分析方法。



摘要： 本文提出了一种针对完全耦合前向-后向随机微分方程（FBSDE）的解耦神经近似后的验误差分析框架。该方法引入辅助控制过程以实现前向系数与后向分量的解耦，便于深度学习实现，但需在误差分析中考虑控制不匹配问题。研究建立了连续时间下的稳定性估计，并将其推广到离散时间，推导出仅依赖终端缺陷、路径残差和控制不匹配的可计算后验误差界。数值实验验证了所提指标的诊断作用及不匹配惩罚对数值近似一致性和可重复性的贡献。

item.title: 基于重要性感知的大型多模态模型二值化方法
item.content:encoded:
提出SAB-LVLM提升多模态模型二值化性能



摘要： 本文针对大型视觉语言模型（LVLM）在资源受限设备上部署时的高内存和计算开销问题，提出一种新的重要性感知二值化方法SAB-LVLM。该方法通过构建文本和视觉输入的Hessian矩阵，生成空间重要性图以区分单模态和跨模态激活的全精度权重，并设计模态引导集成策略获得重要性感知二值化图。该图作为误差重加权项融入二值化目标，通过交替重要性加权更新方案进行二值化拟合。实验表明，在约1比特压缩约束下，SAB-LVLM优于现有二值化PTQ方法。

item.title: TF-MoE：一种高效语音分离模型框架
item.content:encoded:
TF-MoE提升语音分离性能且计算成本低。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为TF-MoE的稀疏专家混合框架，用于提升语音分离模型在边缘设备上的部署能力。该方法通过时间与频率维度的动态专家专业化，实现模型容量的增强而几乎不增加推理成本。基于梅尔频带分割的Conformer骨干网络，TF-MoE在低计算条件下表现出色。实验结果显示，在Libri2Mix数据集上，其语音分离性能优于BSRNN，SDR提升3.8 dB，同时保持4.1 GMACs/s的推理成本。这表明TF-MoE是边缘设备部署的有力候选方案。

item.title: 基于面部动作单元与文本融合的异步视频面试人格识别方法
item.content:encoded:
融合面部动作单元与文本提升AVI中人格识别准确度。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于大语言模型的框架，通过语义融合异步视频面试中的面部动作单元（AUs）与文本回答，以提高人格识别的准确性。该方法将AUs序列转化为可解释的文本描述，并与受访者的文本回复进行融合，再通过轻量级回归头生成连续人格评分。实验表明，该方法在AVI-6基准数据集上优于多数基线模型，具有更低的预测误差和更强的人类评分相关性。进一步分析显示，AUs提供的非语言线索能有效补充文本信息，同时在LLM中解耦语义理解与回归预测，提升了训练稳定性和可解释性。

item.title: AI预测器的安全性分析与设计
item.content:encoded:
AI预测器通过贝叶斯后验训练，避免隐含代理行为。



摘要： 本文提出了一种针对科学家AI预测器（SAI Predictor）的安全性论证。该预测器通过近似基于“知识情境化”自然语言语句的数据集的贝叶斯后验进行训练，旨在避免引入未指定的目标导向行为。文章强调，通过区分事实陈述和沟通行为，预测器不会将目标表达视为自身驱动因素，而是作为需要解释的证据。训练过程采用后验搜索目标，使预测器趋向于谨慎、校准的预测。部署后的预测结果不作为奖励信号，系统所需的任何代理行为均由外部约束提供。在特定假设下，模型产生有害预测的概率极低，因为这种协调性错误在初始化分布中罕见且无直接训练信号。安全性与准确性在此框架中相互支持。

item.title: DART-VLN：一种无需训练的离散视觉语言导航控制框架
item.content:encoded:
DART-VLN提升离散视觉语言导航的可靠性和效率。



摘要： 本文提出DART-VLN，一种无需训练的测试时控制框架，用于改善基于记忆的离散视觉语言导航（VLN）系统。该框架结合了测试时记忆衰减和反循环正则化，通过重加权记忆内容和惩罚立即回退动作，有效减少过时信息和局部回溯，从而提升导航性能和路径效率。实验表明，该方法在R2R和REVERIE数据集上均取得显著效果，且不改变原有模型结构。

item.title: FARS：全自动化AI研究系统在大规模部署中的表现与评估
item.content:encoded:
FARS系统生成大量AI/ML论文，展示其研究能力与潜在问题。



摘要： FARS是一个完全自动化的AI研究系统，能够在多个AI/ML主题上自主进行研究项目，包括提出想法、规划、实验和撰写论文。在首次公开部署中，FARS生成了166篇完整论文，覆盖67个细分领域，并保留了中间成果作为可审计的文献库。通过282份志愿者评审，评估了其中140篇论文的质量，结果显示FARS能够产出具有研究价值的成果，但也暴露出实验范围狭窄、方法局限和完整性问题等缺陷。

item.title: 基于位平面的图像模糊方案Bit-ViP提升视觉隐私保护
item.content:encoded:
提出Bit-ViP图像模糊方案，增强视觉隐私与可用性。



摘要： 随着计算机视觉应用的快速发展，如监控系统和社交媒体，数据存储在云端引发了安全和视觉隐私问题。图像模糊技术能够在保持可用性的前提下保护隐私，但现有方案存在易受攻击或无法训练的问题。本文提出一种基于位平面的新型图像模糊方案Bit-ViP，通过引入端到端模糊函数生成安全且可使用的图像。该方案利用洛伦兹混沌系统和差分隐私生成不可逆噪声，使攻击者难以还原原始图像。实验在UCF101和HMDB51数据集上验证了Bit-ViP的有效性，并通过重建攻击、像素频率、信息熵和像素相关性分析证明其安全性优于现有方法。

item.title: 大语言模型推理安全方法评估与新型防御机制研究
item.content:encoded:
评估多种防御方法对大模型的攻击效果，提出响应时间探测新策略。



摘要： 本文系统评估了五种大语言模型推理阶段的安全防御方法，包括无防御、静态引导、CAST、AlphaSteer 和探测门控，在七种指令调优模型和五种攻击类型上的表现。研究发现，基于提示时间激活的防御对预填充攻击无效。AlphaSteer 在 GCG、AutoDAN 和意图欺骗攻击中表现优异，但在预填充攻击中成功率高达 50%。文章提出响应时间探测机制，通过在生成首字时进行线性探测，AUROC 达到 0.97-1.00。结合停止机制后，有效将预填充攻击成功率降至 0/40，优于 Llama Guard 3。此外，研究指出 MMLU 无法准确反映防御成本，且多样化的负样本训练可显著降低探测误报率。相关代码和数据已公开。

item.title: 基于3D场景图的多场景高斯点云融合方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种基于3D场景图的高斯点云融合方法，提升大规模3D映射精度。



摘要： 本文研究了多场景3D Gaussian Splatting（3DGS）融合问题，提出一种基于3D场景图的新方法，将3DGS注册问题转化为图注册问题。该方法通过构建更高层次的3D场景图，实现语义信息和结构上下文的全局一致性理解，从而提高注册精度。为确保融合后的场景无缝一致，引入自监督测试时优化策略，并通过优化合并后的高斯点以减少遮挡伪影。实验表明，该方法在真实和合成基准上均表现出色。

item.title: 基于RGB的单目6D位姿感知方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出GAP-GDRNet框架提升单目6D位姿估计精度。



摘要： 本文针对非合作对接和在轨服务中的单目相对位姿感知问题，提出GAP-GDRNet框架。该方法在GDR-Net基础上进行改进，引入注意力特征增强模块（AFR）和基于补丁的几何自注意力模块（PGSA），以提升弱纹理、光照变化等复杂条件下的位姿估计性能。通过Blender生成的标注数据进行监督训练，包括目标掩码、可见区域掩码、密集模型坐标图等，为最终的位姿回归提供支持。

item.title: 基于模块化社区结构的脑网络自监督学习框架BrainPICM
item.content:encoded:
BrainPICM通过个性化社区感知掩码提升脑网络分析效果。



摘要： 本文提出BrainPICM，一种基于模块化社区结构的自监督学习框架，用于更准确地分析脑网络。该方法通过渐进式非平衡最优传输过程生成ROI到社区的软分配和置信度评分，并采用课程式掩码策略逐步引入低置信度区域，以捕捉个体差异和病理变化。同时，偏差感知聚合模块通过测量功能重组来增强模型可解释性。实验表明，BrainPICM在多个fMRI数据集上均优于现有方法，显示出更强的功能一致性与泛化能力。

item.title: 基于对称性的深度网络优化方法DDC
item.content:encoded:
DDC优化器通过保持对称性提升模型性能。



摘要： 本文提出DDC（Dead-Direction Conditioner）优化器，利用网络参数的连续对称性（如logit偏移、ReLU缩放、LayerNorm尺度和注意力旋转），使优化轨迹保持在对称商空间中。DDC在Adam和Muon基础上构建，能够提升语言模型和视觉Transformer的训练稳定性与性能，减少过拟合并提高可测量性。实验表明，DDC在多个任务中优于传统优化器，显示出更强的泛化能力和更优的损失表现。

item.title: 基于上下文感知门控解码器的医学图像分割方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新型解码器提升医学图像分割精度。



摘要： 本文从解码器角度出发，提出一种上下文感知门控解码器，通过多尺度通道校准、空间竞争的门控跳跃融合以及全局上下文聚合机制，提升医学图像分割的准确性。该方法在11个基准数据集上进行了广泛实验，验证了其有效性，并优于现有强基线方法，同时保持计算效率。

item.title: 基于OR的多仓库库存分配模型ORLA研究
item.content:encoded:
ORLA模型提升电商多仓库库存分配精度。



摘要： 本文研究了大规模电商供应链中的多仓库库存分配问题，提出一种结合运筹学（OR）和大语言模型（LLM）的ORLA框架。该模型通过生成、验证和选择OR公式来优化库存分配，采用三种混合整数规划公式家族，并引入惩罚机制处理不可行情况。实验结果显示，ORLA在29个实际评估批次中提升了4.5个百分点的分配精度，优于现有方法。

item.title: 基于传感器失效与噪声下的可靠事件检测研究
item.content:encoded:
研究在传感器失效和噪声环境下事件检测的可靠性。



摘要： 本文研究了在传感器失效和信号被噪声掩盖的情况下，如何实现可靠的事件检测。通过构建一个统一的基准数据集，评估了一种容错检测器（CEPHALON）与其他标准检测器的性能。实验表明，在干净数据下所有模型表现接近完美，但在传感器逐步失效时，简单模型已具备一定鲁棒性，而CEPHALON在噪声环境下表现出更优的稳定性。进一步分析显示，训练方法是性能差异的主要原因，而非架构冗余。研究还提供了可复现的完整流程。

item.title: 基于检索增强的小型语言模型在本体构建中的应用
item.content:encoded:
本文提出一种检索增强的SLM框架用于本体构建。



摘要： 本文介绍了一种基于检索增强的小型语言模型（SLM）框架，用于支持本体构建中的知识扩展。该框架利用形式概念分析（FCA）作为符号验证循环，从种子属性出发，生成蕴含关系并进行验证。实验表明，在罕见共济失调数据集上，该方法能够有效提升关系和蕴含关系的识别效果。研究还发现，增加种子集数量有助于提高评估结果，但对正样本的识别仍具挑战性。

item.title: 基于AI的植物表型分析框架提升科研效率
item.content:encoded:
AI代理框架将植物表型分析从数据工厂转变为智能发现平台。



摘要： 文章介绍了一种基于AI的端到端代理框架，用于解决高通量植物表型分析中数据采集与分析速度不匹配的问题。该框架通过自然语言处理和视觉Transformer技术，使科学家能够与AI代理协作，实现快速、自动化的特征提取与分析。系统由对话式科学代理和计算代理组成，分别负责理解科学问题和执行大规模计算任务，确保分析过程的安全性和可追溯性。该方法将原本需要数天至数周的分析流程缩短为秒级交互，显著提升了科研效率。

item.title: MVFusion-GS：提升动态场景重建的运动感知方法
item.content:encoded:
MVFusion-GS通过运动感知机制提升动态场景重建效果。



摘要： 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) 能够实现实时新颖视角合成，但其在动态场景中的应用仍面临挑战。现有变形网络缺乏明确的运动感知，导致前景变形不准确和背景伪静态残留。为此，MVFusion-GS引入两种互补的运动感知机制：运动方差引导细化模块通过时间统计估计运动方差，并指导变形预测中的动态-静态分离；运动变换器时间注意力模块利用Transformer自注意力建模局部运动依赖，提高时间一致性。实验表明，该方法在动态场景重建和无干扰重建基准上均达到先进水平，显著提升了前景运动建模和静态背景重建效果。

item.title: VISTA：提升长周期工具代理上下文管理的模型内状态接口
item.content:encoded:
VISTA提升长周期工具代理的上下文管理能力。



摘要： 本文提出VISTA，一种无需训练、与模型无关的层，用于展示工具代理的工作内存状态。该接口通过类型化、可寻址的块形式表示工作记忆，并提供运行时仪表板显示每个块的使用情况、时间戳和访问历史，同时支持完整保真度的归档。实验表明，VISTA在多个基准测试中有效提升模型性能，尤其在高上下文压力下表现显著。其核心优势在于揭示模型内部状态，而非依赖外部策略。

item.title: Atompack：面向机器学习训练的高效分子数据存储与分发方案
item.content:encoded:
Atompack提升分子数据训练效率并减小数据体积。



摘要： 文章介绍了Atompack，一种专为机器学习训练设计的存储格式和分发层。与传统数据存储方式不同，Atompack通过追加记录、生成不可变索引以及优化内存映射读取路径，提高了训练过程中的性能。实验对比了Atompack与HDF5、LMDB和ASE等基线系统的性能，结果显示在64原子的工作负载下，Atompack在随机打乱读取方面比ASE LMDB快96倍，且生成的数据集体积减少79%。研究证明，直接提供完整分子记录比字段块或重构对象更有利于提高训练吞吐量，并保持数据集的小型化以便公开发布。
